title: 7010: The church as the people of God
content:
• 7020: church, the
  • 7030: unity

title: 7020: church, the
content: The community of faithful believers, of whom Jesus Christ is the head, called out from the world to serve God down the ages. Scripture emphasises that the church is the body of Christ whose members are intended to be filled with the Holy Spirit. Scriptural understanding of the church is corporate, rather than solitary or individual.

title: 7021: church, OT anticipations of
content:
As the people of God, the Christian church is continuous with Israel. The OT provides important anticipations of the church.

OT terms for the people of God

The descendants of Abraham

2CH 20:7; ; ISA 41:8–41:9; ; ROM 4:16; ; GAL 3:7 “children of Abraham” in the NT refers to those who, like Abraham, put their faith in God

The saints

The term for saints'' meansset apart for God'', ``made holy'', and is the most frequently used NT term for Christians: 1SA 2:9; ; 2CH 6:41 parallel PSA 132:9; ; PSA 16:3, PSA 31:23; ; DAN 7:18

The assembly

assembly'' meansa community gathered together in response to God's call to serve and worship him''. Its NT equivalents include the synagogue and the church: PSA 1:5, PSA 107:32, PSA 149:1

The scope of the OT people of God

Israel as God's people

EXO 6:7 See also EXO 19:5–19:6; LEV 26:12; JER 30:22; EZK 36:28; HOS 2:23

Not all Israelites are God's people

ROM 9:6–9:7 Even within Israel there are believers and unbelievers. See also EZR 9:8 Those Israelites who believed were sometimes called “a remnant”; ISA 1:9, ISA 11:11, ISA 11:16; JER 23:3; EZK 14:22; MIC 2:12

God's people included non-Israelites

ISA 2:2–2:3 parallel MIC 4:1–4:2 See also EXO 12:38, EXO 12:48–12:49; DEU 31:12; RUT 4:10–4:11

The formation of the OT people of God

God's people are formed through God's promises

GEN 12:2 See also GEN 15:5, GEN 17:5–17:6, GEN 22:17–22:18; EXO 1:7

God's people are chosen and called

DEU 7:6 See also 1CH 16:13 parallel PSA 105:6; PSA 33:12; EZK 20:5

God's people have been redeemed

DEU 5:6 parallel EXO 20:2 See also EXO 6:6, EXO 19:4; EZK 20:6

God formed a covenant with his people

EXO 24:8 See also EXO 19:5, EXO 34:10, EXO 34:27; DEU 4:13; JDG 2:1; JER 31:31

OT images of the people of God

As God's bride

JER 2:2 This OT image is taken up in the many references to Jesus Christ as the bridegroom and the church as his bride. See also ISA 62:5; HOS 2:16, HOS 2:19–2:20; EPH 5:25–5:27; REV 21:2

As God's vine

ISA 5:1–5:7 PSA 80:8; ; JER 2:21; ; EZK 17:5–17:6, EZK 19:10; ; HOS 10:1

As God's flock

PSA 95:7 See also PSA 74:1, PSA 77:20, PSA 100:3; ISA 40:11; ZEC 9:16; MAT 26:31; ZEC 13:7; LUK 12:32; JHN 10:16ACT 20:28–20:29; 1PE 5:2–5:3

As God's inheritance

DEU 4:20 See also PSA 28:9, PSA 33:12; ISA 19:25; MAL 3:17

As God's family

AMO 3:1–3:2 See also EXO 4:22–4:23 Israel was sometimes called God's “firstborn son” as well as his children; DEU 1:31, DEU 32:6; PSA 103:13; ISA 1:2, ISA 30:9; HOS 11:1; MAL 2:10

Requirements of the people of God

To obey God's word

DEU 5:1 See also EXO 24:3; DEU 6:1–6:3, DEU 13:4; JOS 1:7; 1SA 15:22

To remember their redemption

DEU 5:15 See also DEU 7:18, DEU 15:15, DEU 16:12; PSA 105:5

To commemorate their redemption

EXO 12:25–12:27 The focus of Israel's commemoration of their redemption was the Passover, which is one of the ceremonies lying behind the Lord's Supper. See also NUM 9:2–9:3; DEU 16:1; LUK 22:14–22:20

To love God wholeheartedly

DEU 6:5 See also DEU 10:12, DEU 11:1, DEU 19:9; JOS 23:11

The marks of the people of God

Circumcision as a mark of God's people

GEN 17:10–17:14; ; LEV 12:3; DEU 10:16, DEU 30:6; ; JER 4:4; ; ROM 2:27; ; COL 2:11

The presence of God

EXO 25:8, EXO 33:15–33:16, EXO 40:35; ; NUM 10:33–10:36; ; 2CH 5:14

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 7022: church, and Jesus Christ
content: See 2212: Jesus Christ, head of the church

title: 7023: church, and Holy Spirit
content: See 3275: Holy Spirit, in the church

title: 7024: church, nature and foundations of
content:
The church is the people called by God, who are united by their faith in Christ and by their common life in him. Various descriptions and metaphors emphasise the continuity between the people of God in the OT and NT.

NT images of the church

The body of Christ

ROM 12:4–12:5 See also 1CO 12:12, 1CO 12:27; EPH 3:6, EPH 5:23; COL 1:18, COL 1:24, COL 2:19, COL 3:15

God's building or temple

1CO 3:16–3:17 See also 1CO 3:10; 2CO 6:16; EPH 2:21–2:22; HEB 3:6, HEB 10:21; 1PE 2:5

A plant or vine

JHN 15:1–15:8 See also ROM 11:17–11:24; 1CO 3:6–3:8

Jesus Christ's flock

JHN 10:14–10:16 See also MAT 25:33; LUK 12:32; ACT 20:28–20:29; 1PE 5:2–5:4

The bride of Christ

REV 21:2 See also EPH 5:25–5:27, EPH 5:31–5:32; REV 19:7, REV 22:17

God's household or family

EPH 2:19 See also JHN 8:35–8:36; GAL 6:10; EPH 3:15; 1TI 3:15; HEB 2:11; 1PE 2:17, 1PE 4:17

NT descriptions of the church

Emphasising continuity with the OT church

Abraham's offspring: ROM 4:16; ; GAL 3:7, GAL 3:29; ROM 9:25; ; 2CO 6:16; ; HEB 13:12; ; 1PE 2:9–2:10; GAL 4:26; ; HEB 12:22; ; REV 3:12, REV 21:2, REV 21:9–21:10; GAL 6:16 the Israel of God

Emphasising God's call and authority in the church

Sons of God: MAT 5:9; ; JHN 1:12; ; ROM 8:15–8:16; ; 2CO 6:18; ; GAL 3:26, GAL 4:5–4:6; ; 1JN 3:10; MAT 24:22; ; ROM 11:7; ; 2TI 2:10; ; 1PE 1:1; ROM 8:17; ; GAL 3:29, GAL 4:7; ; TIT 3:7; ; HEB 1:14, HEB 6:17; ; 1PE 1:4; 1PE 2:5, 1PE 2:9; ; REV 1:6, REV 5:10, REV 20:6

Descriptions applied to the church by outsiders

Followers of the Way: ACT 9:2, ACT 19:9, ACT 19:23, ACT 22:4, ACT 24:14; ACT 11:26 Christians; ; ACT 24:5 “the Nazarene sect” means people belonging to Jesus the “Nazarene” (Mt 2:23)

Descriptions used by Christians

The believers. All these titles are seldom in the singular, emphasising the corporateness of Christian life: ACT 1:15, ACT 2:44, ACT 5:12; ; GAL 6:10; ; 1TI 4:12; ; 1PE 2:17; ACT 6:1–6:2, ACT 9:19, ACT 11:26, ACT 14:22, ACT 20:1; ROM 1:7, ROM 15:25; ; 1CO 6:1, 1CO 14:33; ; EPH 1:1; ; PHP 4:21; ; COL 1:12; ; JUD 1:3

Other NT descriptions of the church

1TI 3:15 the pillar and foundation of the truth; ; HEB 12:23 The church of the firstborn. “the firstborn” is plural, referring to Christians as those born again through their union with Christ, who is the firstborn.

The foundation of the church

Jesus Christ as the church's foundation-stone

1CO 3:11 See also MAT 7:24–7:25, MAT 21:42 parallels MRK 12:10, LUK 20:17; ACT 4:11; EPH 2:20; 1PE 2:6; ISA 28:16; 1PE 2:7; PSA 118:22

Apostles and prophets as founders of the church

EPH 2:19–2:20 See also MAT 16:18–16:19; REV 21:14

The church as God's people

A people chosen by God

1PE 2:9 See also JHN 15:16; ROM 8:33; EPH 1:4; COL 3:12; 2TH 2:13; JAS 2:5; 1PE 1:2

A people called by God

ROM 1:6 See also ACT 2:39; 1CO 1:2, 1CO 1:9; GAL 1:6; 2TH 2:14; 2TI 1:9; JUD 1:1

A people loved by God

1PE 2:10 See also EPH 2:1–2:5; TIT 3:4–3:7

God's covenant people

HEB 8:8–8:10 See also ROM 11:27; ISA 59:20–59:21; HEB 10:16; JER 31:31–31:33

title: 7025: church, unity and fellowship of
content:
The church is one in essence, because it is founded on one gospel, united to one Lord and indwelt by one Spirit. Its unity is under constant threat because of the tendency to division that is inherent in fallen humanity, and needs to be continually maintained and actively expressed in fellowship.

The unity of the church

The church is one

ROM 12:5 See also 1CO 12:12, 1CO 12:20; EPH 4:25

The church transcends all barriers

COL 3:11 See also JHN 10:16; ACT 10:28–10:29, ACT 10:47, ACT 15:8–15:9; GAL 3:28; EPH 2:14–2:16, EPH 3:6 The great divide threatening the first Christians was between Jew and Gentile, but the church was able to unite the two into one body in Christ.

The church's unity reflects the unity within the Trinity

EPH 4:4–4:6 The unity of the church is built around the persons of the Trinity: one Spirit, one Lord, one Father. See also JHN 17:11; ROM 3:29–3:30, ROM 10:12–10:13; GAL 3:27–3:28

The church's unity is the work of the Trinity

EPH 2:16–2:18 See also JHN 11:52; ACT 10:45–10:47; 1CO 12:13; EPH 2:22, EPH 4:3

The purpose of the church's unity

To lead others to faith

JHN 17:23 See also JHN 17:21

To lead believers to maturity

EPH 4:13

The nature of the church's unity

PHP 2:1–2:2 Emphasis is placed on an inner unity of mind and spirit rather than external uniformity. See also 2CO 13:11; PHP 1:27; COL 2:2

Appeals for unity in the church

EPH 4:3 See also ROM 12:10, ROM 15:5, ROM 15:7; 1CO 12:25; COL 3:14; 1PE 3:8

The church's unity is expressed in fellowship

Fellowship with God

1CO 1:9 See also 2CO 13:14; PHP 2:1; 2PE 1:4; 1JN 1:3, 1JN 1:6–1:7

Fellowship expressed by meeting together

ACT 2:46 See also ACT 2:1, ACT 2:42, ACT 5:12, ACT 6:2; 1CO 14:26; HEB 10:25

Fellowship expressed through sharing resources

ACT 2:44–2:45 See also ACT 4:32, ACT 4:34–4:37, ACT 11:27–11:30; ROM 15:26; 1CO 16:1–16:2; 2CO 8:2–8:5, 2CO 8:13–8:14, 2CO 9:13; PHP 4:14–4:18

Fellowship through suffering

REV 1:9 See also ROM 8:17; 2CO 1:7; PHP 3:10, PHP 4:14; HEB 10:33–10:34, HEB 13:3

Fellowship through shared spiritual blessings

1CO 9:23 See also ROM 11:17; PHP 1:7; 2TH 2:14; 1PE 5:1; JUD 1:3

Specific actions which express fellowship and unity in the church

Sharing in the Lord's Supper

1CO 10:16–10:17 See also ACT 2:46, ACT 20:7; 1CO 11:33

Baptism as an expression of unity

EPH 4:4–4:6 See also 1CO 12:13

Extending hospitality

ACT 28:7; ; ROM 12:13, ROM 16:23; ; 1TI 5:10; ; TIT 1:8; ; 1PE 4:9; ; 3JN 1:8

Greeting one another

ACT 18:27; ; ROM 16:3–16:16; ; 1CO 16:19–16:20; ; COL 4:10; ; PHM 1:17

Welcoming former opponents

ACT 9:26–9:27; ; GAL 2:9; ; 2CO 2:5–2:8

Divisions in the church

Causes of division in the NT church

Personal ambition: MRK 9:34, MRK 10:35–10:41 parallel MAT 20:20–20:24; ACT 6:1; ACT 15:37–15:40; ; PHP 4:2; ROM 16:17; ; JUD 1:19; 1CO 1:11–1:12, 1CO 3:3–3:4; 1CO 6:1–6:6 litigation and disputes; 1CO 11:18, 1CO 11:20–11:21; ; JAS 4:1–4:3

Warnings against divisions in the church

1CO 1:10 See also ROM 12:16, ROM 16:17; 2CO 12:20; EPH 4:31; JAS 4:11

Acceptable differences in the church

In secondary matters of conscience, Christians are to respect rather than judge each other. These things need not impair the essential unity that is in Christ: ROM 14:1–14:3, ROM 14:5–14:6; ; 1CO 8:9–8:13; 1CO 12:4–12:6, 1CO 12:14–12:25; ; GAL 2:7

Necessary divisions in the church

Between the true gospel and heretical alternatives: 2CO 11:2–11:6, 2CO 11:13–11:15; ; GAL 1:6–1:9; ; COL 2:8, COL 2:16–2:19; ; 1TI 4:1–4:6; ; 1JN 2:18–2:19; ; 2JN 1:9–1:11; ; JUD 1:18–1:20; 1CO 5:9–5:10; ; 2TH 3:6; ; 1TI 6:3–6:5; ; 2TI 3:2–3:9; ; 2PE 1:20–1:21, 2PE 2:1–2:3; ; REV 2:20, REV 2:24, REV 3:1, REV 3:4; ACT 15:2, ACT 15:5–15:6, ACT 15:19; ; GAL 2:11

title: 7026: church, leadership of
content:
Jesus Christ is the absolute head of the church. He sets leaders in the church to enable the whole church to grow into maturity. Christ's authority in the church is acknowledged more by the church's obedience to God than through any particular form of government.

Jesus Christ alone is head of the church

COL 1:18 See also MAT 23:8–23:10; EPH 1:22, EPH 4:15, EPH 5:23; COL 2:19; HEB 3:3

The Holy Spirit directs the church

ACT 13:2 See also ACT 15:28, ACT 16:6–16:7, ACT 20:28; ROM 8:14; 1CO 12:11; REV 2:7, REV 2:11

The appointment of leaders in the church

God calls and equips leaders

EPH 4:11 See also MAT 16:18; ACT 1:24–1:26, ACT 9:15–9:16, ACT 20:28, ACT 26:16–26:18; 1CO 12:28; GAL 1:15–1:17

Delegated leadership

ACT 6:3–6:6, ACT 14:23; ; TIT 1:5

The appointment of apostles

MRK 3:13–3:19 parallels MAT 10:1–10:4, LUK 6:12–6:16 1CO 9:1–9:2; ; 2CO 3:3; ; EPH 2:20; ; REV 21:14; ACT 2:42, ACT 15:6, ACT 15:22–15:23; ; 1TH 2:6; ; 2PE 3:2; ; JUD 1:17

Prophets as leaders

ACT 15:32 Judas and Silas were leaders in the Jerusalem church (Ac 15:22). See also ACT 11:27–11:30, ACT 13:1–13:2; ROM 12:6 The role of prophets as leaders is distinct from the gift of prophecy, which was in principle available to all; 1CO 12:28, 1CO 14:29–14:30; EPH 3:5

Evangelists as leaders

ACT 21:8 See also EPH 4:11; 2TI 4:5

Pastors and teachers as leaders

ACT 20:28 See also JHN 21:15–21:17; ACT 13:1; ROM 12:7; 1CO 12:28; 1TI 3:2; TIT 1:9; JAS 3:1; 1PE 5:2 Pastors are also called “shepherds”.

Elders as leaders

1TI 3:1 “Elder” and “overseer” or “bishop” are more or less interchangeable. See also ACT 11:30, ACT 14:23, ACT 15:2, ACT 15:22, ACT 20:17; 1TI 5:17; TIT 1:5; JAS 5:14; 2JN 1:1

Deacons as leaders

PHP 1:1 “Deacon” means “one who serves”. See also ACT 6:5–6:6; 1TI 3:8

Qualifications for church leadership

The first apostles were witnesses of Jesus Christ's life and resurrection: ACT 1:21–1:22, ACT 10:41; ; 1CO 9:1–9:2, 1CO 15:7–15:8; ; 2PE 1:16; ACT 6:3; ; 1TI 3:1–3:12, 1TI 5:17; ; TIT 1:6–1:9; ; 1PE 5:1–5:4

Responsibilities of church leaders

To preach the gospel

ROM 1:15; ; 1CO 1:17; ; GAL 2:8; ; EPH 3:8; ; 1TI 2:7

To teach sound doctrine

1TI 4:6, 1TI 4:13, 1TI 5:17; ; HEB 13:7

To give direction in church life

ACT 15:2, ACT 15:6, ACT 15:22–15:23, ACT 16:4, ACT 20:28–20:31; ; 1TI 5:17; ; 1PE 5:2

To be an example in loving service

MAT 20:26–20:28 parallel MRK 10:43–10:45; ; MRK 9:35; ; JHN 13:13–13:15; ; HEB 13:7; ; 1PE 5:3

To train and appoint other leaders

ACT 14:23; ; 1TI 4:14; ; 2TI 2:2; ; TIT 1:5

To pray for the sick

JAS 5:14

To exercise discipline in the church

2CO 13:10; ; 1TH 5:12; ; 1TI 1:20, 1TI 5:20; ; TIT 3:10; ; 3JN 1:10

The church's responsibilities to its leaders

To respect and submit to its leaders

ACT 16:4; ; 1TH 5:12–5:13; ; 1TI 5:19; ; HEB 13:17

To pray for its leaders

EPH 6:19; ; 1TH 5:25

To support its leaders financially

1CO 9:7–9:14; ; PHP 4:15–4:19; ; 1TI 5:17–5:18

The corporate government of the church

In choosing leaders

ACT 6:3–6:6

In implementing decisions

ACT 15:22–15:29

In building up the church

ROM 12:4–12:8; ; 1CO 12:4–12:12, 1CO 12:27; ; EPH 4:3, EPH 4:7–4:16; ; 1PE 4:10–4:11

In discerning true and false teachings

1JN 4:1–4:3; ; 2JN 1:10; ; REV 2:2

In exercising discipline

MAT 18:15–18:20; ; 1CO 5:4–5:5; ; 2CO 2:6–2:8; ; 2TH 3:14–3:15

The structure of the church

The pattern of church life

ACT 2:42 See also ACT 2:46, ACT 5:42

The house church

ACT 1:13–1:14, ACT 12:12, ACT 16:40; ; ROM 16:5; ; 1CO 16:19; ; COL 4:15; ; PHM 1:2

The local church

ACT 13:1; ; ROM 16:1; ; 1CO 1:2 It is unknown whether there were subdivisions of the church in towns such as Corinth; ; 1TH 1:1

Churches in a region

ACT 9:31, ACT 15:41; ; 1CO 16:1; ; 2CO 8:1; ; GAL 1:2, GAL 1:22; ; REV 1:4 Normally the NT speaks of “churches” in an area rather than of a unified regional structure.

The universal church

MAT 16:18 1CO 12:28; ; EPH 1:22, EPH 3:10, EPH 5:25

resource.label: 2212: Jesus Christ, head of the church

title: 7027: church, purpose and mission of
content:
The church is called to praise and glorify God, to establish Jesus Christ's kingdom, and to proclaim the gospel throughout the world.

God's purposes for the church

To praise God

1PE 2:9 See also EPH 1:5–1:6, EPH 1:11–1:12, EPH 1:14; HEB 13:15; 1PE 2:5

To share God's glory

ROM 8:29–8:30 See also MAT 13:43; JHN 17:24; ROM 9:23; 1CO 2:7; PHP 3:21; COL 3:4; 2TH 2:14; REV 2:26–2:27, REV 3:4–3:5, REV 3:21

God will build his church

MAT 16:18–16:19 See also MAT 27:40 parallel MRK 15:29; JHN 2:19–2:22; 1CO 3:9; EPH 2:21–2:22, EPH 4:11–4:13; HEB 3:3–3:6; 1PE 2:5

To challenge Satan's dominion

EPH 3:10–3:11 “rulers and authorities in the heavenly realms” refers to the powers of evil. See also MAT 16:18; EPH 6:12; 1JN 2:14

To go into the world in mission

2CO 5:18 See also MAT 5:13–5:16, MAT 28:19–28:20; MRK 16:15; LUK 24:48; JHN 20:21; ACT 1:8; PHP 2:15–2:16; COL 1:27

The church's mission

To preach the gospel to the world

MRK 13:10 parallel MAT 24:14 See also MAT 28:19; LUK 24:47; JHN 10:16; ACT 13:47

To do good to all

GAL 6:10 See also MAT 25:37–25:40; LUK 6:35; ACT 9:36; EPH 2:10; 1TI 6:18; JAS 1:27; 1PE 2:12

Images of the church's mission

MAT 5:13–5:16; JHN 15:5–15:8 MAT 7:18–7:19; ; ROM 7:4; ; EPH 5:9–5:10; ; PHP 1:11; ; COL 1:6, COL 1:10; ; JAS 3:17; MRK 9:50; ; LUK 14:34–14:35; ROM 13:12–13:14; ; EPH 5:8; ; PHP 2:15; ; 1TH 5:5–5:6

The growth of the church

Numerical growth among the first Christians

ACT 11:21 See also ACT 2:41, ACT 2:47, ACT 4:4, ACT 5:14, ACT 6:1, ACT 6:7, ACT 9:31, ACT 9:42, ACT 11:24, ACT 12:24, ACT 13:49, ACT 16:5, ACT 17:4, ACT 18:8, ACT 19:20

The church is to grow to maturity

EPH 4:12–4:13 See also PHP 1:6, PHP 3:13–3:15; 2TH 1:3

Aspects of growth

Growth in character: 2CO 9:10; ; 1TH 3:12; EPH 4:15; ; COL 1:10; ; 2PE 3:18; HEB 6:1 growth in understanding

Prayers for the growth of the church

EPH 3:14–3:19 See also EPH 1:17–1:19; PHP 1:9–1:11; COL 1:9–1:12; 1TH 3:11–3:13; 2TH 1:11–1:12

Visions of the church's final destiny

REV 7:9–7:10 John's vision of the church in glory. See also MAT 24:31; JHN 10:16; EPH 1:10; 1TH 4:16–4:17; HEB 12:22–12:23; REV 21:2

title: 7028: church, life of
content:
The church lives its life in union with Christ and in the power of the Holy Spirit. It is called to mutual love, holy living, and to worship.

The church lives its life in union with Christ

The church lives in Christ and Christ lives in the church

1CO 1:30 See also MAT 18:20; JHN 15:5, JHN 17:21; 1CO 8:6; GAL 2:20; COL 1:27, COL 2:6; 1JN 4:13

Believers are united with Christ in baptism

GAL 3:27 See also ACT 2:38, ACT 19:5; ROM 6:3–6:4; COL 2:12

The church lives its life in the power of the Holy Spirit

The church lives in the Spirit, and the Spirit lives in the church

ROM 8:9–8:11 The indwelling Spirit is identified with the presence of Jesus Christ. See also 1CO 3:16; GAL 5:16, GAL 5:25; EPH 2:22; 2TI 1:14; 1JN 2:27

The Spirit is given to the church

ACT 1:4–1:5 See also JHN 20:22; ACT 2:4, ACT 15:8; ROM 7:6, ROM 15:16; 1CO 6:11; GAL 3:3; 1PE 1:1–1:2

The Spirit seals the church

EPH 1:13 See also 2CO 1:22, 2CO 5:5; EPH 4:30

The Spirit guides the church

REV 2:7 See also ACT 8:29, ACT 10:19, ACT 11:12, ACT 13:2, ACT 20:23, ACT 21:4

The Spirit teaches the church

1CO 2:13 See also JHN 16:13; EPH 1:17

The Spirit sanctifies the church

ROM 15:16 See also 1CO 6:11; 2TH 2:13; 1PE 1:2

The Spirit endows the church with gifts

1CO 12:7 See also ROM 12:6–12:8; 1CO 12:8–12:11, 1CO 12:28; EPH 4:11

The church as a fellowship

As a fellowship of mutual love

1TH 5:11 See also ROM 15:2; 1CO 14:12; GAL 6:10; 1TH 4:18; HEB 10:25

As a fellowship of ordinary people

1CO 1:26–1:27 See also LUK 6:20; JAS 2:5

The distinctiveness of the church

The church as a chosen people

1PE 2:9 See also EPH 1:4; COL 3:12

The church as a holy people

HEB 12:14 See also ROM 11:16, ROM 15:16; 1CO 1:2; EPH 2:21, EPH 5:3; 1TH 3:13; HEB 2:11; 1PE 1:15–1:16; 2PE 3:11

The church as a people set apart

2CO 7:1 The repeated call to be separate from evil is the practical outworking of the church's sanctification. See also 1CO 5:9–5:12; 2CO 6:14–6:18; EPH 5:7; 2TH 3:14; 2TI 3:1–3:5; TIT 3:10–3:11

The church as a heavenly people

JHN 17:14–17:16 See also LUK 10:20; 1CO 15:48; GAL 4:26; EPH 2:6; PHP 3:20; COL 3:1–3:4; HEB 11:16, HEB 12:22–12:23

The church and worship

Praise in the life of the church

HEB 13:15 See also ACT 2:47; 1CO 14:26; EPH 5:18–5:20; PHP 3:3; COL 3:16

Prayer in the life of the church

Corporate prayer in the church: ACT 1:14, ACT 2:42, ACT 4:24–4:31, ACT 12:5, ACT 12:12, ACT 20:36, ACT 21:5; ROM 15:30; ; EPH 6:19–6:20; ; COL 4:2–4:4; ; 1TH 5:25; ; 2TH 3:1; ; HEB 13:18; EPH 6:18 prayer for all Christians; ; 1TI 2:1–2:2 prayer for all, including secular leaders

Baptism in the life of the church

ACT 2:38; ; 1CO 1:13; ; GAL 3:26–3:27; ; EPH 4:4–4:5; ; COL 2:11–2:12; ; 1PE 3:21

title: 7030: unity
content: The bringing together of separate or fragmented parts into a unified whole. It is God's ultimate goal to unite the whole of creation; his desire for unity is evident, too, in the life of his people. God himself acts as a unity in all his works.

title: 7031: unity, God's goal of
content:
God's purpose is to bring together and reconcile to himself the whole of creation. This desire for unity is a reflection of the unity within the Godhead; it is expressed in marriage, and demonstrated in the fellowship of the church.

Unity is a reflection of God's person

DEU 6:4; JHN 17:22 See also MRK 12:29, MRK 12:32; JHN 1:1, JHN 17:11; GAL 3:20; 1JN 5:7

Alienation is a result of sin

Alienation from God

GEN 3:8, GEN 3:23–3:24; ; ISA 59:2; ; EZK 28:16; ; JAS 4:4

Alienation from one another

GEN 4:7–4:9, GEN 4:23–4:24, GEN 6:11; ; JAS 4:1–4:2; ; JUD 1:19

God's purpose in unity

To bring together all peoples

PSA 133:1; EPH 1:9–1:10

To unite all peoples under his rule

ZEC 14:9 See also GEN 49:10; PSA 72:8–72:11; ISA 2:2–2:4 parallel MIC 4:1–4:3; JHN 10:16; PHP 2:9–2:11; REV 11:15

To unite all peoples in worship

ISA 19:23–19:25 See also PSA 86:9; ISA 56:6–56:8; ZEC 8:20–8:23, ZEC 14:16; MRK 11:17; REV 15:4

To bring reconciliation between Jew and Gentile

EPH 2:11–2:19 See also ISA 14:1; ROM 10:12; GAL 3:28; COL 3:11

To bring reconciliation among his people

JER 50:4 See also ISA 11:10–11:13; EZK 37:21–37:24; MIC 2:12; MAT 5:24; PHP 4:2

To reconcile humanity to himself

COL 1:19–1:22 See also ROM 5:10–5:11; 2CO 5:18–5:19; HEB 10:19–10:20

Expressions of unity brought by God

Unity between husband and wife

GEN 2:24 See also MAL 2:15; MAT 19:5–19:6 parallel MRK 10:7–10:8; 1CO 6:16, 1CO 7:4, 1CO 7:10–7:11

Marriage as an expression of unity between God and his people

JER 2:2; EPH 5:31–5:32 See also ISA 54:5; JER 31:32; HOS 2:16; JHN 3:29; REV 19:7, REV 21:2, REV 21:9

Unity within the church

JHN 17:21 See also ACT 2:1, ACT 4:32; 1CO 1:10, 1CO 10:17, 1CO 12:12; EPH 4:13

title: 7032: unity, of God's people
content:
A distinguishing characteristic of God's people, which derives from their common relationship with God, and is expressed in commitment to one another, mutual concern, concerted action and harmony within the believing community.

As children of God

As the people of God

1PE 2:9–2:10 See also EXO 6:7, EXO 19:5–19:6; DEU 7:6; 2SA 7:24 parallel 1CH 17:22; MAL 3:17; EPH 1:14; HEB 11:25

United in purpose

PHP 1:27 See also JDG 20:11; 1SA 14:7; EZR 3:8–3:10; NEH 4:6; MAT 18:19–18:20

Of one mind

1CO 1:10 See also 2CH 30:12; PHP 2:2

Unity expressed in worshipping together

ROM 15:5–15:6 See also PSA 22:22, PSA 68:26, PSA 122:1; 1CO 14:26; HEB 2:12

Unity marks out God's people

JHN 17:21–17:23 See also JHN 13:34–13:35

title: 7105: believers
content:
Those who have faith in God. After Pentecost, the term came to be used specifically to refer to Christians, that is, to those who believe and trust in God, as he has made himself known through Jesus Christ. The NT stresses that Jesus Christ brings the faith of OT believers to perfection and to its proper fulfilment.

Believers as those who have faith in God

1KI 18:3–18:4 See also JHN 4:39–4:42; ACT 16:15; JAS 2:1

OT believers looked forward to the NT faith

HEB 11:13 See also HEB 11:39; 1PE 1:10–1:12

The church is referred to as the believers

ACT 5:12 See also ACT 1:15, ACT 2:44, ACT 4:32, ACT 9:41; 1TI 4:12

In the early church a distinction was sometimes made between Jewish and Gentile believers

ACT 15:23 See also ACT 10:45, ACT 11:1–11:3, ACT 15:5, ACT 21:20–21:25

The term believer is synonymous with Christian

1CO 7:12–7:13 See also ACT 15:2, ACT 16:1; 1CO 6:5, 1CO 14:22; 2CO 6:14–6:15; 1TH 1:7; 1TI 5:16

Believers are members of God's family

GAL 6:10 See also 1TI 6:2; 1PE 2:17

title: 7110: body of Christ
content:
The term refers both to the physical body of Jesus Christ, which was broken on the cross in order that redemption might be accomplished through him, and to the church.

Jesus Christ's physical body

His birth

JHN 1:14 See also ROM 1:3; GAL 4:4; 1TI 3:16

His bodily needs

MAT 8:24 parallels MRK 4:38, LUK 8:23; ; MRK 11:12 parallel MAT 21:18; ; LUK 4:2 parallel MAT 4:2; ; JHN 4:6, JHN 19:28

His physical suffering

MAT 27:26 parallels MRK 15:15, JHN 19:1 See also MAT 27:28–27:30 parallels MRK 15:17–15:19, JHN 19:2–19:3; LUK 22:44

His real physical death

JHN 19:31–19:37 See also MAT 27:35 parallels MRK 15:24–15:25, LUK 23:33, JHN 19:23; ACT 2:23

His burial

MRK 15:43–15:46 parallels MAT 27:58–27:60, LUK 23:52–23:53, JHN 19:38–19:42

His physical resurrection

LUK 24:37–24:43 See also MAT 28:9; JHN 20:19–20:20, JHN 20:27

The body of Christ symbolised in the Lord's Supper

MAT 26:26 parallels MRK 14:22, LUK 22:19 See also JHN 6:51–6:58; 1CO 10:16, 1CO 11:23–11:30

The church as the body of Christ

1CO 12:27 See also ROM 12:4–12:5; 1CO 12:12; EPH 4:4; COL 1:24

COL 1:18 See also EPH 1:22, EPH 5:23

JHN 2:19–2:22 See also EPH 2:19–2:22

1CO 12:13 See also GAL 3:27

Believers receive spiritual life through the body of Christ

COL 2:19 See also 1CO 10:16–10:17; EPH 4:15–4:16

Believers care for each other in the body of Christ

1CO 12:25–12:26 See also EPH 4:25, EPH 5:29–5:30; COL 3:15

Believers have different gifts in the body of Christ

ROM 12:4–12:5 See also ROM 12:6–12:8; 1CO 12:12–12:31; EPH 4:11–4:13

title: 7115: children of God
content:
Israel and the church are the chief objects of God's fatherly love. People become God's children through faith and are then encouraged by Scripture to imitate their heavenly Father in their daily living.

Human beings as God's children

GEN 1:27, GEN 6:2, GEN 6:4; ; PSA 82:6; ; MAL 2:10; ; LUK 3:38; ; EPH 3:14

Israel as God's children

HOS 11:1 See also EXO 4:22–4:23; PSA 73:15, PSA 80:15; ISA 43:6, ISA 63:16; JER 31:20; ROM 9:4

Israel as God's rebellious children

ISA 1:2 See also DEU 32:5, DEU 32:20; ISA 30:1, ISA 30:9; JER 3:4–3:5, JER 4:22; EZK 20:21

God's son

Promises of God's son

2SA 7:12–7:14 parallel 1CH 17:11–17:13 These words referred originally to David's son, Solomon, but in the NT they are applied to Jesus Christ. See also 1CH 22:10, 1CH 28:6; PSA 2:7, PSA 89:26–89:27

Jesus Christ as God's son

HEB 1:5 See also MAT 3:17 parallels MRK 1:11, LUK 3:22; LUK 1:32; ACT 13:33; ROM 1:4

Jesus Christ's followers as God's children

MAT 9:2 parallel MRK 2:5; ; MAT 9:22 parallels MRK 5:34, LUK 8:48

Becoming God's children through faith in Jesus Christ

Becoming God's children by new birth

JHN 1:12–1:13 See also JHN 12:36; ROM 9:8; GAL 3:26; 1JN 5:1

Becoming God's children by adoption

EPH 1:5 See also ROM 8:23 This adoption will be fully realised in the future; GAL 4:4–4:5

Becoming God's children by the Spirit

ROM 8:15 See also GAL 4:6

God's dealings with his children

God loves his children

1JN 3:1 See also DEU 1:31; PSA 103:13; EPH 5:1

God disciplines his children

DEU 8:5 See also PRO 3:11–3:12; HEB 12:5–12:10

God does not abandon his children

HOS 1:10 God had previously (Hos 1:9) announced his judgment that Israel would be his people no longer. See also DEU 31:6, DEU 31:8; JOS 1:5; HEB 13:5; JER 31:9; MAL 3:17; LUK 15:24; ROM 9:26

Behaviour expected of God's children

God's children imitate their heavenly Father

EPH 5:1 See also MAT 5:48; LUK 6:36

God's children live by the Father's standards

PHP 2:15 See also LEV 11:44–11:45; DEU 14:1–14:2; MAT 5:9, MAT 5:44–5:45 parallel LUK 6:35–6:36; ROM 8:14; 2CO 6:17–6:18; 1PE 1:14–1:16; 1JN 3:10

JHN 8:35 See also ROM 8:16, ROM 8:21; 1JN 5:19

MAT 7:9–7:11 parallel LUK 11:11–11:13 See also MAT 6:25–6:32 parallel LUK 12:22–12:30 needs for the present life; LUK 20:36; ROM 8:23 promises for the life to come

God's children have a sure inheritance

REV 21:7 See also JER 3:19; ROM 8:17–8:19; GAL 4:7; 1JN 3:2

title: 7120: Christians
content:
Those who belong to Jesus Christ; other designations in Scripture are believers, disciples and the children of God. Originally a nickname, the term is only used three times in the Bible.

The use of the term

Disciples first called Christians

ACT 11:26

Agrippa not persuaded

ACT 26:28

Suffering as a Christian

1PE 4:16

Other biblical designations of Christians

Christians described as believers

ACT 2:44 See also ACT 4:32, ACT 5:12; GAL 6:10; JAS 2:1; 1PE 2:17

Christians described as brothers

HEB 2:11 See also ACT 15:3; COL 1:2; 2TH 2:13; REV 19:10

Christians described as disciples

MAT 28:19 See also ACT 6:1, ACT 6:7, ACT 13:52, ACT 14:22

Christians described as children of God

1JN 3:1 See also JHN 1:12; ROM 8:16, ROM 8:21; GAL 3:26–4:7

ACT 12:5 See also ACT 5:11, ACT 8:1, ACT 15:3; ROM 16:5; 2CO 1:1; COL 1:18, COL 1:24; 1TI 3:15

Christians described as the elect

1PE 1:1 See also MAT 24:22; ROM 11:7; 2TI 2:10; TIT 1:1

ROM 1:7 See also ROM 15:25; 1CO 6:2; 2CO 1:1, 2CO 13:13; EPH 1:15, EPH 1:18; 1TI 5:10; JUD 1:3

content:
Those chosen by God, entirely of his grace, for the special privilege of experiencing salvation.

DEU 7:6 See also EXO 19:4–19:6; PSA 105:6, PSA 105:43, PSA 135:4; ISA 41:8–41:9, ISA 43:10, ISA 44:1–44:2, ISA 45:4; AMO 3:2; ROM 11:1–11:18

Abraham

GEN 18:18–18:19

Moses

PSA 106:23

David

1KI 8:16; ; PSA 89:3–89:4

Rufus

ROM 16:13

The elect are assured of the protection of God

MAT 24:22 parallel MRK 13:20 See also ROM 8:33–8:39; REV 17:14

MRK 13:26–13:27 parallel MAT 24:30–24:31 See also ISA 65:17–65:25; JHN 14:1–14:3; JAS 2:5; 2PE 1:10–1:11

content:
God's people are frequently portrayed as a flock of sheep, for whom he cares with the tenderness and concern of a loving shepherd.

The divine shepherd

God portrayed as a shepherd

PSA 23:1 See also PSA 80:1

Jesus Christ portrayed as a shepherd

JHN 10:11 See also MAT 26:31; ZEC 13:7

God's people are his flock

Israel and Judah depicted as God's flock

PSA 77:20 See also PSA 78:52; ISA 63:11; JER 13:17, JER 31:10, JER 50:17; MIC 2:12, MIC 7:14

NT believers depicted as God's flock

ACT 20:28 See also LUK 12:32; JHN 10:14–10:16

God cares for his flock

ISA 40:11 See also PSA 95:6–95:7; EZK 34:11–34:16; MIC 5:4; ZEC 9:16

God judges his flock

EZK 34:17–34:24 See also ZEC 11:7–11:16

EZK 34:7–34:10 See also JER 23:1–23:5, JER 25:34–25:36; ZEC 10:3, ZEC 11:17

title: 7135: Israel, as the people of God
content:
God called Israel out of Egypt to be his own treasured possession and his covenant faithfulness with them is maintained, despite their persistent disobedience.

God's election of Israel

The nation was founded upon God's promises to the patriarchs

GEN 12:1–12:3 See also GEN 17:1–17:8, GEN 35:9–35:13

Israel as God's chosen possession

1KI 8:53 See also EXO 19:3–19:6; DEU 7:6, DEU 32:7–32:12; PSA 135:3–135:4

God redeemed Israel from Egypt

EXO 6:6–6:7 See also EXO 3:7–3:10; 2SA 7:22–7:24 parallel 1CH 17:20–17:22

God enters into a special covenant relationship with Israel

EXO 24:3–24:8

Israel's privileged status within the covenant

ROM 9:4–9:5 See also ROM 3:1–3:2; PHP 3:4–3:6

Israel's disobedience to the covenant

Israel provoked God in the wilderness

PSA 78:40–78:41 See also EXO 32:1–32:10; NUM 11:1–11:6, NUM 14:1–14:4

Israel's unfaithfulness in the promised land

ISA 1:4 See also JDG 2:10–2:13; JER 11:9–11:10; EZK 20:27–20:28

Israel exiled for her sins

2KI 18:11–18:12 See also 2KI 25:8–25:12 parallels JER 39:8–39:10, JER 52:12–52:16

God's promise to restore Israel

God's promise not to abandon Israel completely

DEU 4:31 See also LEV 26:44–26:45; ISA 6:11–6:13 Though Israel will be destroyed, a holy remnant will be left; JER 31:35–31:37

God's promise to bring Israel back to the land

JER 30:3 See also ISA 43:5–43:7; EZK 37:11–37:14

God's promise to reunite Israel

JER 3:18 See also EZK 37:15–37:23; HOS 1:10–1:11

The promise of a restored relationship between Israel and God

JER 31:31–31:34 See also ISA 40:1–40:2; EZK 36:24–36:28

Israel returns from exile

EZR 1:1–1:5 parallel 2CH 36:22–36:23

Jesus Christ, the Saviour of Israel

Jesus Christ's ministry was specifically to the nation of Israel

MAT 10:5–10:6 See also MAT 15:21–15:28 parallel MRK 7:24–7:30

Jesus accepts the title ``Christ'' (Messiah), God's anointed deliverer for Israel

MAT 16:16–16:17 parallels MRK 8:29, LUK 9:20 Although Jesus accepted the title “Messiah” (Christ), he did not fulfil contemporary Israelite hopes of a political deliverer.

Jesus Christ declares to Israel the good news of God's reign

MRK 1:14–1:15 Jesus Christ takes up Isaiah's message of good news, that God is coming in sovereign power to restore Israel (see Isa 40:9–10, 52:7). See also MAT 4:23, MAT 9:35; LUK 8:1

Israel warned that rejection of Christ means exclusion from the kingdom

MAT 8:11–8:12 See also MAT 21:33–21:46 parallels MRK 12:1–12:12, LUK 20:9–20:19; MAT 22:1–22:10 parallel LUK 14:16–14:24; LUK 13:28–13:30

Israel's rejection of the gospel

ROM 9:30–10:4 See also ACT 28:24–28:28; ISA 6:9–6:10; 1TH 2:14–2:16

God's faithfulness to Israel maintained

Membership of the true Israel is based on God's election, not merely on physical descent from Abraham

ROM 9:6–9:18

A remnant of Israel has been elected by grace

ROM 11:1–11:12

Paul declares that ``all Israel'' will be saved

ISA 59:20–59:21; ; ROM 11:25–11:32 The phrase “all Israel” is interpreted as either: (1) the total number of the elect (either Jews, or Jews and Gentiles) of every generation, or (2) the great majority of Jews of the final generation who will be saved when Christ returns.

The church as the new Israel

GAL 6:15–6:16; PHP 3:3 See also 1PE 2:9–2:10

The church described as the 12 tribes of Israel

JAS 1:1 See also REV 7:4–7:8 Some see this as a reference to members of actual Jewish tribes, but others see it as symbolic of all the faithful believers who live through the time of tribulation; REV 21:12 The number 12 emphasises the continuity of the NT church and the OT people of God.

The apostles as the 12 leaders of the new Israel

MAT 19:28 See also LUK 22:29–22:30; REV 21:14

title: 7140: people of God
content: Those called out to belong to God and serve him in his world. God called Israel to be his people in the OT, anticicpating the calling of the church in the NT.

The OT relates how God called Israel to be his people.

The NT relates how the Christian church is the new people of God, assuming all the privileges and responsibilities of Israel in the OT, but with a universal mission to all the world, rather than any one nation.

God's chosen people

God's children

God's possession

A small group of people who remain faithful to God, despite widespread unbelief or faithlessness around them. God preserves a remnant in order to ensure a renewed and restored people in the future.

People from the northern kingdom of Israel who had moved to Judah and so escaped its destruction by Assyria: 2CH 11:14, 2CH 15:9, 2CH 31:6, 2CH 34:9

Judah as the remnant of God's people

title: 7203: ark, Noah's
content:
The craft built at God's command by Noah and his family as a means of escape from the flood. The ark is seen as corresponding to the redemption offered to believers through Jesus Christ.

The building of the ark

The command to build

GEN 6:14 See also GEN 6:1–6:13, GEN 6:15–6:16

Its purpose for Noah and his family

GEN 6:17–6:18

Its purpose for the animals

GEN 6:19 See also GEN 6:20–6:22

The ark and the flood

Noah obeyed God

GEN 7:5 See also GEN 7:1–7:4, GEN 7:6–7:16

The ark served its purpose

GEN 7:17 See also GEN 7:18–7:24, GEN 8:1–8:19, GEN 9:1–9:17

The significance of the ark elsewhere in Scripture

An instance of judgment

MAT 24:38–24:39 parallel LUK 17:26–17:27 Judgment clearly divides humanity into the saved and the lost. See also 2PE 2:5

An example of faith

HEB 11:7 Noah took God at his word in circumstances which must have appeared ridiculous.

A symbol of salvation

1PE 3:20–3:21 The link between the ark and baptism is in the twin ideas of water and salvation.

content:
A group, small or large, of people. In Scripture it denotes the people of Israel, especially in pre-monarchic times and particularly during the period of the wilderness wanderings.

content:
A period of absence from one's homeland, often imposed as a form of punishment. The OT regards Israel's exile in Babylon as a form of divine punishment for past sin and as an opportunity for renewal and rededication.

title: 7215: exile, the
content: The period of Israel's captivity in foreign lands, under Assyria in the eighth century B.C., and under Babylon in the sixth. Such exile is regarded by biblical writers as a judgment upon Israel for her folly and unfaithfulness. It is also seen as a time of repentance and renewal leading eventually to restoration.

title: 7216: exile, in Assyria
content:
People of the northern kingdom of Israel were taken into exile after their defeat by the Assyrians and the fall of Samaria. The Israelites were transported to Assyria in stages, starting with the upper strata of society. They were replaced by immigrants from other parts of the Assyrian empire. The Israelites had to share their land with these people when they returned from exile after the downfall of the Assyrians. This was the origin of the Samaritan people.

Israel was warned of coming punishment for her misconduct

God warned his people

DEU 28:62–28:66; ISA 5:12–5:13 See also LEV 26:27–26:28; DEU 28:49–28:57; ISA 10:5–10:6; HOS 8:1–8:8, HOS 13:16; AMO 3:2, AMO 3:11, AMO 5:27, AMO 9:4, AMO 9:7–9:9; MIC 1:6–1:7

Some prophecies referred specifically to Assyria as God's instrument

ISA 8:4–8:7, ISA 10:5–10:6 See also HOS 8:9–8:10; NAM 1:11–1:13

Warning through Solomon

1KI 8:46–8:50

The history of Israel's capture

Tiglath-Pileser III invades Israel in 743 B.C. but withdraws upon payment of slave-indemnity

See also 2KI 15:19–15:20

A further attack takes place ten years later

2KI 15:29; ; 1CH 5:26

Shalmaneser V comes on the scene

2KI 17:3–17:6 After a three-year siege, Samaria falls in 722 B.C.

Israelites are removed to Assyria under Sargon II

2KI 17:5–17:7 See also 2KI 17:23

HOS 1:10–1:11; ; AMO 9:14–9:15

The southern kingdom of Judah was conquered by Babylon under Nebuchadnezzar. Before and after the destruction of Jerusalem in 587 B.C. the population was taken into exile in three stages.

The entire episode was within the purposes of God and under his control

title: 7221: exodus, the
content: The escape of the Israelites from slavery in Egypt under Moses' leadership. Despite their great deliverance, hardness of heart and disobedience towards God persisted. Divine deliverance in the exodus foreshadowed the act of salvation through Jesus Christ.

title: 7222: exodus, events of
content:
The escape of the Israelites from slavery in Egypt and their deliverance in the crossing of the Red Sea. The beginning of a long and difficult journey under Moses' leadership to Canaan, the promised land.

God's promise of deliverance

EXO 6:1–6:8 See also EXO 3:7–3:10

The LORD smites the Egyptians with ten plagues

Pharaoh refuses, despite the plagues, to let the Israelites leave: EXO 11:9–11:10; ; PSA 105:26–105:36

The plague on the firstborn and the Passover

EXO 12:31 See also EXO 12:23

The Israelites leave Egypt

EXO 12:37–12:41; NUM 33:3–33:5 See also PSA 105:37–105:38

The crossing of the Red Sea

EXO 14:29–14:31 See also EXO 13:17–14:28

God's mighty deliverance remembered in confession

PSA 106:6–106:7; DAN 9:15

Events of the exodus: remembered in the Passover Feast

EXO 12:24–12:28 See also JOS 5:10

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 7223: exodus, spiritual significance of
content:
Scripture records the many instances of praise being given to God on account of the exodus. The events of the exodus, however, reveal the lack of faith, the hardness of heart and the spiritual stubbornness that can persist among believers. Scripture warns of the consequences of these things. In addition the exodus is seen as an act of divine deliverance which foreshadows the greater act of salvation through Jesus Christ.

How great God is: remember what he has done

The song of Moses and Miriam after the crossing of the Red Sea

EXO 15:1–15:4

The psalmist praises God for his deliverance in the exodus

PSA 77:19–77:20 See also PSA 78:12–78:14, PSA 78:51–78:53, PSA 105:26–105:45, PSA 106:7–106:12, PSA 114:1–114:4, PSA 136:13–136:16

HEB 4:2 See also HEB 3:1–4:13

The exodus as a sign of God's love

HOS 11:1

The exodus as an example of faith

HEB 11:29

title: 7227: flood, the
content:
A universal catastrophe by means of which the world was overwhelmed by water as the judgment of God on sinful humanity. Only Noah, his family and the animals were saved.

Details of the flood

Its source

See also GEN 7:4, GEN 7:11–7:12, GEN 8:2

Its extent

GEN 7:17–7:20

Its duration

GEN 7:24

Its effect

See also GEN 7:21–7:23

The flood was God's judgment on sinful humanity

Wickedness had become universal

GEN 6:5, GEN 6:11–6:13

Judgment would be universal

GEN 6:7, GEN 6:13, GEN 6:17, GEN 7:4

Judgment was universal

GEN 7:21–7:22

Those not destroyed by the flood

Noah, who was blameless in the midst of wickedness

GEN 6:8–6:9

Noah and his family kept safe in the ark

GEN 7:23 See also GEN 6:14, GEN 6:18, GEN 7:1, GEN 7:7, GEN 7:13, GEN 8:15–8:16, GEN 8:18; 2PE 2:5, 2PE 2:9

God kept the animals safe in the ark

GEN 6:19–6:21, GEN 7:2–7:3, GEN 7:8–7:9, GEN 7:14–7:16, GEN 8:1, GEN 8:17, GEN 8:19

God decided this in his heart

Of Jesus Christ's second coming

content:
In Scripture, lists of the descendants or forebears of an individual, or a record of the names of particular groups of people.

MAT 1:1–1:17 parallel LUK 3:23–3:38 See also GEN 10:1–10:32, GEN 11:10–11:32, GEN 36:10–36:43; 1CH 1:1–6:30, 1CH 9:35–9:44; HEB 7:3

Kinds of genealogies

Population lists

title: 7233: Israel, as northern kingdom
content:
The northernmost region of the two kingdoms, comprising the ten northern tribes which broke away from Judah in 930 B.C. and remained independent until the Assyrian conquest of 722 B.C., when the nation was exiled because of its sinfulness.

Origins of the division between Judah and Israel

Israelite settlement in Canaan had developed around two distinct regions in the north and south

JOS 18:5

The northern Israelite tribes were less ready to transfer allegiance from Saul to David

2SA 2:10 See also 2SA 19:41–20:2 Israel seems to have been ready to follow Sheba, because he came from the same tribe as Saul.

Israel's separation from Judah was the result of disobedience under Solomon

1KI 11:26–11:33

Rehoboam's folly provoked Israel's rebellion

1KI 12:1–12:17 parallel 2CH 10:1–10:17

Israel's foreign affairs

Israel at war with Judah (c. 930–886 B.C.)

1KI 14:30 parallel 2CH 12:15; ; 2CH 13:2–13:3, 2CH 16:1 parallel 1KI 15:16–15:17

Israel's wars with Aram (c. 886–753 B.C.)

1KI 15:18–15:20 parallel 2CH 16:2–16:4; ; 1KI 20:1, 1KI 20:26; ; 2KI 6:24–6:25, 2KI 10:32–10:33, 2KI 13:3, 2KI 13:24–13:25, 2KI 14:28

Israel's alliance with Judah against Aram and Moab (853 B.C.)

1KI 22:1–22:4 parallel 2CH 18:1–18:3; ; 2KI 3:4–3:7

Israel attacked by Assyria (c. 743–734 B.C.)

2KI 15:19–15:20, 2KI 15:29

Israel's alliance with Aram against Judah (c. 735/734 B.C.)

2KI 16:5; ; ISA 7:1

1KI 12:26–12:33 See also 2KI 10:29

1KI 16:32–16:33

See also HOS 12:11

Israel's worship condemned

The corruption of Israel's priests condemned

HOS 6:9 See also HOS 4:6–4:9

HOS 5:7 See also 2KI 17:7–17:23; HOS 4:10–4:13

God's call to Israel to repent

1KI 17:1 See also AMO 4:6–4:11

God's rejection of Israel

AMO 5:26–5:27 See also HOS 10:5–10:8; AMO 3:11–3:12

God's intention to restore Israel

HOS 1:10 See also HOS 2:23, HOS 14:4–14:8; AMO 9:11–9:15

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 7236: Israel, as united kingdom
content:
The 12 tribes proclaimed Saul as their first king in 1050 B.C., and remained united under David and Solomon, until the rebellion of the ten northern tribes in 930 B.C.

Israel's request for a king

Israel requested a king because it had been governed by corrupt judges

1SA 8:1–8:5

Israel requested a king to be like the other nations

1SA 8:19–8:20

Israel requested a king because of the threat from Ammon

1SA 12:12

Israel's request for a king seen as a rejection of God

1SA 8:6–8:7 See also 1SA 10:19, 1SA 12:16–12:19

Israel under Saul (1050–1010 B.C.)

Israel oppressed by the Philistines

Saul chosen as Israel's deliverer

Israel victorious under Saul

7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 7239: Jerusalem
content: The city in southern Palestine, situated in the Judean hills, around 20 miles west of the Dead Sea. Established as the Israelite capital by David, it remained the capital of Judah from the reign of Rehoboam until its destruction by the Romans in A.D. 70.

title: 7240: Jerusalem, history of
content:
Jerusalem was originally a Jebusite city, occupied by Israel at the time of David. At this stage, it became the capital city of Israel. Following the disintegration of the united kingdom, the city became the capital of the southern kindom of Judah.

Pre-Israelite Jerusalem

Inhabited by Jebusites

JDG 19:9–19:12 See also GEN 10:15–10:16, GEN 15:18–15:21; EXO 3:7–3:8, EXO 23:23, EXO 34:11; NUM 13:29; DEU 7:1–7:2; JOS 3:10, JOS 9:1–9:2, JOS 10:1–10:7, JOS 15:7–15:8, JOS 18:28

Israelites unable to occupy Jerusalem

JOS 15:63 See also JDG 1:4–1:8 Although the men of Judah defeated the Jebusites in this battle, their victory was not sufficient to conquer them nor to gain permanent control over the city; JDG 1:21

Jerusalem conquered by David

1CH 11:4–11:9 parallel 2SA 5:6–5:10

Jerusalem as the site of the ark

See also 2SA 6:12–6:19 parallel 1CH 15:25–16:3

Jerusalem capital city of David and Solomon

2SA 5:4–5:5 See also 2SA 9:13, 2SA 11:1, 2SA 12:29–12:31 parallel 1CH 20:1–20:3; 2SA 14:23–14:24, 2SA 15:13–15:14; 1KI 2:10–2:12, 1KI 2:36–2:45, 1KI 3:1, 1KI 8:1, 1KI 10:1–10:5, 1KI 10:26–10:27, 1KI 11:41–11:43

Jerusalem capital of the southern kingdom

1KI 14:21 See also 1KI 12:16–12:19 parallel 2CH 10:16–10:19; 1KI 12:25–12:27, 1KI 15:9–15:10, 1KI 22:42 parallel 2CH 20:31; 2KI 8:16–8:17 parallel 2CH 21:5; 2KI 12:1 parallel 2CH 24:1; 2KI 15:1–15:2 parallel 2CH 26:3; 2KI 18:1–18:2 parallel 2CH 29:1; 2KI 18:13–18:35 parallels 2CH 32:9–32:19, ISA 36:1–36:22; 2KI 21:1 parallel 2CH 33:1; 2KI 23:31–23:33 parallel 2CH 36:2–36:3

Jerusalem conquered by the Babylonians

2KI 24:18–25:12 parallels 2CH 36:11–36:20, JER 39:1–39:10, JER 52:1–52:16

The rebuilding of Jerusalem

See also 2CH 36:23; EZR 1:1–1:4; NEH 2:1–6:19

NEH 1:1–1:3 See also EZR 3:1, EZR 4:4–4:6, EZR 10:7; NEH 13:6–13:7, NEH 13:15–13:16

Jerusalem in NT times

MAT 2:1–2:3 See also MAT 3:1–3:6 parallel MRK 1:3–1:5; MAT 4:25, MAT 15:1 parallel MRK 7:1; LUK 2:22, LUK 2:41, LUK 4:9; JHN 11:55; ACT 9:1–9:2, ACT 25:1–25:3

LUK 21:20–21:24 See also LUK 23:26–23:31

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 7241: Jerusalem, significance of
content:
Chosen by God as the location for his sanctuary, Jerusalem was his symbolic dwelling and became the only acceptable place for sacrificial worship. It retained its symbolic significance in NT times when it came to represent the hope of restoration and renewal for the people of God.

Jerusalem chosen by God

1KI 11:32 See also 1KI 8:44–8:51 parallel 2CH 6:34–6:39; 1KI 11:36, 1KI 14:21 parallel 2CH 12:13; 2KI 23:27; NEH 1:8–1:9

The ark of the covenant brought to Jerusalem

1CH 15:1–15:3 See also 2SA 6:1–6:19 parallel 1CH 15:25–16:3

The temple located in Jerusalem

2CH 3:1 See also 1CH 6:31–6:32; 2CH 20:27–20:28, 2CH 33:7, 2CH 36:14, 2CH 36:22–36:23 parallel EZR 1:1–1:3; PSA 68:28–68:29; MRK 11:11, MRK 11:15–11:16; LUK 2:41–2:51, LUK 4:9; ACT 22:13–22:18

Jerusalem the only acceptable place for sacrificial worship

DEU 12:1–12:7 See also DEU 12:20–12:28; 1KI 12:26–13:3, 1KI 14:22–14:24, 1KI 22:42–22:43 parallel 2CH 20:31–20:33; 2KI 12:1–12:3, 2KI 16:1–16:4 parallel 2CH 28:1–28:4; 2KI 23:1–23:20 parallel 2CH 34:29–34:32; 2CH 30:1–31:1; ISA 66:20; HOS 10:8; JHN 4:20–4:21

Jerusalem referred to as God's dwelling-place

PSA 135:21 See also ISA 24:23, ISA 31:9; JER 3:17

Personification of Jerusalem

ISA 52:1–52:2 See also PSA 147:12; ISA 3:8, ISA 40:2, ISA 41:27, ISA 52:9, ISA 62:6–62:7; JER 2:1–2:2, JER 4:14, JER 6:8, JER 13:9, JER 13:26–13:27, JER 15:5; LAM 1:17, LAM 2:13–2:15; EZK 5:8, EZK 16:1–16:63, EZK 23:1–23:49; ZEP 3:14–3:17; ZEC 9:9–9:10; MAT 23:37–23:39 parallel LUK 13:34–13:35

Jerusalem the place of Jesus Christ's triumphal entry

LUK 19:28–19:38

Jerusalem the place of Jesus Christ's death and resurrection

LUK 23:26–23:49, LUK 24:1–24:12, LUK 24:13–24:33

The outpouring of the Holy Spirit in Jerusalem

ACT 1:4–1:9 See also ACT 2:1–2:21

ACT 8:1 See also ACT 8:14, ACT 8:25, ACT 11:1–11:22, ACT 11:27, ACT 12:25, ACT 13:13, ACT 15:1–15:29, ACT 16:4; 1CO 16:1–16:3; GAL 1:15–1:19, GAL 2:1–2:10

The new Jerusalem

REV 21:1–21:5 See also EZK 48:30–48:35; GAL 4:25–4:26; HEB 12:18–12:24; REV 3:11–3:13, REV 21:9–21:27

title: 7245: Judah, kingdom of
content:
The southernmost of the two kingdoms into which Israel split after the death of Solomon. It was ruled by a series of Davidic kings until the sixth century B.C., when it became a province of the Babylonian empire.

Origin of the kingdom of Judah

1KI 12:1–12:24 parallel 2CH 10:1–11:4

Loss of the wealth amassed in Judah

1KI 14:25–14:26 parallel 2CH 12:9 See also 1KI 15:18–15:19 parallel 2CH 16:2–16:3; 2KI 12:17–12:18, 2KI 14:14 parallel 2CH 25:24; 2KI 16:8 parallel 2CH 28:21; 2KI 18:14–18:16; 2CH 24:23

Apostasy in Judah

1KI 14:22–14:24 See also 2KI 8:16–8:19 parallel 2CH 21:5–21:7; 2KI 16:10–16:16, 2KI 21:2–21:16 parallel 2CH 33:2–33:10; 2CH 28:22–28:23

Religious revival in Judah

The reign of Athaliah in Judah

2KI 11:1–11:16 parallel 2CH 22:10–23:21

Coalitions between Judah and Israel

War in Judah

With Israel

1KI 15:6 See also 1KI 14:30 parallel 2CH 12:15; 1KI 15:16–15:17 parallel 2CH 16:1; 2KI 14:8–14:14 parallel 2CH 25:17–25:24; 2KI 16:5

With other nations

2KI 14:7 See also 2KI 8:20–8:22 parallel 2CH 21:8–21:10; 2KI 18:8, 2KI 18:13–19:36 parallels 2CH 32:9–32:19, ISA 36:1–36:22; 2CH 21:16–21:17, 2CH 25:11–25:12, 2CH 28:17–28:18

Judah's loss of independence and change of status from kingdom to province

2KI 25:1–25:12 parallels 2CH 36:17–36:20, JER 39:1–39:10 See also 2KI 25:22–25:24 parallel JER 40:7–40:9

title: 7248: patriarchs
content:
The patriarchs: Abraham, Isaac, Jacob and his twelve sons

HEB 7:4 See also JHN 7:22; ACT 7:8–7:9; ROM 9:5, ROM 11:28, ROM 15:8

David also called a patriarch

ACT 2:29

title: 7251: pillar of cloud and fire
content:
A physical manifestation of the presence of God among his people; a special form of theophany particularly associated with Israel being offered guidance on the exodus journey.

Functions of the pillar of cloud and fire

To lead Israel

EXO 13:21–13:22 See also EXO 40:36–40:37; NUM 9:17, NUM 14:14; NEH 9:12, NEH 9:19

To separate and protect Israel from the armies of Egypt

EXO 14:19–14:20 This was just before the crossing of the Red Sea.

To represent God's presence, especially to Moses

EXO 33:9 See also NUM 12:5–12:6; DEU 31:15–31:16; PSA 99:7

Effects of the pillar of cloud and fire

It evoked worship

EXO 33:10

It caused the nations to speak of God

NUM 14:14

Experiencing the cloud did not guarantee acceptance by God

1CO 10:1–10:5

title: 7254: plumb-line
content:
A cord weighted with lead that is used in building to check that vertical structures are true. It is used symbolically to refer to the divine standard against which God, the builder of his people, tests and judges them. It also symbolises the standards by which God will rebuild his people.

The plumb-line as the measuring tool of a structure

ZEC 1:16, ZEC 4:9–4:10 Using the plumb-line Zerubbabel will carry out his God-given task of building the temple. See also JER 31:38–31:40; ZEC 2:1–2:2

God's plumb-line reveals the nation's failure

Although built true, Israel has become corrupt

AMO 7:7–7:8 See also ISA 1:21–1:24, ISA 5:1–5:2, ISA 5:7, ISA 59:14

God's testing brings judgment

2KI 21:13 See also ISA 34:11; LAM 2:8

God will rebuild his people

God's promise to rebuild Israel

JER 31:4 See also PSA 69:35–69:36, PSA 147:2; ISA 54:11–54:12

They will be rebuilt true to God's plumb-line

ISA 28:16–28:17 See also PSA 51:18–51:19; ISA 1:25–1:26; PSA 89:14, PSA 97:2, PSA 99:4; ISA 16:5

title: 7257: promised land, the
content: The territory of the Canaanite nations given by God to Abraham and his descendants as part of his covenant with them.

title: 7258: promised land, early history of
content:
The promise of a homeland, made to Abraham, began to unfold through Moses and Joshua. Moses taught that the land was to be held in trust by God's people, obedience to God's ways bringing blessing in the land and disobedience bringing his judgment.

The location of the promised land

GEN 15:18–15:21 See also NUM 34:1–34:12; DEU 7:1

Traditional descriptions of the promised land

EXO 3:17 See also NUM 13:26–13:27; DEU 8:7–8:8; JER 32:22; EZK 20:6

The promised land and Abraham

The covenant with Abraham

GEN 15:7–15:21 See also GEN 13:14–13:17, GEN 17:7–17:8

The promised land given to Abraham's descendants

GEN 12:7 See also GEN 17:8, GEN 24:7; EXO 33:1

A burial plot was Abraham's only earthly possession in the promised land

GEN 25:9–25:10 See also GEN 23:3–23:20

The promised land and the patriarchs

GEN 26:2–26:5 See also GEN 28:12–28:15, GEN 35:11–35:12, GEN 50:24–50:25

Later generations look back to the promise made to the patriarchs

DEU 1:8 See also EXO 32:13–32:14; DEU 6:20–6:23; NEH 9:7–9:8; PSA 105:8–105:11 parallel 1CH 16:15–16:18

The promised land and Moses

Moses is called to lead Israel into the promised land

DEU 10:11 See also EXO 3:16–3:17, EXO 6:1–6:8; NUM 11:12

A whole generation is not permitted to enter the promised land

JOS 5:6 See also NUM 14:20–14:38, NUM 32:9–32:13; PSA 106:24–106:27

Moses and Aaron are not permitted to enter the promised land

NUM 20:12 See also NUM 20:23–20:29, NUM 27:12–27:14; DEU 32:48–32:52, DEU 34:1–34:4

Moses' instructions to destroy the inhabitants of the land and their practices

DEU 7:16 See also NUM 33:50–33:53; DEU 7:1–7:6, DEU 12:2–12:3, DEU 18:9–18:12

The instructions for distributing the promised land

NUM 33:54 See also NUM 34:13–34:15, NUM 36:5–36:9; JOS 14:1–14:5; PSA 78:55

Obedience to the law was a condition for enjoying the blessings of the promised land

DEU 6:1–6:3 See also EXO 20:12 parallel DEU 5:16; LEV 26:3–26:12; DEU 6:17–6:19, DEU 7:12–7:15, DEU 11:8–11:15, DEU 28:1–28:8; JER 11:1–11:5

Warnings not to forget God and follow Canaanite ways

DEU 11:16–11:17 See also EXO 34:12–34:16; LEV 18:1–18:5; NUM 33:55–33:56; DEU 8:10–8:14, DEU 28:15–28:19; JOS 23:14–23:16

Laws concerning the land

LEV 25:23–25:24 The promised land is seen as belonging to God. See also EXO 23:10–23:11; LEV 25:1–25:7, LEV 25:8–25:13; NUM 35:33–35:34

The promised land and Joshua

Joshua is called to lead the Israelites into the promised land

DEU 31:7–31:8 See also DEU 3:28; JOS 1:1–1:6

The Israelites enter the promised land and begin to possess it

JOS 3:14–3:17, JOS 11:16–11:23, JOS 21:43

God's people are given rest

JOS 21:44–21:45 See also DEU 12:10; JOS 1:13–1:15

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 7259: promised land, later history of
content:
Established firmly in the land under David and Solomon, God's people ultimately began to neglect the terms of the covenant and were warned by the prophets of the consequent judgment and exile.

Israel's failure to take full possession of the promised land under the judges

JDG 1:19 See also JDG 1:27–1:35, JDG 2:1–2:3, JDG 18:1

Israel was finally established in the promised land under David and Solomon

1CH 22:18 See also 2SA 7:10–7:11 parallel 1CH 17:9–17:10; 1KI 4:21, 1KI 4:25

The promised land and the prophets

The prophetic condemnation of the life of God's people in the promised land

JER 2:7 See also JER 3:1–3:3; EZK 22:23–22:29; HOS 4:1–4:3

Prophetic warning of coming judgment on the land and exile from it

ISA 13:9 See also ISA 8:6–8:8; JER 10:17–10:18, JER 16:10–16:13; EZK 7:2–7:4; AMO 7:17

Prophetic assurance of ultimate deliverance and restoration to the promised land

JER 30:3 See also ISA 14:1–14:2, ISA 60:15–60:22; JER 24:5–24:7; EZK 11:17, EZK 37:11–37:14; AMO 9:14–9:15; ZEC 2:10–2:12

The desolation of the promised land

By Antiochus Epiphanes

DAN 8:9–8:14 The growing horn is Antiochus Epiphanes who tried to wipe out the Jewish faith in 168 B.C., forbidding Jewish practices and defiling the temple with pagan sacrifices and prostitution. See also DAN 11:29–11:35 The help that would be received by God's people probably refers to the Maccabean revolt and the cleansing of the temple in 165 B.C., although by NT times the land would be occupied by Rome.

By Rome

LUK 21:20–21:24 parallels MAT 24:15–24:21, MRK 13:14–13:19 Jesus Christ prophesies the desolation of the nation by Rome in A.D. 70.

The promised land in the NT

Referred to by Stephen

See also ACT 7:3–7:7, ACT 7:45–7:47

Referred to by Paul

ACT 13:16–13:19

Referred to by Hebrews

HEB 11:8–11:10 Abraham was looking for a more permanent city than the promised land could ever provide; ; HEB 11:13–11:16

The concept of spiritual rest

HEB 3:18–4:11

The church looks beyond the promised land

ACT 1:6–1:8 The apostles' concern was for the deliverance of the promised land from Roman domination, but Jesus Christ shows God's concern to be far wider, to all nations. See also REV 5:9–5:10, REV 7:9–7:17, REV 21:9–21:27

title: 7263: theocracy
content:
The form of government among the early Israelites in which God was their supreme ruler, and his laws their laws.

The covenant at Sinai established the theocracy

EXO 19:5–19:6 See also EXO 19:8, EXO 24:3, EXO 24:7–24:8; DEU 5:27

Moses and Joshua renewed the theocratic covenant

DEU 26:16–26:19, DEU 29:9–29:15; ; JOS 24:24–24:25

Under the theocracy God was the Israelites' supreme ruler

DEU 33:5 “Jeshurun” means “the upright one”, i.e., Israel. See also NUM 23:21; DEU 33:26; JDG 8:23; PSA 145:1

In the theocracy Israel was governed by God's laws

EXO 20:1–20:17 parallel DEU 5:6–5:21; ; EXO 21:1; ; DEU 4:1–4:2, DEU 4:13–4:14, DEU 17:18–17:19, DEU 31:12–31:13; ; JOS 1:7–1:8

Israel rejected the theocracy

1SA 8:7 See also 1SA 8:19, 1SA 10:19, 1SA 12:12

title: 7266: tribes of Israel
content:
The divisions of Israel on lines of kinship, according to descent from the twelve sons of Jacob. These divisions were most pronounced in the period before Saul became king of Israel but were maintained throughout Israel's history.

Origin of the tribes of Israel

Twelve sons of Jacob

GEN 35:22–35:26 See also GEN 29:31–29:35, GEN 30:3–30:13, GEN 30:17–30:24, GEN 35:16–35:18

Increased in number while living in Egypt

GEN 46:5–46:27 See also EXO 1:1–1:7

Blessings on the tribes of Israel

GEN 49:1–49:28; ; DEU 33:1–33:29

Association of the twelve apostles with the tribes of Israel

MAT 19:28 See also MAT 10:1–10:4 parallels MRK 3:13–3:19, LUK 6:12–6:16; LUK 22:28–22:30; ACT 1:12–1:26; REV 21:12–21:14

Other NT references to the tribes of Israel

The genealogy of Judah

1CH 12:26–12:28 Their fighting men join David at Hebron; ; 1CH 23:2–23:24 David divides them into three groups and organises their responsibilities; ; 1CH 27:16–27:17 David sets an army officer over them.

The half-tribe of Manasseh was allocated territory in Gilead: NUM 32:33, NUM 32:39–32:42; ; DEU 3:13–3:15, DEU 29:7–29:8; ; JOS 12:4–12:6, JOS 13:29–13:31; ; 1CH 5:23; JOS 13:6–13:7, JOS 16:1–16:4, JOS 17:1–17:13; ; JDG 1:27; JOS 22:1–22:34 the threat of war against the half-tribe of Manasseh and the other Transjordan tribes

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 7269: Zion
content: The geographical and spiritual centre of life among God's people.

title: 7270: Zion, as a place
content:
Originally the name of the southernmost hill on which the Canaanite fortress-city of Jebus was located. It was conquered by David around 1000 B.C. and renamed Jerusalem. Situated on the borders of Judah and Israel, it became David's capital. As the city expanded, the name Zion came to be applied to the whole city.

The name of Zion

1CH 11:4–11:5 parallel 2SA 5:6–5:7

The early history of Zion

Early mention of Zion

GEN 14:18

Early attempts to take control of Zion

JOS 15:63 parallel JDG 1:21

Zion as the City of David

Considered an impregnable fortress in David's time

2SA 5:6 parallel 1CH 11:4–11:5 See also 2SA 5:8

Captured by David

2SA 5:7 parallel 1CH 11:4

Zion, established by David as his new capital and renamed

2SA 5:9 parallel 1CH 11:7

Further strengthened by David

1CH 11:8 parallel 2SA 5:9

The location of David's palace

2SA 5:11 parallel 1CH 14:1

The new resting place for the ark of the covenant

1CH 15:1–16:6 parallel 2SA 6:1–6:23

Zion as the name of the extended city of Jerusalem

ISA 33:20 See also 2KI 19:20–19:21 parallel ISA 37:21–37:22; ISA 4:3; LAM 1:4–1:8; JOL 2:32; ZEP 3:14–3:16

title: 7271: Zion, as a symbol
content:
Central to the life of the nation of Israel, it became the symbol of the whole nation and was seen, especially by the prophets, as the focus of God's promises and his final victory. In the NT Zion is a symbol of the new Israel, the church.

Zion as the dwelling-place of God

PSA 135:21 See also PSA 76:2, PSA 132:13–132:14; JOL 3:17

Zion as a symbol of God's people

A symbol of Jerusalem

PSA 9:14 See also ISA 52:1–52:2; LAM 2:8–2:10; MIC 4:6–4:8; MAT 21:5 parallel JHN 12:15; ZEC 9:9

A symbol of Judah

PSA 78:68 See also JER 14:19

A symbol of Israel

LAM 2:1 See also ISA 46:13

Zion as the object of God's attention

The object of God's love

PSA 87:2 See also PSA 102:13–102:16

The object of God's blessing

PSA 133:3 See also PSA 132:13–132:16, PSA 147:12–147:14

The object of God's judgment

JER 26:18 See also MIC 3:12; ISA 1:8–1:9; JER 6:2–6:5, JER 6:22–6:23; JOL 2:1–2:2

Zion as the focus of God's presence and promises

The security of Zion because of God's presence

PSA 46:4–46:7 See also PSA 48:1–48:14, PSA 125:1–125:2; ISA 37:22–37:23 parallel 2KI 19:21–19:22; JOL 3:16

The restoration of Zion because of God's promises

JER 30:17–30:22 See also PSA 126:1–126:3; JER 29:10–29:14, JER 31:38–31:40; MIC 4:6–4:7

The salvation of Zion because of God's coming king and Saviour

ZEC 9:9–9:10 parallels MAT 21:5, JHN 12:15 See also ISA 62:11–62:12; MIC 4:8

Zion destined to be a city for all peoples

PSA 102:21–102:22 See also PSA 87:4–87:6; ISA 2:2–2:4 parallel MIC 4:1–4:3; ISA 56:3–56:7; ZEC 14:16

Zion as an NT symbol of the new Israel, the church

HEB 12:22–12:23 See also ROM 9:33; 1PE 2:6; ISA 8:14, ISA 28:16; HEB 11:16; REV 14:1

title: 7302: altar
content:
A construction, usually of wood, stone or metal, for the offering of sacrifice. The conflict between true and false religion often focused on altars, which were of key significance in biblical times.

Early altars built to the LORD

GEN 8:20 These early altars were erected at sites where God's presence or power had been experienced. GEN 12:7–12:8, GEN 13:18, GEN 22:9; GEN 26:25 by Isaac; GEN 33:20, GEN 35:7; EXO 17:15, EXO 24:4; JOS 8:30 by Joshua; ; JDG 6:24 by Gideon; ; JDG 21:4 by unnamed Israelites; ; 1SA 7:17 by Samuel; ; 1SA 14:35 by Saul; ; 2SA 24:25 parallel 1CH 21:26 by David

Solomon's temple altar

It was preceded by the tabernacle altar of burnt offering: EXO 27:1–27:8, EXO 30:1–30:10, EXO 37:25–37:28, EXO 38:1–38:7; ; HEB 9:3–9:4; 1KI 8:22, 1KI 6:20

OT pagan altars

A symptom of decline in the northern kingdom

1KI 16:32; ; 2KI 23:15; ; HOS 8:11, HOS 10:1

A symptom of decline in the southern kingdom

2KI 16:10 ignoring the command in Dt 12:2–3; ; 2KI 21:3; ; ISA 17:8; ; JER 11:13, JER 19:13; ; ZEP 1:5

Josiah's reform targeted illicit altars

2KI 23:12 See also DEU 7:5, DEU 12:8–12:14 Josiah was implementing the law requiring sacrifice to be offered only on the altar at the sanctuary of God's choice; 2KI 23:15–23:20

The altar in the second temple

EZR 3:2–3:3 See also EZR 7:17

Laws concerning the altar of burnt offering

Its construction

EXO 20:24–20:26 An earlier more basic form than in the tabernacle. No human hand was to shape the stones. See also EXO 27:1–27:8, EXO 38:1–38:7

Its use for the daily sacrifice

EXO 29:38–29:39 See also EXO 30:28; NUM 4:13, NUM 7:10, NUM 7:84

Its use for asylum

1KI 1:50–1:51 See also EXO 21:14; 1KI 2:28; AMO 3:14

Laws concerning the incense altar

Its construction

EXO 30:1–30:5 See also EXO 37:25–37:28

Its use

EXO 30:7 See also EXO 30:8–30:10; NUM 4:11

Altars in the NT

The Jerusalem temple altar

MAT 5:23–5:24 See also MAT 23:18–23:20; 1CO 9:13, 1CO 10:18; HEB 7:13, HEB 9:4

Pagan altars

ACT 17:23

The cross as an ``altar''

HEB 13:10

Altars in heaven

REV 6:9 The lives of martyred saints are compared to the blood of offerings poured out at the base of the altar; REV 8:3 The prayers of God's people are like incense, offered to him on the heavenly altar. See also REV 9:13, REV 11:1, REV 14:18, REV 16:7

title: 7304: anointing
content:
The application of oil was associated with times of rejoicing and celebration. It also possessed a deeper significance, including that of being singled out by God for special favour or responsibilities.

Anointing as a social custom

In personal grooming

ECC 9:8 The associations here are of joy and well-being. See also RUT 3:3; PSA 92:10; ISA 57:9; AMO 6:6; MAT 6:17

Anointing guests as a mark of honour

PSA 23:5 See also LUK 7:36–7:39, LUK 7:44–7:47; JHN 12:3

Anointing corpses as a burial preparation

MRK 16:1 See also MAT 26:6–26:12; JHN 19:38–19:40

Anointing religious objects

EXO 40:9–40:11 Anointed objects are set apart (consecrated), to be used only in the performance of religious ceremonies. See also GEN 28:18; EXO 30:22–30:33

Anointing people for office

Priests

EXO 40:12–40:15 See also LEV 4:16, LEV 21:10–21:12; 1CH 29:22

Kings

1KI 1:39 See also 1SA 10:1, 1SA 16:12–16:13; 2SA 5:3; 2KI 11:12

A prophet

1KI 19:15–19:16 the only reference to the anointing of a prophet

Anointing people for other purposes

For purification

LEV 14:15–14:18

For healing

MRK 6:13 It has been suggested that anointing in a healing context may be related to the use of oil for medicinal purposes. See also JAS 5:14

The figurative use of anointing

Anointing by God

1SA 26:9 Israel's king is frequently referred to as “the LORD's anointed”. His physical anointing is seen as symbolising a divine anointing. The word “Messiah” literally means “the anointed one”; ISA 45:1 Cyrus, as God's agent for a specific task, is referred to as “his anointed”. See also 2SA 23:1; PSA 2:2, PSA 45:2, PSA 89:20; EZK 28:14

God's people in the OT

1CH 16:22 parallel PSA 105:15

Christian believers

2CO 1:21–1:22 See also 1JN 2:20, 1JN 2:27 This anointing is not received in an outward ceremony but by sharing in the Holy Spirit's anointing of Jesus Christ.

Jesus Christ as God's Anointed One (the Messiah)

ACT 4:26–4:27 Jesus Christ's receiving of the Holy Spirit at his baptism was his “anointing” for his Messianic work; ACT 10:38 Note the link between anointing and the Holy Spirit. See also DAN 9:25–9:26; LUK 4:18; ISA 61:1; HEB 1:9

title: 7306: ark of the covenant
content:
A rectangular wooden box, overlaid with gold, measuring 3.75 x 2.25 x 2.25 feet (1.1 x 0.7 x 0.7 metres). It contains the Law tablets and symbolises God's presence with his people.

Descriptions of the ark

EXO 25:22; ; LEV 16:2; ; NUM 10:33; ; JOS 3:6, JOS 4:5, JOS 4:9; ; 1SA 4:11

The construction of the ark

EXO 25:10–25:16; ; DEU 10:1; ; EXO 31:1–31:7, EXO 35:10–35:12, EXO 37:1–37:5; ; DEU 10:3; ; EXO 39:33, EXO 39:35, EXO 40:1–40:5, EXO 40:20–40:21

The construction of the atonement cover

EXO 25:17–25:21, EXO 26:34, EXO 30:6, EXO 37:6–37:9, EXO 39:35, EXO 40:20

The care of the ark

NUM 3:30–3:32, NUM 4:5; ; DEU 10:8, DEU 31:9

Major events in the history of the ark

The ark is taken to the promised land

JOS 3:3, JOS 3:6, JOS 3:15–3:16, JOS 6:4–6:16, JOS 8:33

The ark is the focus of the covenant renewal at Mount Ebal

JDG 20:26–20:28

The ark is captured by the Philistines

1SA 4:1–4:11, 1SA 4:17–4:22, 1SA 5:1–5:12

The ark is returned to Israel

1SA 6:1–6:3, 1SA 6:10–6:15, 1SA 6:19, 1SA 7:1–7:2

The ark is brought to Jerusalem

2SA 6:1–6:12 parallel 1CH 13:3–13:14; ; 2SA 6:17 parallel 1CH 16:1; ; 1CH 15:1, 1CH 16:37; ; 2SA 7:2–7:7 parallel 1CH 17:1–17:6; ; 2SA 15:24–15:25, 2SA 15:29 David is fleeing from Absalom.

The ark is placed in the temple

1KI 8:1–8:6 parallel 2CH 5:2–5:7

The ark in later Israelite history

2CH 35:3 The ark must have been moved from the temple.

The function of the ark

To contain the tablets of the Law

DEU 10:5 See also EXO 25:16, EXO 25:21, EXO 40:20; DEU 31:24–31:26; 1KI 8:9; HEB 9:4 The jar of manna and Aaron's rod were originally placed beside the ark (Ex 16:34; Nu 17:10), and were probably put inside it at a later date.

As the place where God reveals his commands to Moses

NUM 7:89 See also EXO 25:22, EXO 30:6, EXO 30:36

As a symbol of the presence of God

LEV 16:2 See also NUM 10:33–10:36; JOS 7:6; JDG 20:27; 2SA 7:2 parallel 1CH 17:1; 2CH 6:41 parallel PSA 132:8

Used on the Day of Atonement

LEV 16:13–16:15

The ark's holiness and effect

Irreverent treament of the ark brings judgment

2SA 6:6–6:7 parallel 1CH 13:9–13:10 See also JOS 3:4; 1SA 5:1–5:4, 1SA 5:8–5:12, 1SA 6:19

The presence of the ark brings blessing

2SA 6:11 parallel 1CH 13:14

A restored Israel in which the ark has no place

JER 3:16

The ark in John's revelation

REV 11:19 The ark here symbolises the presence of God and is open and visible, not hidden in the Most Holy Place.

title: 7308: Atonement, Day of
content:
The most holy day of Israel's year (the tenth day of the seventh month) on which the high priest entered the Most Holy Place to offer sacrifices for the sins of the nation. Hebrews sees this day as symbolic of the achievements of Jesus Christ on the cross.

The priestly rituals on the Day of Atonement

The priest's own preparations

See also LEV 16:4, LEV 16:6, LEV 16:11–16:14

The preparation of two male goats

LEV 16:7–16:10; EXO 30:10; ; LEV 16:15–16:19; LEV 16:10, LEV 16:20–16:22

Concluding procedures

LEV 16:23–16:28

Additional offerings on the Day of Atonement

NUM 29:8–29:11

Features unique to the Day of Atonement

The priest entered the Most Holy Place

HEB 9:7 See also LEV 16:2

Sacrifice was made for all the sins of all the people

LEV 16:30 See also EXO 30:10; LEV 16:16–16:17, LEV 16:21–16:22, LEV 16:34

It was a festival requiring humility and fasting

NUM 29:7 This provides a contrast to all other religious festivals which were times of joyous celebration and feasting. “deny yourselves” includes the idea of fasting. See also LEV 16:29, LEV 16:31, LEV 23:27–23:32; ACT 27:9

The Day of Atonement is paralleled and contrasted with the achievements of Jesus Christ in his death

Jesus Christ entered the Most Holy Place

HEB 9:24 See also HEB 6:19–6:20, HEB 9:11–9:12

Jesus Christ's blood was offered in sacrifice

HEB 9:12 See also ROM 3:25

Jesus Christ's sacrifice was outside the city gates

HEB 13:11–13:12

Jesus Christ's sacrifice was once for all

HEB 9:25–9:26 See also HEB 10:12

Jesus Christ's sacrifice gives inner rather than ritual cleansing

HEB 9:13–9:14 See also HEB 9:9–9:10

Jesus Christ's sacrifice gives access to God

HEB 10:19–10:20 On the Day of Atonement no-one else was allowed to be in the Tent of Meeting, let alone in the Most Holy Place. Access into the intimate presence of God is now the right of all believers. See also LEV 16:17; MAT 27:51; EPH 2:18, EPH 3:12

title: 7310: avenger of blood
content:
The one responsible in OT law for carrying out the death penalty in the case of murder, usually, the murdered person's next of kin.

The responsibility of the avenger of blood

NUM 35:16–35:21 See also NUM 35:26–35:27; DEU 19:11–19:12

Protection from the avenger of blood

NUM 35:10–35:15 See also NUM 35:22–35:25; DEU 19:4–19:6; JOS 20:1–20:6

Examples of avenging of blood

GEN 4:24; ; 2SA 3:27, 2SA 14:11

God as an avenger of blood

DEU 32:43 See also JDG 9:23–9:24; 2KI 9:7; PSA 9:12, PSA 79:10; REV 6:10, REV 19:2

title: 7312: Baal
content:
Literally “master”, used to refer to a variety of local gods in the Near East, encountered by Israel in Canaan. In the later period, the word comes to refer especially to the local god of the city of Tyre (Baal Melgart), on account of the increasing importance of that city in the region.

The history of Baal worship

In the exodus period

NUM 25:3–25:5 A Moabite deity whose worship involved sexual immorality. See also NUM 22:41; DEU 4:3; PSA 106:28; HOS 9:10

In the time of the judges

JDG 2:12–2:13 Ashtoreth was the consort of Baal. See also JDG 3:7, JDG 6:31–6:32, JDG 8:33, JDG 10:6; 1SA 7:4, 1SA 12:10

In the time of Elijah

1KI 17:1 The proclamation of a drought and the contest on Carmel challenged the supposed power of Baal. See also 1KI 16:30–16:33, 1KI 18:18–19:1, 1KI 22:53; 2KI 10:18–10:29, 2KI 11:17–11:18

During the later monarchy in the north

HOS 2:2–2:13 Lovers are gods such as Baal. See also HOS 11:2, HOS 13:1

During the later monarchy in the south

2CH 28:1–28:2 The Judean kingdom copied the idolatry in the north. See also JER 2:8, JER 7:9; HOS 3:3

title: 7314: blood
content: The symbol of life, which thus plays an especially important role in the sacrificial system of the OT. The shedding of the blood of a sacrificial animal represents the giving up of its life. The “blood of Christ” refers to Jesus Christ's obedient giving of his life, in order to achieve redemption and forgiveness.

title: 7315: blood, as basis of life
content:
Scripture treats blood as the basis of life, and regards the shedding of blood as representing the end of life.

Blood symbolises life

LEV 17:10–17:14 See also GEN 9:4–9:6; DEU 12:20–12:25; 2SA 23:15–23:17; PSA 72:14; JER 2:34; EZK 33:1–33:6; MAT 27:3–27:4; LUK 11:50–11:51 parallel MAT 23:35–23:36

Blood indicating violent death

GEN 4:8–4:11 See also GEN 9:6; JDG 9:22–9:24; 1KI 2:5–2:6, 1KI 2:28–2:34; 2KI 9:30–9:33

Shedding blood as a sin

EXO 20:13 parallel DEU 5:17 See also GEN 42:21–42:22; DEU 21:1–21:9; 2KI 21:16; PRO 1:10–1:19; ISA 26:21; MAT 15:18–15:19 parallel MRK 7:20–7:21; ROM 1:28–1:29

The eating of blood forbidden

LEV 3:17 See also LEV 7:26–7:27, LEV 17:10–17:14; DEU 12:23; 1SA 14:31–14:34; ACT 15:19–15:20, ACT 15:29

title: 7316: blood, and OT sacrifices
content:
The pouring out of animals' blood in sacrifice was God's provision, under the old covenant, for the atonement of sin.

God's provision of blood sacrifice to establish the old covenant

EXO 24:4–24:8 See also HEB 9:18–9:20

God's provision of blood sacrifices within the old covenant

Blood sacrifices and the priesthood

EXO 29:10–29:21 parallel LEV 8:14–8:24; ; LEV 9:8–9:14, LEV 16:1–16:14

Blood sacrifices and various offerings

LEV 1:1–1:17 the burnt offering, to gain favour or express devotion; ; LEV 3:1–3:17 the fellowship, or peace, offering, to express gratitude; ; LEV 4:1–4:35 the sin offering, to gain forgiveness; ; LEV 5:14–5:19 the guilt offering, where restitution was required; ; LEV 16:15–16:22 the Day of Atonement, for the cleansing of the nation from unintentional sin

Blood sacrifices and the Passover

EXO 12:1–12:14 See also 2CH 30:15–30:20, 2CH 35:1–35:19; EZR 6:19–6:21; MRK 14:12 parallel LUK 22:7–22:8

Blood sacrifices must come from animals without defect

LEV 22:17–22:25 See also EXO 12:5; LEV 1:3, LEV 3:1, LEV 4:3, LEV 5:15; NUM 28:9, NUM 28:11; DEU 15:21; EZK 43:22–43:27

Blood sacrifices must not come from human beings

2KI 23:10 See also 2KI 17:17, 2KI 21:1–21:6; EZK 20:25–20:26; MIC 6:7

The limited effect of blood sacrifices

They can become mere external ritual

ISA 1:11–1:13 See also ISA 66:2–66:4

They are worthless without obedience to God

HOS 6:6 See also 1SA 15:22–15:23; PSA 40:6–40:8; AMO 4:4–4:5, AMO 5:21–5:27

They are unable to cleanse the conscience

HEB 9:9–9:10 See also HEB 10:1–10:4

title: 7317: blood, of Jesus Christ
content:
The shedding of the blood of Jesus Christ is seen as representing the giving of his life as an atoning sacrifice for the sins of humanity.

The blood of Jesus Christ as part of his humanity

JHN 19:33–19:34 See also LUK 22:44; ACT 5:28; 1JN 5:6

The blood of Jesus Christ as a sacrifice

HEB 9:12–9:14, HEB 9:23–9:26, HEB 10:3–10:14, HEB 13:11–13:12

The blood of Jesus Christ as a symbol of atonement

ROM 3:25 See also EPH 1:7; REV 7:14

The effects of the blood of Jesus Christ

The institution of the new covenant

1CO 11:25 parallels MAT 26:27–26:28, MRK 14:23–14:24, LUK 22:20 See also HEB 9:11–9:15, HEB 12:24, HEB 13:20

Redemption

ACT 20:28; ; 1PE 1:1–1:2, 1PE 1:18–1:19; ; REV 5:9–5:10

Forgiveness and justification

ROM 5:9 See also MAT 26:28 parallels MRK 14:24, LUK 22:20; ROM 3:25–3:26

Victory over evil and Satan

REV 7:14–7:17, REV 12:10–12:11

Liberation from sin

REV 1:5–1:6

The promise of total restoration

COL 1:19–1:20

The blood of Jesus Christ and believers

Believers are cleansed from all sin

HEB 9:14, HEB 10:22, HEB 13:12; ; 1JN 1:6–1:9

Believers have a new confidence before God

EPH 2:13; ; COL 1:19–1:22; ; HEB 10:19–10:22

The blood of Jesus Christ and the Lord's Supper

Invitations to share in the blood of Jesus Christ

1CO 10:16 See also JHN 6:53–6:57

Warnings about sinning against the blood of Jesus Christ

1CO 11:27 See also HEB 10:28–10:31

title: 7318: blood, as symbol of guilt
content:
Blood is often used as an image of people's sin and guilt, and the judgment which follows. Blood-guilt is ascribed to those who are responsible for the shedding of innocent blood.

Blood as an image of sin and guilt

ISA 59:2–59:3 See also LEV 20:9–20:13, LEV 20:27; 2SA 1:14–1:16; ISA 1:15–1:18; NAM 3:1; ACT 18:6

Blood as an image of judgment

ISA 34:5–34:6 See also EXO 7:14–7:21; PSA 78:44, PSA 105:29; ACT 2:19–2:20; JOL 2:30–2:31

Blood as a sign of the end times

ACT 2:19–2:20 See also JOL 2:30–2:31; REV 6:12–6:15, REV 8:7–8:9, REV 16:3–16:6

Blood-guilt, the result of shedding innocent blood

Examples of blood-guilt

GEN 4:8–4:11 See also 2SA 1:14–1:16, 2SA 4:5–4:12; EZK 35:5–35:9; HAB 2:12; MAT 27:3–27:8, MAT 27:24; ACT 5:28

The right to avenge blood-guilt under the law

DEU 19:11–19:13 See also NUM 35:16–35:28

Restraint to avoid further blood-guilt

DEU 4:41–4:42 See also GEN 4:15; NUM 35:6–35:34; DEU 19:4–19:10; JOS 20:1–20:9

Blood-guilt cannot be forgotten

GEN 9:5–9:6 See also GEN 4:10–4:16, GEN 42:22; 1KI 2:28–2:33; ISA 26:21; MAT 23:30–23:31

title: 7320: breastpiece
content:
A pouch worn on the chest of the high priest.

The breastpiece was worn by the high priest

LEV 8:5–8:9 See also EXO 28:15–28:16, EXO 28:29, EXO 39:8–39:9

It was adorned with twelve precious stones

EXO 28:17–28:21 See also EXO 25:3–25:7, EXO 39:10–39:14

It contained the Urim and Thummim

EXO 28:30 The Urim and Thummim were used in decision-making. See also LEV 8:8

It was attached to the ephod

EXO 28:22–28:28 See also EXO 39:15–39:21

title: 7322: burnt offering
content:
Probably the earliest and most basic form of sacrifice. It is seen fundamentally as a gift to God, either in thanksgiving for his goodness or for atonement for sin.

Hebrew names for the burnt offering

DEU 33:10 The rarer Hebrew expression means something whole or complete; PSA 66:13 The commoner expression in Hebrew means that which “goes up” to God. See also 1SA 7:9; PSA 51:19

Early examples of burnt offerings

GEN 8:20 GEN 22:2–22:8, GEN 22:13; ; EXO 10:25, EXO 18:12, EXO 20:24, EXO 24:5; ; JOB 1:5

Regulations concerning the burnt offering

How the offering is to be made

LEV 1:1–1:17, LEV 6:8–6:13, LEV 7:8 the entitlement of the priest; ; MAL 1:8

Occasions for making the offering

NUM 28:9–28:10 It was an additional offering on the Sabbath.
