title: 7703: apologetics
content:
The art of explaining the faith in such a way as to make a reasoned defence against its detractors. Paul's Areopagus sermon is a classic example of biblical apologetics, which can be of value to all who are called upon to defend the faith today.

Paul's Areopagus sermon, a classic example of biblical apologetics

Paul speaks to his listeners on their own ground, starting on their own terms

ACT 17:22–17:23

Paul presents the Christian position

ACT 17:24–17:27

Paul supports his argument in a culturally appropriate way

ACT 17:28 Paul quotes from two secular writers, first from the Cretan poet Epimenides, and secondly from the Cilician poet Aratus.

Paul concludes his argument and calls for a response

ACT 17:29–17:31

Apologetics as a regular feature of Paul's ministry

ACT 18:4 See also ACT 9:26–9:30, ACT 18:19, ACT 19:8–19:10, ACT 28:17–28:31

Apologetics is part of the work of church leaders

TIT 1:9

All Christians share responsibility for the task of apologetics

1PE 3:15

Other examples of Christians engaging in apologetics

ACT 18:28 See also ACT 2:14–2:41, ACT 6:8–6:10

Apologetics alone is an inadequate way of presenting the gospel

1CO 1:17–1:25 See also 1CO 2:1–2:5

title: 7706: apostles
content: Appointed representatives, deriving authority and responsibilities from the person or group represented. The word refers especially to Jesus Christ's twelve disciples and also to Paul. Scripture identifies the functions of apostles and their authority, which is derived directly from Jesus Christ.

title: 7707: apostles, designation of
content:
An apostle is one who is “sent” by Jesus Christ. The word is used to refer especially to the inner circle of Christ's twelve disciples, but is also used specifically to refer to Paul, who was commissioned as an apostle by the risen Christ. The term is also applied more loosely to certain other leading Christians in the NT period.

Jesus Christ as an apostle

HEB 3:1 This is the only place where the word “apostle” is used of Jesus Christ, though many passages refer to him as “sent”; JHN 20:21 Jesus Christ, as God's “sent one”, sets the standard and pattern for all who are likewise “sent” (i.e., for all apostolic ministry). See also MAT 10:40; MRK 9:37; JHN 5:23–5:24, JHN 12:44–12:45

The twelve apostles called by Jesus Christ

Their appointment

MRK 3:13–3:19 parallels MAT 10:1–10:4, LUK 6:12–6:16

Their number

JHN 6:70 “the Twelve” is frequently used to designate these specially chosen disciples. The number twelve evokes the twelve tribes of Israel. See also MAT 19:28, MAT 20:17; MRK 11:11; LUK 8:1; ACT 6:2; 1CO 15:5; REV 21:14

Their commissioning by the risen Christ

MAT 28:18–28:20

The replacement for Judas

See also ACT 1:15–1:26 The symbolic number (twelve) is thus preserved.

The qualifications for being an apostle

ACT 1:21–1:22

Paul as an apostle

His self-designation

GAL 1:1 See also ROM 1:1; 1CO 1:1; 1TI 2:7

His commissioning by Jesus Christ

ACT 26:12–26:18 Although not one of the Twelve, Paul saw the risen Christ and was directly commissioned by him, sent especially to the Gentiles; ; 1CO 15:3–15:10; ; GAL 2:8

Paul's apostleship and the church

1CO 9:1–9:2 In Paul's case the sense of title or office and the ministry and function virtually coincide. He is an apostle both because he has seen and been commissioned by the risen Christ and because he functioned as an apostle by founding churches.

Other apostles

Barnabas

Here apostles'' may refer to their ministry as missionary evangelists or to their role assent'' by the church at Antioch: ACT 14:4, ACT 14:14

James, the Lord's brother

1CO 15:7 The most natural meaning of the words is that James is an apostle. This interpretation is confirmed by other references to James; ; GAL 1:19, GAL 2:9

Andronicus and Junias

ROM 16:7

Silas

1TH 2:6

``Apostles'' of the churches

2CO 8:23 “representatives” is the translation here for “apostles”; ; PHP 2:25 “messenger” is the translation here for “apostle”

False apostles

2CO 11:5 Paul ironically refers to his opponents as “super-apostles”; ; 2CO 11:13, 2CO 12:11; ; REV 2:2

title: 7708: apostles, function of
content:
In the NT apostles are servants, first of Jesus Christ and then of the church. Apostolic service embraces a wide range of specific functions.

The functions of apostles during the earthly life and ministry of Jesus Christ

Being with Jesus Christ

MRK 3:14 See also MAT 17:1; MRK 6:30–6:32 parallel LUK 9:10

Learning from Jesus Christ

MRK 8:31 See also MAT 18:1–18:4; MRK 4:10–4:12; ACT 1:2

Being sent out on a mission by Jesus Christ

LUK 9:1–9:6 parallels MAT 10:5–10:10, MRK 6:7–6:13 As Jesus Christ's representatives their ministry was an extension of his. See also MRK 3:14–3:15

Being commissioned to witness to Jesus Christ

ACT 1:8, ACT 10:39 See also MAT 28:18–28:20; MRK 16:15; LUK 24:46–24:49; JHN 17:18, JHN 20:21

Apostles in the early church

They were witnesses of the resurrection

ACT 1:21–1:22 See also ACT 2:32, ACT 3:15, ACT 4:33, ACT 13:31; 1PE 5:1

They were servants of Jesus Christ

2PE 1:1 See also ROM 1:1; 1CO 4:1; 2CO 11:23; PHP 1:1; TIT 1:1

They were servants of the church

2CO 13:4 See also ROM 15:25; 2CO 4:5, 2CO 11:8; 1PE 5:1–5:3

They were the foundations of the church

EPH 2:20 Probably a reference to the apostolic preaching. The absence of the article before “prophets” suggests that “the apostles and prophets” represents one group of people, not two. See also 1CO 12:28; GAL 2:9; JUD 1:17 the prophetic ministry of the apostles

They preached the gospel

ROM 1:1–1:5 See also MRK 16:20; ROM 15:19–15:20; 1CO 1:17; GAL 1:15–1:16

They were guardians and teachers of the truth

ACT 2:42 See also JHN 16:13–16:15; ACT 5:42, ACT 6:2; EPH 3:2–3:6; 2TH 2:15; 2TI 1:11

They founded churches

1CO 9:1–9:2 See also 1CO 3:5–3:6, 1CO 3:10

They were authority figures within the church

ACT 6:2–6:6 The apostles took the initiative. Whilst they did not themselves appoint the Seven, they confirmed their appointment.

They were called to suffer

2TI 1:11–1:12 See also ACT 5:18; 1CO 4:9; 2CO 11:23–11:29

They were empowered by the Spirit

ACT 4:8 See also JHN 20:22; ACT 1:8, ACT 2:4; EPH 3:4–3:5

They were able to bestow the Spirit

ACT 8:14–8:18 Peter and John officially induct Samaritans as members of the body of Christ; ; ACT 19:6

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 7709: apostles, authority of
content:
Apostolic authority derives directly from Jesus Christ, who commissions apostles. It is rigorously upheld within the church.

Apostolic authority is based on divine commission

ROM 1:5 See also ROM 1:1; 2CO 1:1; GAL 1:1; EPH 1:1; COL 1:1; 1TI 1:1; 2TI 1:1

Apostolic authority derives from Jesus Christ

MAT 10:40 See also MAT 28:18–28:20; MRK 6:7; 2PE 3:2

The authority of apostles is related to their ministry role

1CO 12:28 There appears to be a definite order of priority with apostles at the head. See also EPH 4:11

The exercise of apostolic authority within the church

In building up the body

2CO 13:10 Paul had special authority as founder of this church. See also 2CO 10:8

In general oversight

ACT 4:34–4:35, ACT 6:2–6:6, ACT 15:6, ACT 15:22–15:23, ACT 16:4

In teaching

1TH 4:2 See also GAL 1:11–1:12; 1TH 2:4, 1TH 4:8

In appointing leaders

ACT 14:23

In church discipline

ACT 5:3–5:4, ACT 5:7–5:9; ; 1CO 5:3–5:5

The right to be supported

1TH 2:6 Paul did not demand such support (see 1Th 2:9). See also 1CO 9:4, 1CO 9:11–9:14; 2CO 11:7–11:9

The marks of a true apostle

False apostles exist

1CO 9:2 See also 2CO 11:4–11:5, 2CO 12:11

Fruit confirms apostolic ministry

2CO 3:1–3:3

Signs confirm apostolic authority

2CO 12:12 See also MRK 16:20; ACT 5:12; ROM 15:19

title: 7712: convincing
content:
Persuading one's hearers of the truth of one's case. Scripture gives examples of people who have been convinced of the truth of God's word and of the gospel.

Examples of prophets convincing their hearers

Elijah at Mount Carmel

1KI 18:20–18:39

Jonah at Nineveh

JON 3:1–3:9

Hardness of heart can prevent individuals from being convinced of the truth

The ministry of the prophets

JER 6:10 See also 1KI 22:15–22:28 parallel 2CH 18:14–18:27; JER 35:15; EZK 2:4–2:5; AMO 7:10–7:13

The ministry of Jesus Christ

JHN 12:37–12:40 See also ISA 53:1, ISA 6:10; MAT 13:13–13:15 parallels MRK 4:10–4:12, LUK 8:10; ISA 6:9–6:10; JHN 7:25–7:43

Examples of individuals convinced of the truth of the gospel

The Ethiopian eunuch

ACT 8:26–8:38

Paul

ROM 8:38–8:39 See also 2CO 5:14–5:15; 2TI 1:12

Paul sought to convince others of the truth of the gospel

ACT 18:4 See also ACT 17:2–17:4, ACT 19:8, ACT 26:28–26:29, ACT 28:23–28:24; 2CO 5:11

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 7715: deacons
content:
Those who “serve” in the Christian community, assisting the elders or overseers (bishops). The Greek word which the NIV translates four times as “deacon” occurs a further 25 times in the NT, where it is translated as “servant”. It seems that all Christians have a general servant role, whereas some (deacons) are called to a specific office of service within the church.

The Seven: assistants to the apostles

Why they were needed

ACT 6:1–6:4 The verb “wait” means “to serve”.

Their qualifications and selection

ACT 6:3

Their names and appointment

ACT 6:5–6:6 Although the Seven are not actually called “deacons”, they were probably the earliest example of Christians carrying out the tasks which came to be linked with deacons.

Deacons were assistants to overseers in Philippi

PHP 1:1 All Christians are “saints” (that is, set apart for God), but overseers and deacons have specific roles assigned to them.

Qualifications for men and women deacons

1TI 3:8–3:13 There is no NT description of the precise role of deacons (although Ac 6:1–4 may imply a certain role). These qualifications would be appropriate for people responsible for financial management, administration, and social service, such as that offered to widows; 1TI 3:11 The women may be deacons' wives (NIV text), or deaconesses (NIV footnote).

Phoebe, a deaconess

ROM 16:1 NIV footnote.

title: 7718: elders
content: Senior figures within Israel and the Christian church who are given special responsibilities for their people.

title: 7719: elders, as community leaders
content:
Since older men act as responsible leaders in many communities, “elder” is used as the title for a recognised leader.

Elders in early Israel

Elders represented their people

EXO 19:7 See also EXO 3:16, EXO 4:29, EXO 12:21–12:28, EXO 24:1

Elders assisted Moses

NUM 11:16–11:17 See also EXO 18:21–18:26; NUM 11:24–11:25

Elders met in a national assembly

JOS 23:2, JOS 24:1; ; JDG 21:16

Elders functioned as judges, sometimes in the town gate

DEU 19:11–19:12; ; JDG 8:14; ; RUT 4:1–4:12

Elders during the period of the monarchy

Tribal and town elders

1SA 30:26–30:31; ; 2KI 10:5, 2KI 23:1; ; JER 19:1

Elders were local representatives at national functions

1KI 8:1

Elders advised the king

1KI 12:6–12:8

Elders functioned as judges at the town gate

PRO 31:23 See also 1KI 21:8–21:14

Elders in Israel during and after the exile

EZR 10:8, EZR 10:14; ; EZK 8:1, EZK 14:1, EZK 20:1–20:3

Elders in NT Jewish society

Elders were local town and synagogue leaders

MRK 5:22; ; LUK 7:1–7:5

Elders were members of the Sanhedrin

MRK 15:1 See also MAT 5:22

Elders were guardians of religious traditions

MAT 15:1–15:2 parallel MRK 7:5 See also MRK 7:3

The elders' rejection of Jesus Christ predicted by him

MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31, LUK 9:22

The involvement of the elders in the trial and condemnation of Jesus Christ

MAT 27:1 parallel MRK 15:1

The elders' involvement with the first Christians

See also ACT 4:5, ACT 4:8, ACT 4:23, ACT 5:21, ACT 6:12, ACT 23:14, ACT 25:15

title: 7720: elders, in the church
content:
In the NT a group of leaders responsible for managing and teaching a local church. As the name implies, they were normally men of experience and maturity. Although women played a very significant part in early Christian ministry, there is no specific NT evidence for women as elders.

Elders in the Jerusalem church

ACT 15:4 See also ACT 11:30, ACT 15:2, ACT 15:6, ACT 15:22–15:23, ACT 16:4, ACT 21:18

Elders are also called ``overseers''

ACT 20:28 In Ac 20:17 they are described as “elders”. Here they are “overseers”. See also TIT 1:6–1:7

Elders are appointed by apostles or their delegates

ACT 14:23 Here, as always in the NT, people are appointed to a joint eldership. There is no NT example of a single elder leading a church. See also TIT 1:5

Qualifications for elders

TIT 1:6–1:9 As in Ac 20:17,28, “elder” is here synonymous with “overseer”. See also 1TI 3:1–3:7

The role of elders

As leaders

1TI 5:17 See also 1TI 3:5

As teachers

1TI 5:17 See also 1TI 3:2

As pastors and guardians against false teaching

ACT 20:28–20:31 See also TIT 1:9; 1PE 5:1–5:4

In the healing ministry

JAS 5:14

The remuneration and correction of elders

See also 1TI 5:17–5:20

Elders are to receive respect and submission from church members

HEB 13:17 “leaders” is an alternative term for “elders”. See also 1TH 5:12; HEB 13:7

The twenty-four elders in heaven

The elders are seated on thrones

REV 4:4 These elders may be angelic beings corresponding to the 24 orders of Levites (1Chronicles chapters 24–25) or symbolise Israel and the church (compare Rev 21:12–14). See also REV 5:5–5:6, REV 11:16, REV 14:3

The elders worship God and the Lamb

REV 4:10–4:11 See also REV 5:8–5:14, REV 7:11–7:13, REV 19:4

title: 7724: evangelists
content: Those called by God to announce good news and, especially, to proclaim the good news of Jesus Christ. The work was first committed to the apostles and then to other believers gifted by the Holy Spirit for the task.

title: 7725: evangelists, identity of
content:
Some OT figures announced good news, but most evangelists are seen in the NT. The task was first given by Jesus Christ to the apostles and then to other believers. All Christians have a part in evangelism; there is, too, a special role of evangelist within the church, fulfilled by those with a particular gifting from the Holy Spirit.

An evangelist as one who announces good news

God as an announcer of good news

GAL 3:8 See also GEN 12:2–12:3, GEN 22:18, GEN 26:4; JER 31:3–31:4; ACT 10:36

OT messengers announce good news

PSA 96:2–96:3 parallel 1CH 16:23–16:24ISA 40:9 The good news of the end of the Babylonian exile. See also 2KI 7:9; PSA 40:10; ISA 40:1–40:5, ISA 41:27, ISA 52:7; NAM 1:15 the good news of the deliverance from Assyria; MAT 12:41 parallel LUK 11:32; ROM 10:15; 2PE 2:5

Angels announce good news

LUK 2:10–2:11 See also LUK 1:19; REV 14:6

John the Baptist announces good news

LUK 3:18 See also LUK 3:3, LUK 16:16

Jesus Christ as an evangelist

LUK 4:18–4:19 See also ISA 61:1–61:2; MAT 4:23, MAT 9:35; MRK 1:14–1:15; LUK 4:43, LUK 8:1

The disciples as evangelists

Jesus Christ sends out the Twelve

MRK 3:14 See also MAT 10:7–10:8; LUK 9:1–9:2, LUK 9:6

Jesus Christ commissions the apostles

MRK 16:15 See also MAT 28:18–28:20; LUK 24:47; ACT 1:8

The evangelism of the apostles

ACT 5:42 See also ACT 8:25 Peter and John preach the gospel in Samaria; ACT 15:7 Peter preaches the gospel to Cornelius.

Other believers as evangelists

Evangelists are gifted by God

EPH 4:11

Paul as an evangelist

ACT 20:24 See also ROM 1:9, ROM 15:19–15:20; 1CO 1:17; 2CO 2:12; GAL 2:7

Philip the evangelist

ACT 21:8 See also ACT 8:5, ACT 8:12, ACT 8:35, ACT 8:40

Other evangelists

ACT 8:4; 2TI 4:5 Timothy's calling includes that of an evangelist. See also MRK 5:20 parallel LUK 8:39; JHN 4:28–4:29; ACT 18:26 Priscilla and Aquila; 1PE 3:15

Evangelists within the family

MRK 5:19 1CO 7:16; ; 1PE 3:1–3:2; EPH 6:4; ; 2TI 1:5; ; TIT 1:6

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 7726: evangelists, ministry of
content:
Evangelists proclaim the good news of Jesus Christ, calling those who hear to believe in him. This may be in the context of missionary journeys, often in partnership with others, or within a more settled ministry. Carrying out the task requires integrity and commitment in the face of hardships and danger; it also brings joy at seeing the church grow.

The task of evangelists

Announcing good news

ISA 52:7; LUK 4:43 See also ACT 5:42, ACT 8:12, ACT 11:20; ROM 10:15

Proclaiming the gospel

ROM 15:16, ROM 15:20–15:21 See also ACT 8:5, ACT 15:7; 1CO 1:17, 1CO 9:16, 1CO 15:1–15:2; 2CO 10:15–10:16

Testifying to the truth about Jesus Christ

ACT 10:42–10:43 See also JHN 1:35–1:36, JHN 15:26–15:27; ACT 1:8, ACT 2:32–2:33, ACT 17:2–17:3; 1JN 1:1–1:3

Calling people to believe in Jesus Christ

ROM 10:13–10:14 See also MRK 1:14–1:15; ACT 13:38–13:39, ACT 16:30–16:34, ACT 18:4, ACT 26:28; ROM 1:5; 1CO 15:11; 2CO 5:11

The context of the evangelist's work

Missionary journeys

ACT 13:4–13:5, ACT 14:24–14:25, ACT 16:9–16:10

In partnership with others

LUK 10:1 See also MRK 6:7; ACT 10:23, ACT 14:1, ACT 16:6, ACT 17:14–17:15; PHP 1:5

Within a settled period of ministry

2TI 4:5 Timothy as a pastor is also to do the work of an evangelist. See also ACT 18:9–18:11 Paul spends a year and a half in Corinth; ACT 28:30–28:31 Paul preaches the gospel whilst under house arrest in Rome; 1TI 4:13–4:14; 2TI 4:2

The evangelist's integrity

2CO 2:17 See also 2CO 1:12; 1TH 2:3–2:5

The evangelist's commitment

Working so as not to be a burden

1TH 2:8–2:9 See also ACT 20:33–20:34; 1CO 4:12, 1CO 9:6

Facing hardship and danger

2CO 11:23–11:29 ACT 5:18, ACT 20:23–20:24, ACT 21:33; ; EPH 6:19–6:20; ACT 5:40, ACT 16:22–16:23; ACT 7:58, ACT 14:19; ACT 9:29, ACT 21:30–21:31; ACT 20:19; ; 1TH 2:2; ROM 9:1–9:2, ROM 10:1

Financial support for evangelists

1CO 9:11–9:14 See also 1TI 5:18

The evangelist's blessing

Joy at seeing the church grow

1TH 3:7–3:9 See also ACT 15:3; 1TH 2:19–2:20

The satisfaction of seeing new converts grow in their faith

1CO 1:4–1:7; ; EPH 1:15–1:16; ; PHP 1:3–1:6; ; 1TH 1:2–1:3

title: 7730: explanation
content:
The provision of the understanding of a dream, vision, parable, teaching or other matter, the meaning of which is less than obvious.

Examples of the explanation of dreams

By Joseph

GEN 40:6–40:19, GEN 41:10–41:32

By Daniel

DAN 2:16, DAN 2:24, DAN 2:27–2:45, DAN 5:12, DAN 5:17

By angels

DAN 7:15–7:18, DAN 7:23–7:27

Examples of the explanation of visions

By Gabriel

DAN 8:15–8:16

By an angel

See also ZEC 1:8–1:10, ZEC 1:18–1:21; REV 17:3–17:7, REV 17:18

By Peter

ACT 11:4–11:17

Jesus Christ gave his disciples explanations

Of his parables

MRK 4:34 See also MAT 15:15–15:20 parallel MRK 7:17–7:23; MAT 13:36–13:43

Of the Scriptures referring to himself and his relationship to his Father

LUK 24:27 See also JHN 16:25, JHN 16:29–16:30

Of his suffering, death and resurrection

MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31, LUK 9:22 See also JHN 4:25–4:26

The gospel was explained

By Peter, at Pentecost: ACT 2:14, ACT 2:16; ACT 8:30–8:31, ACT 8:34–8:35; ACT 17:2–17:3, ACT 28:23; ACT 18:24–18:26 by Aquila and Priscilla

Examples of other explanations

JDG 14:12–14:17 The Philistines desperately wanted the answer to Samson's riddle.; 1SA 8:9, 1SA 10:25; 1KI 4:29–4:34, 1KI 10:1–10:3 parallel 2CH 9:1–9:2; ; NEH 8:7–8:8

title: 7733: leaders
content: God calls individuals to positions of leadership, both spiritual and political. Such leadership is seen as carrying responsibilities towards both God and his people and is to be exercised with humility.

title: 7734: leaders, spiritual
content:
Under the old covenant God called priests and prophets as spiritual leaders. Later the Pharisees gave spiritual leadership. After Jesus Christ had inaugurated the new covenant, authority was given to the apostles, who in turn appointed leaders in the churches they founded.

Priests as spiritual leaders

They were called by God

HEB 5:4 See also EXO 28:1; DEU 18:5

The high priest represented the people in the presence of God

HEB 5:1 See also EXO 28:29–28:30; NUM 27:21; HEB 8:3

Priests taught and explained God's law

MAL 2:7 See also LEV 10:11; HAG 2:11–2:13

Examples of priests as spiritual leaders

1SA 4:18 The priest Eli was described as leading Israel; ; 1SA 7:5–7:9 Samuel acted as priest on behalf of the people; ; 2CH 24:14 Jehoiada the priest influenced Joash to serve God.

Prophets as spiritual leaders

They were called by God

2PE 1:21 See also ISA 6:8–6:9; JER 1:5

Examples of prophets as spiritual leaders

Samuel led the people from idolatry back to the worship of God: 1SA 7:3–7:4, 1SA 12:20–12:24; 1KI 18:18–18:21 Elijah; ; HAG 1:7–1:9 Haggai

Spiritual leaders in NT times

MAT 2:4 officially recognised teachers of the law; ; MAT 16:1, MAT 21:23 At a local level, elders had social and religious authority in local councils. Nationally, they were included in the Sanhedrin; ; MAT 23:2, MAT 26:59

The failure of spiritual leaders

In the OT

ISA 28:7; ; JER 23:11, JER 23:13–23:17, JER 23:25–23:32; ; MAL 2:1–2:2

In the NT

MAT 23:4–23:15; ; MRK 10:42–10:45 Jesus Christ taught his disciples the qualities required for spiritual leadership.

Spiritual leadership in the church

The apostles delegated authority

ACT 4:34–4:35, ACT 6:2–6:3, ACT 14:23; ; TIT 1:5

The qualifications for church leadership

1TI 3:1–3:9; ; TIT 1:6–1:9

Advice to leaders

ACT 20:28 See also ACT 20:31, ACT 20:35; 1TI 5:17–5:20; 1PE 5:2–5:3

title: 7735: leaders, political
content:
Since God is the ultimate ruler of all things, earthly authority derives from him. Although political leadership was exercised in various ways in Israel, there was always some overlap between political and spiritual leadership.

Israel had a variety of political leaders

Judges

JDG 2:16, JDG 4:4

Kings

1SA 8:1–8:21, 1SA 10:1, 1SA 16:1–16:13; ; 2SA 7:12; ; 1KI 1:28–1:40

Tribal elders and judges

EXO 19:7; ; DEU 21:1–21:2; ; RUT 4:1–4:2; ; JOB 29:7–29:12

Foreign powers imposed rulers on Israel

LUK 3:1

Examples of those who were both political and spiritual leaders

1SA 7:15 Samuel was both prophet and judge. EXO 34:1, EXO 34:27–34:28; JDG 6:25–6:27 Gideon; ; 2KI 18:4 parallel 2CH 31:1 Hezekiah; ; 1CH 6:31 David

Political leaders, like spiritual ones, were called by God

1KI 11:31 The prophet Ahijah confers authority on the leader of a revolt. See also EXO 3:10 Moses; JDG 6:14 Gideon1SA 16:1, 1SA 16:6–16:12 ISA 45:1–45:2 Cyrus was a Persian king, yet he is described as God's “anointed”.

To reject God's leadership is to reject God

1SA 8:6–8:8 See also 1SA 8:5, 1SA 8:19–8:20 Israel wanted a human ruler so that they might be like the surrounding nations.

Responsibilities of leaders

To protect the weak

PSA 72:12–72:14

To administer justice

PSA 72:2; ; PRO 20:8, PRO 29:4, PRO 29:14; ; ISA 9:7, ISA 11:3–11:4

To shepherd the people

PSA 78:70–78:72; ; EZK 34:2–34:6

Attributes required in leaders

ISA 11:1–11:2 Being anointed by the Spirit of God. DEU 17:18–17:20; ; ISA 11:3; ISA 11:5 trustworthiness; ; PRO 8:15–8:16 wisdom

The failure of leaders

Through exploiting their subjects

MAT 20:25 See also DEU 17:16–17:17; 1SA 8:11–8:17; 1KI 12:4; JER 22:13

Through usurping religious authority

1SA 13:8–13:13; ; 2CH 26:16–26:18

Through apostasy

1KI 11:7–11:8; ; 2KI 21:1–21:6

Through trusting in alliances with heathen powers

ISA 31:1 See also 2KI 20:12, 2KI 20:17–20:18; ISA 30:1–30:2; HOS 7:11

Godless leadership is evil

LUK 13:32 See also DAN 7:1–7:7; REV 13:1–13:3

People must have a right attitude to political leaders

They are to be obeyed

ROM 13:1 See also ROM 13:5; 1PE 2:13–2:17

But obedience to God must come first

ACT 5:29 See also DAN 3:18, DAN 6:13

Prayers are to be made for them

1TI 2:1–2:2

title: 7739: missionaries
content: Those called and commissioned by God to fulfil specific tasks, especially that of taking the gospel to those who have not heard it. Missionaries may be sent to other towns, cities or nations, or may be called to fulfil their task closer to home.

title: 7740: missionaries, call of
content:
God calls and sends out his people into the world. All believers are called to share the good news of the gospel, though some are specially commissioned by God, confirmed by the church and compelled by the gospel message to missionary service.

The call to all God's people

PSA 96:2–96:3 parallel 1CH 16:23–16:24MAT 28:19 See also MRK 16:15; JHN 20:21; ACT 1:8

Missionaries in the OT

Those sent to their own people

ISA 6:8–6:10 See also 2PE 2:5 Noah; JER 2:1–2:4 Jeremiah's ministry extended further but was primarily to his own people, Judah; EZK 2:3 Ezekiel

Those sent to other peoples

2KI 17:27–17:28 A priest from Samaria returns to teach the resettled population about the LORD. ISA 42:6, ISA 49:6; ; ACT 13:47; AMO 7:15 Amos, from Judah, is sent to the northern kingdom of Israel.; JON 1:1–1:2, JON 3:1–3:2

Missionaries in the NT

John the Baptist

JHN 1:6–1:7 See also MAL 4:5–4:6; MAT 11:10 parallel LUK 7:27; MRK 1:2–1:4; MAL 3:1; ISA 40:3; JHN 1:33

Jesus Christ's disciples

LUK 9:1–9:6 Healing and deliverance accompanies preaching as part of the disciples' mission. See also MAT 10:5–10:16 During Jesus Christ's earthly ministry the disciples' mission was limited to Israel; MRK 6:7–6:11; LUK 10:1

Other believers

ACT 11:19–11:20 See also ACT 8:5 Philip; ACT 18:24 Apollos; ACT 26:15–26:18 Paul

Receiving the missionary call

The call given by God

JER 1:4–1:10 See also MAT 4:19 Peter and Andrew; ACT 8:26 Philip; ACT 22:21 Paul

The call confirmed by the church

ACT 13:2–13:3 See also ACT 14:26–14:27, ACT 15:40, ACT 22:14–22:15 Paul's call to be a missionary to the Gentiles is communicated through Ananias; GAL 2:7–2:9 Paul's mission to the Gentiles is recognised by the other apostles.

The call through circumstances

ACT 8:4 Persecution drove the early church to wider mission. See also GEN 45:4–45:5 Joseph sold into slavery in Egypt; DAN 1:3–1:6 Daniel among those taken into exile in Babylon; ACT 18:1–18:2 Priscilla and Aquila left Rome because of persecution.

Missionaries compelled by the message

ACT 4:18–4:20 See also 1CO 9:16; 2CO 5:14

title: 7741: missionaries, task of
content:
God commits various tasks to his servants. A principal task of those sent by God is to take the good news of the gospel to those who have not heard it, and so to be a means through which others come to faith.

The need for missionaries

ROM 10:14–10:15 See also ISA 52:7; MAT 9:37–9:38 parallel LUK 10:2

The work of missionaries

Proclaiming good news

ISA 61:1–61:2 See also LUK 4:18–4:19LUK 4:43 See also MAT 4:23, MAT 9:35ACT 11:20 See also ACT 5:42, ACT 8:12, ACT 8:25, ACT 14:6–14:7; ROM 15:16

Calling people to turn to God

ACT 26:20 See also 2CH 30:6–30:9; MRK 1:4 parallel LUK 3:3; 2CO 5:19–5:20

Announcing judgment

JON 3:4 See also JER 25:30–25:31, JER 26:12–26:13; EZK 21:2–21:3; ACT 10:42

Teaching and baptising new believers

MAT 28:19–28:20 See also ACT 2:41–2:42, ACT 8:12–8:13; 1CO 4:17

Taking the message further afield

2CO 10:15–10:16 See also ROM 15:18–15:20, ROM 15:28; MAT 24:14; LUK 24:47; ACT 16:9–16:10, ACT 23:11

Strengthening newly established churches

ACT 18:23 See also ACT 8:14–8:17, ACT 14:21–14:23, ACT 15:36–15:41, ACT 16:4–16:5

Examples of other specific assignments

EXO 3:10 Moses is sent by God to Pharoah; ; JOS 22:3 The eastern tribes complete their mission to help the others take possession of Canaan; ; JDG 6:14 Gideon is sent by God against the Midianites; ; 1SA 15:18–15:20 Saul is given the task of destroying the Amalekites; ; 1SA 16:1 Samuel is sent by God to anoint David as king; ; ISA 48:15 Cyrus is appointed to bring deliverance from Babylon.; ACT 11:30, ACT 12:25; EPH 6:22; ; COL 4:7–4:8

resource.label: 6717: reconciliation, of the world to God

title: 7742: missionaries, support for
content:
Though facing hardships, missionaries receive strength from God to enable them to carry out their task. They are entitled, too, to receive support, encouragement and financial assistance from other believers.

Missionaries face hardships

Physical and emotional stress

MAT 10:17–10:23 parallels MRK 13:11–13:13, LUK 21:12–21:192CO 7:5 See also ROM 8:35–8:36; 2CO 4:11–4:13, 2CO 6:3–6:5, 2CO 6:8–6:10, 2CO 11:23–11:29; PHP 4:12

Opposition to the message

1CO 16:9 See also ACT 4:17–4:18, ACT 6:9, ACT 13:6–13:8; 1TH 2:2

Missionaries strengthened by God

The Holy Spirit gives power to witness

JHN 20:21–20:22 See also LUK 24:49; ACT 1:8; ROM 15:18–15:19; 1CO 2:4–2:5

Jesus Christ promises his presence

MAT 28:20 See also MRK 16:20; ACT 18:10

Missionaries supported by the church

Missionaries supported by the sending church

ACT 14:26 See also ACT 13:3, ACT 15:40

Missionaries supported by believers

ACT 18:27 See also ROM 15:24; 2CO 8:23–8:24; TIT 3:13

Missionary work supported by church leaders

ACT 8:14 See also ACT 11:22–11:23, ACT 15:30–15:32; GAL 2:7–2:9

Missionaries given financial support

PHP 4:14–4:18 See also PHP 2:25; 1CO 9:11–9:14, 1CO 16:5–16:6; GAL 6:6; 1TH 2:6–2:8

Missionaries supported in prayer

COL 4:3 See also EPH 6:19–6:20; PHP 1:19; 1TH 5:25; 2TH 3:1

Missionaries encouraged by visits and news

ACT 28:15 See also PHP 2:19; 2TI 4:9–4:11; TIT 3:12; PHM 1:12–1:13

resource.label: 8787: opposition, to God's person and work

title: 7745: ordination
content:
The appointment to a public ministry or office, including investing with the authority required for this ministry. The most important biblical model of ordination is provided by the OT priesthood.

Ordination of the OT priesthood

The ceremony of ordination

EXO 28:41 See also EXO 29:29–29:46, EXO 30:30; LEV 8:30, LEV 8:33; NUM 3:3

The role of the ordained priest

To properly apply the blood of the ordination offering: EXO 29:20–29:21; ; LEV 7:37, LEV 8:18; LEV 16:32 to make atonement; ; LEV 21:10–21:12 to observe a strict code of behaviour

Jesus is the Messiah, the anointed one

MAT 16:16 The Greek word “Christos”, like the Hebrew word “Messiah”, means “anointed (by God)”.

Ordination in the NT

Jesus Christ appointed disciples without reference to a ceremony of ordination

MRK 3:14–3:15 parallel LUK 6:13

The apostles commissioned people for service by laying on of hands

ACT 6:6 See also NUM 8:10–8:11, NUM 27:18–27:23; ACT 13:3 Barnabas and Saul were commissioned for missionary work.

The appointment of elders

ACT 14:23; ; 1TI 1:18, 1TI 4:14, 1TI 5:22; ; 2TI 1:6; ; TIT 1:5, TIT 1:6–1:9 Certain qualities are required in an appointed elder or minister.

title: 7748: overseers
content:
In the OT a variety of supervisory roles are mentioned. In the NT “overseers” (traditionally translated “bishops”) describes the role of local church leaders.

Overseers in the OT

In secular life

EXO 5:6; ; RUT 2:5–2:6; ; 1KI 5:16; ; 2CH 2:2; ; NEH 3:5; ; PRO 6:6–6:7

In religious life

NUM 8:22; ; 1CH 25:2; ; 2CH 23:18; ; EZR 3:9; ; NEH 11:11; ; JER 20:1

Overseers in the NT

In secular life

MAT 20:8

In the church

PHP 1:1 A title for local church leaders.

The role of local church ``elders''

ACT 20:28 Here, as elsewhere in the NT, “elders” and “overseers” are virtually synonymous. See also 1PE 5:1–5:4

Their qualifications

1TI 3:1–3:7 See also TIT 1:7

A title of Jesus Christ

1PE 2:25

resource.label: 2203: Jesus Christ, titles and names of

title: 7751: persuasion
content:
Attempting to win others over to one's own point of view. It can be either positive, as with preaching the gospel, or it can spring from a malign intent to seduce people from the truth.

Means of persuasion

The persuasiveness of argument

ACT 16:15 See also JDG 19:3, JDG 19:7; 2SA 3:35; ACT 5:40, ACT 19:32–19:41, ACT 21:14

The persuasiveness of proof

ACT 1:3 See also GEN 45:25–45:28

The persuasiveness of experience

1CO 14:24–14:25

The first Christians were persuaded of the truth of their faith

2TI 1:12 See also ROM 4:20–4:21, ROM 8:38–8:39; 2TI 3:14

The apostles tried to persuade others of the truth of the gospel

ACT 18:4 See also ACT 17:2–17:4, ACT 19:8, ACT 26:26–26:29, ACT 28:23–28:24; 2CO 5:11

Scripture advises patience when seeking to persuade those in power

PRO 25:15 See also LUK 18:1–18:5

Scripture warns against the persuasiveness of evil

The persuasiveness of adulterers

PRO 7:21 See also PRO 5:3, PRO 7:5

The persuasiveness of false prophets

JER 28:15

Examples of God's servants suffering through the persuasiveness of others

MAT 27:20 parallel MRK 15:11 See also ACT 14:19

title: 7754: preaching
content: The announcing of the good news of God by his servants through the faithful revelation of God's will, the exposition of God's word and the proclamation of Jesus Christ, the Son of God. Jesus Christ had an important preaching ministry.

title: 7755: preaching, importance of
content:
Preaching has a central place among God's people and is vital to their life and growth. It is authorised by God, empowered by the Holy Spirit and expressed supremely by Jesus Christ.

Preaching has its origin in God

It is a divine command

MRK 16:15 parallel MAT 28:18–28:20 See also JON 1:1–1:2; MAT 10:5–10:7 parallels MRK 6:7–6:12, LUK 9:1–9:6

It is rooted in God's grace

EPH 3:7–3:9 See also ISA 6:1–6:10; ROM 15:15–15:16

It is empowered by God's Spirit

ACT 1:8 See also ISA 61:1–61:3; LUK 24:46–24:49; ACT 2:1–2:11, ACT 4:8–4:12, ACT 10:44; 1CO 2:4–2:5

The importance of preaching and Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ himself came to preach

MRK 1:38 parallel LUK 4:43 See also EPH 2:17

Jesus Christ's own ministry involved much preaching

MAT 4:23 See also MAT 11:1–11:5 parallel LUK 7:18–7:22

Jesus Christ commissioned his disciples to preach

MRK 3:14–3:15 See also MAT 10:5–10:7 parallel LUK 9:1–9:2

The importance of preaching and the church

It is a natural part of the church's life

ACT 8:4 See also ACT 3:11–3:26, ACT 15:35

It is a trust from God

GAL 2:7 See also 1TH 2:4; 1TI 1:11; TIT 1:3

It is an integral aspect of key ministries in the church

1TI 3:2 See also EPH 4:11–4:12; 2TI 4:2–4:5; TIT 1:7–1:9

Its importance to Paul

1CO 1:17–1:18 See also ACT 9:20–9:22, ACT 18:5; ROM 1:14–1:15; 1CO 1:22–1:25

It is an apostolic command

1TI 4:13 See also 2TI 4:2

The importance of preaching for salvation

ROM 10:14–10:15 See also ISA 52:7; ROM 10:17; 1CO 1:21

The importance of preaching means preachers and teachers will be judged more strictly

JAS 3:1 See also ROM 2:17–2:24

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 7756: preaching, content of
content:
Preaching is centred on the nature and will of God and his claims on all people. Expressed in prophecy, declaration or teaching, it includes the proclamation of the way of salvation to unbelievers and instruction about the faith to believers.

Preaching and the revelation of God's character, word and will

ACT 20:26–20:27 See also EXO 8:1; 1KI 12:21–12:24; JER 7:1–7:11; EZK 2:3–3:4

Preaching and the declaration of the gospel

Declaring the kingdom

MRK 1:14–1:15 parallel MAT 4:17 See also MAT 4:23 parallel LUK 8:1; ACT 19:8, ACT 20:25

Declaring the person of Jesus Christ and his life

ACT 2:22 See also ACT 10:36–10:38, ACT 28:31; 2CO 1:19

Declaring the facts of the cross and the resurrection

ACT 2:23–2:24 See also ACT 5:30, ACT 10:39–10:42, ACT 13:28–13:31; 1CO 1:22–1:24, 1CO 15:12–15:17

Declaring the victory and exaltation of Jesus Christ

ACT 2:33–2:35 See also ACT 5:31; 1PE 3:18–3:22

Declaring that Jesus is both Messiah (Christ) and Lord

ACT 2:36 See also ACT 5:42, ACT 8:5, ACT 9:20–9:22, ACT 10:36, ACT 18:5

Declaring the call to repent

ACT 17:30 See also MRK 1:15 parallel MAT 4:17; ACT 2:38, ACT 3:19, ACT 26:20

Declaring the promise of forgiveness

ACT 13:38 See also LUK 24:46–24:47; ACT 2:38, ACT 5:31, ACT 10:43

Preaching finds expression in the teaching of believers

The central place of teaching in the lives of the first Christians

ACT 2:42 See also ACT 6:2, ACT 11:25–11:26, ACT 15:35, ACT 18:24–18:26, ACT 20:20

Teaching from the Scriptures

ACT 18:11 See also 2CH 17:7–17:9; NEH 8:2–8:8

Teaching on how to live

MAT 28:19–28:20 See also EPH 4:20–4:24; 1TH 4:1–4:2; TIT 2:1–2:15

Preaching and the edification of believers

2TI 4:2 See also ACT 13:42–13:43, ACT 14:21–14:22, ACT 20:2; 1CO 14:26–14:31

Preaching and the continuation of apostolic doctrine

Preaching should be rooted in apostolic doctrine, which is to be faithfully handed on

2TI 2:2 See also 1CO 11:2; 2TH 2:15; 2TI 1:13–1:14

Preaching that does not conform to apostolic doctrine is to be rejected

1TI 1:3–1:4 See also GAL 1:6–1:9; 1TI 4:1–4:7; TIT 1:9–1:14

Preaching and the rejection of merely human wisdom

1CO 2:1–2:5 See also 1CO 1:18–1:25

title: 7757: preaching, effects of
content:
Faithful preaching leads to, through the grace of God, the repentance of sinners, the birth of faith and the nourishment of believers. It can also evoke hostility from unbelievers.

The basis of effective preaching

It depends upon the grace of God

ACT 4:33 See also ISA 55:10–55:11; ACT 14:26–14:27; 1CO 15:10–15:11

It depends upon the power of the cross, not human wisdom

1CO 1:17–1:25 See also 1CO 2:1–2:5

It depends upon the power of the Holy Spirit

LUK 4:18–4:19 See also ISA 61:1–61:2; ACT 2:1–2:11, ACT 10:44–10:48; 1CO 2:4–2:5

It requires effective, supporting prayer

COL 4:3–4:4 See also ACT 4:29–4:31, ACT 6:2–6:4, ACT 13:1–13:5; 2TH 3:1

It needs to be received with faith

HEB 4:2 See also 1TH 2:13

Preaching and the repentance of sinners

MAT 12:41 parallel LUK 11:32 See also JON 3:1–3:10; MAT 3:1–3:6 parallels MRK 1:3–1:5, LUK 3:3–3:6

Preaching and the birth of faith

ACT 4:4 See also ACT 2:38–2:41, ACT 8:9–8:13, ACT 17:11–17:12; 1CO 15:1–15:2

Preaching and the nourishment of believers

ACT 14:21–14:22 See also ROM 16:25–16:27; EPH 4:11–4:16; COL 2:6–2:7

Effective preaching and its authentication by miracles

MRK 16:20 See also MAT 4:23–4:25; LUK 9:1–9:6 parallels MAT 10:5–10:14, MRK 6:7–6:11; ACT 4:29–4:30, ACT 8:5–8:8, ACT 14:1–14:3

Inappropriate responses to preaching

Amazement

MAT 7:28–7:29 See also LUK 4:31–4:32 parallel MRK 1:21–1:22; ACT 2:5–2:12, ACT 4:13–4:14

Offence

MRK 6:1–6:6 parallel MAT 13:54–13:58 See also 1CO 1:22–1:23

Mere academic interest

ACT 17:16–17:32 Paul in Athens and before the Areopagus; ; ACT 24:22–24:26 Paul before Felix and Drusilla

Mockery

ACT 17:32 See also 2CH 36:15–36:16; ACT 2:13

Hostility

ACT 17:13 See also ACT 4:1–4:3, ACT 5:27–5:40, ACT 7:54–7:60, ACT 13:49–13:51, ACT 17:5–17:9

title: 7758: preachers, call of
content:
God declares his word through called and anointed preachers. He overcomes their reluctance, helps their weakness, authorises their message and confirms the truth of what they declare.

God's call of preachers

It is not dependent upon background

AMO 7:14–7:15 See also JER 1:1–1:5; MAT 4:18–4:20 parallels MRK 1:16–1:19, LUK 5:1–5:11; ACT 9:10–9:16

It is not dependent upon ability

EXO 4:10–4:12 See also JER 1:6–1:9; ACT 4:13

It is not dependent upon willingness

EXO 4:13 See also JON 1:1–1:3; ACT 9:1–9:6

It is a matter of God's sovereign choice

JER 1:4–1:5 See also ISA 49:1–49:6; EZK 2:1–2:5; AMO 7:14–7:15; LUK 4:18–4:19; ISA 61:1–61:2; GAL 1:15

God's commissioning of preachers

They are commissioned to declare God's word

EZK 3:1; ROM 10:13–10:15 See also JER 1:9; JON 3:1–3:2; MAT 28:18–28:20

They are to declare God's word with authority

TIT 2:15 See also JER 1:9–1:10; MRK 3:14–3:15; LUK 9:1–9:2

They are to declare God's word without fear

JER 1:7–1:8 See also EZK 2:3–2:7, EZK 3:7–3:9; ACT 5:27–5:29; 2TI 1:6–1:8

God's constraint upon preachers

1CO 9:16 See also JER 20:8–20:9; ACT 4:18–4:20; ROM 1:14–1:15

God's confirmation of his call upon preachers

ACT 14:3 See also EXO 4:1–4:9; 1KI 17:17–17:24; MRK 16:20; ACT 4:29–4:31; 1CO 2:4

title: 7759: preachers, qualifications for
content:
Those entrusted with the task of preaching must ensure that their lives are in line with their message. They must be of good character and conduct, be consistent in all they teach and do and be accountable to others.

Preachers must be of good character

1TI 4:12 See also 1TH 2:9–2:10; 1TI 4:16; 2TI 2:20–2:26; TIT 2:7–2:8; JAS 3:1 That the judgment is based on character, and not just on teaching, seems clear from the references to guarding the tongue which follow.

Preachers must practise what they preach

MAT 23:2–23:4 See also ROM 2:21–2:23; GAL 2:11–2:14

Preachers must be accountable to others and to God

MRK 6:30 parallel LUK 9:10 See also ACT 14:26–14:27, ACT 21:17–21:19; GAL 2:1–2:2

Preachers must not look for honour for themselves

MRK 12:38–12:40 parallels MAT 23:5–23:12, LUK 20:45–20:47 See also ACT 14:11–14:15; 1CO 3:5–3:6; 1TH 2:3–2:6

Preachers must not look for personal gain

2CO 2:17 See also ACT 20:33–20:35; 2CO 11:7–11:9; 1TH 2:6–2:9

Preachers must be people of integrity

2CO 4:2 See also 2CO 1:12; 1TH 2:3–2:6

title: 7760: preachers, responsibilities of
content:
Those entrusted with the responsibility of preaching are to discharge it faithfully, boldly and persistently in the power of the Holy Spirit. The Christian community should honour faithful preachers and challenge those who fail to honour their responsibility.

The responsibilities of preachers

They must be diligent in their preaching

2TI 2:15 See also 1TI 4:13–4:16; 2TI 4:1–4:5

They must be faithful in their preaching

ACT 20:20 See also JER 26:1–26:2, JER 42:4; ACT 20:25–20:27

They must be persistent in their preaching

2TI 4:1–4:5 See also ACT 18:4–18:6, ACT 20:31; 1TH 2:1–2:2

They must be bold in their preaching

ACT 28:31 See also JER 26:7–26:15; AMO 7:10–7:17; ACT 4:18–4:20, ACT 5:27–5:29, ACT 14:1–14:3

They must be encouraging in their preaching

ACT 14:21–14:22 See also ACT 15:32; 1CO 14:3–14:5; 1TH 2:11–2:12; 2TI 4:2

They must be filled with the Holy Spirit

ACT 1:8; 1CO 2:4 See also ACT 4:31–4:33; 1TH 1:5; 1PE 1:12

They must be compassionate in their preaching

MRK 6:34 See also MAT 9:35–9:38

The responsibilities of the church towards preachers

Faithful preachers should be honoured

1TI 5:17 See also MAT 13:53–13:57 parallel MRK 6:2–6:4; LUK 10:3–10:8; HEB 13:7

Unfaithful preachers are to be exposed and will be judged

GAL 1:6–1:9 See also JER 14:14–14:16; EZK 13:1–13:23; 1TI 1:3–1:4, 1TI 4:1–4:7; TIT 1:10–1:11; 2PE 2:1–2:3; REV 2:14–2:16, REV 2:20–2:23

title: 7761: preaching, and Holy Spirit
content: See 3224: Holy Spirit, and preaching

title: 7762: preaching, of Jesus Christ
content: See 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 7766: priests
content: A group of men charged with the responsibility of mediating between God and his people, ensuring proper worship, and maintaining the spiritual health of the people of God.

resource.label: 7769: priests, types of in NT times

title: 7767: priests, institution in OT times
content:
Priests played an important role in the OT and were regarded as having a special status and place in the life of Israel.

Pagan priests in the OT

1SA 6:1–6:2; ; 2KI 23:5; ; HOS 10:5; ; ZEP 1:4

The special status of priests

EZR 1:5; ; ISA 24:2; ; HOS 4:9; ; MIC 3:11

Associated with the descendants of Levi through Aaron

The exclusive nature of the priesthood

1CH 6:49 Only God has the right to nominate those who mediate between him and humanity. See also EXO 6:16–6:20 This genealogy shows Moses and Aaron were both descended from Levi; EXO 28:1, EXO 29:44, EXO 30:30; NUM 16:40DEU 18:1; JER 33:21

Micah's priest

JDG 17:13

The Shiloh priesthood

1CH 24:3 See also 1CH 6:27

The priests in Dan

JDG 18:30

Descendants of Zadok

EZK 44:15–44:16 See also 2SA 8:17; 1CH 6:50–6:53, 1CH 24:3

Criticism of priestly appointments that were not Levitical

1KI 12:31 See also 1KI 13:33; 2CH 11:13–11:14, 2CH 13:9

The appointment of priests

Their preliminary purification

EXO 29:4 See also EXO 30:19, EXO 40:12

Their anointing

EXO 30:30–30:32 See also LEV 8:30; NUM 3:3 Kings and priests also were anointed.

Their consecration

EXO 29:1 See also EXO 29:12–29:14, EXO 29:18, EXO 29:20–29:21; LEV 8:12

Their ordination

EXO 29:35 See also EXO 29:9, EXO 29:29, EXO 29:33; LEV 8:22, LEV 8:33; JDG 17:5, JDG 17:12

The dress of the priests

EXO 28:40 See also EXO 28:42, EXO 39:27–39:29; LEV 8:13

title: 7768: priests, function in OT times
content:
Priests had a unique role: administering the sanctuary, offering sacrifice and leading and teaching the people. Accordingly they were set apart by a special code of conduct.

The priests were set apart

They were consecrated to the LORD

LEV 22:9 See also EXO 28:1, EXO 28:4, EXO 28:41, EXO 29:1, EXO 29:44, EXO 30:34, EXO 31:10, EXO 35:19, EXO 39:41

They were not to incur uncleanness

LEV 21:1 Some exceptions to uncleanness by touching a corpse are listed in verses 2–3. See also EZK 44:25

They were not to shave or mutilate themselves

LEV 21:5 See also EZK 44:20

They were not to marry any but a virtuous Israelite

LEV 21:7 See also EZK 44:22

They were not to drink wine before entering the sanctuary

LEV 10:9 See also EZK 44:21

They were disqualified by physical defects

LEV 21:17 See also LEV 21:18–21:22

They were not to allow outsiders to eat the sacred offerings

LEV 22:15–22:16 See also LEV 24:9; 1SA 21:4; MAT 12:4

They were not to own land in Israel

NUM 18:20 See also EZK 44:28

They were not to offer unacceptable sacrifices

LEV 22:20 See also MAL 1:7–1:9

They were to obey God's law meticulously

LEV 22:31 See also EZK 44:24

The priests supervised the sanctuary

They only had access to holy things

NUM 3:10 See also NUM 4:5; EZK 40:45–40:46, EZK 44:16; JOL 2:17

They tended the sanctuary lamps

EXO 27:21

They carried the ark

DEU 31:9 See also JOS 3:8, JOS 3:15, JOS 3:17, JOS 4:9, JOS 4:16, JOS 6:12

The priests supervised the sacrifices

LEV 1:5 See also LEV 1:8, LEV 2:2; 1CH 9:30

The priests led the nation

As role models

MAL 2:6

As teachers

EZK 44:23 See also LEV 10:10; DEU 31:10–31:13; EZR 7:6; MAL 2:7

As judges

DEU 17:9 See also DEU 19:17, DEU 21:5; 2CH 19:8; EZK 44:24

As encouragers in battle

NUM 10:8 See also JOS 6:4; 2CH 13:12

Prophetic criticism of the priests

LAM 4:13 See also JER 2:8, JER 6:13; EZK 7:26, EZK 22:26; HOS 4:6–4:9, HOS 6:9; ZEP 3:4

title: 7769: priests, types of in NT times
content:
The NT points to the fulfilment of the OT priesthood in Jesus Christ, and develops the idea that all Christian believers can be seen as priests. It also records the continuing role of priests in the life of Israel and pagan nations at this time.

Pagan priests

ACT 14:13

Jewish priests

The chief priests

MAT 16:21

The Sadducees

ACT 5:17 The Sadducees were a conservative party composed entirely of priests who were influential in the Sanhedrin.

Zechariah

LUK 1:5 See also 1CH 24:10

Emissaries to John the Baptist

JHN 1:19 Levites were closely associated with priests as temple personnel, perhaps mentioned here because of their teaching function. See also 2CH 35:3; NEH 8:7–8:9; LUK 10:31–10:32

Jesus Christ as high priest

HEB 8:1–8:2 See also HEB 4:14–4:16

Christians as priests

REV 1:5–1:6 See also 1PE 2:5, 1PE 2:9; REV 5:10, REV 20:6

title: 7770: priests, tasks in NT times
content:
The priests mentioned in the NT performed various functions, all of which are fulfilled in Jesus Christ and the church, which is his body.

Priests offered sacrifices

Sacrifices made by pagan priests

ACT 14:13

Jewish sacrifice

HEB 10:11

The sacrifice of Jesus Christ

HEB 9:14 Jesus Christ is both priest and victim: his own body takes the place occupied by sacrifices in the OT ritual. See also HEB 10:10

The Christian sacrifice of a surrendered life

ROM 12:1 See also HEB 13:16

The Christian sacrifice of praise

HEB 13:15

Priests made intercession

The burning of incense

LUK 1:9 See also EXO 30:7–30:8

Christian intercession

REV 5:8 See also 1PE 2:5; REV 1:6, REV 5:10, REV 20:6

Priests were guardians of tradition

They were trained in the teaching and application of OT law

MAT 2:3–2:6; ; JHN 1:19

The Christian ``tradition''

1CO 15:1–15:5; ; 2TI 1:13–1:14, 2TI 2:2; ; JUD 1:3

Priests declared people clean or unclean

They supplied ritual certification of cleansing

MAT 8:4 parallels MRK 1:44, LUK 5:14 See also LEV 13:2–13:3, LEV 14:2; LUK 17:14

Christians pronounce forgiveness

JHN 20:21–20:23; ; JAS 5:16

title: 7772: prophets
content: Individuals called and empowered by God to declare his will to his people, including the disclosure of his future intentions to save and judge his people. The term refers particularly to the great OT prophets, such as Isaiah and Jeremiah, who were raised up to guide the people of God at critical moments in their history and whose ministries and teaching remain of continuing importance today. At several points in his ministry, Jesus Christ was acclaimed as a prophet.

title: 7773: prophets, role of
content:
As agents of God, prophets acted in various capacities, as was required of them, in order to carry out his will and fulfil his purpose.

Prophets as God's spokesmen

They heralded impending judgment

JON 3:4 See also 1KI 16:7; 2KI 22:15–22:17 parallel 2CH 34:23–34:25; JER 1:14–1:16, JER 5:14–5:17; EZK 5:8–5:12, EZK 38:17

They advocated repentance

2KI 17:13 See also 2CH 24:19; JER 3:12–3:13, JER 35:15, JER 44:4; ZEC 1:3–1:6

They conveyed messages from God to the nations

EZK 25:8–25:11 See also JER 46:13, JER 47:1, JER 49:34, JER 50:1; EZK 25:2, EZK 25:12–25:14, EZK 26:3; AMO 1:9; EZK 29:2; AMO 1:3, AMO 1:6, AMO 1:13, AMO 2:1

The supernatural activities of prophets

They revealed future events

EZK 17:16–17:21 See also 1KI 11:29–11:31, 1KI 20:13; ISA 7:13–7:14 the birth of Immanuel; JER 29:10; DAN 2:38–2:44, DAN 9:2; MAT 2:15; HOS 11:1MAT 2:17–2:18; JER 31:15 MAT 8:17; ISA 53:4; ACT 3:18, ACT 11:27–11:29, ACT 21:10–21:11

They received dreams and visions

NUM 12:6 See also EZK 7:26; DAN 7:1, DAN 8:1; HOS 12:10

They worked miracles

2KI 5:3 See also 1KI 17:13–17:24; 2KI 4:3–4:6, 2KI 4:15–4:17, 2KI 4:32–4:35, 2KI 4:40–4:44, 2KI 6:5–6:6

Prophets as intermediaries

Between God and the people

DEU 18:16–18:18 See also EXO 6:30–7:1; 2SA 7:4–7:7, 2SA 12:7–12:14, 2SA 12:24–12:25, 2SA 24:11–24:12; 1KI 20:42; 2KI 20:1 parallel ISA 38:1; 2CH 12:5, 2CH 28:9–28:11

Between the people and God

1SA 7:9 See also GEN 20:7; 2KI 20:11; 2CH 32:20; JER 11:14, JER 27:18; EZK 14:7

Their responsibility as watchmen

EZK 3:17–3:21 The intermediary role of the prophet is highlighted by the image of a watchman who receives messages from God and conveys them to the people. See also EZK 33:1–33:7; HOS 9:8

Prophets as leaders in the community

They gave advice

ZEC 7:2–7:3 See also 1SA 9:6; 1KI 22:6; 2KI 3:11, 2KI 6:8–6:10, 2KI 22:14 parallel 2CH 34:22; 2CH 29:25; ISA 37:2

They gave encouragement

ACT 15:32 See also EZR 5:1–5:2; JER 29:4–29:7; EZK 11:17–11:21; HAG 1:13, HAG 2:4

They led the people

HOS 12:13 See also DEU 18:15; 1SA 7:6; 1KI 18:40

They appointed kings and leaders

1SA 10:1 See also 1SA 16:13; 1KI 1:34, 1KI 1:38, 1KI 19:15–19:16; 2KI 9:6

Prophets as writers

1CH 29:29 See also 2CH 9:29, 2CH 13:22, 2CH 26:22, 2CH 32:32; ISA 30:8; JER 30:2, JER 36:4, JER 51:60; EZK 43:11; HAB 2:2

title: 7774: prophets, false
content:
Men or women who falsely claim to speak on God's behalf. They are denounced in the OT and NT for leading people astray. Scripture provides criteria by which true prophets may be recognised and distinguished from false prophets, who often speak from base motives.

Criteria for recognising true prophets

A true prophet's word will be fulfilled

DEU 18:21–18:22 See also JER 28:8–28:9

A true prophet's teaching commends righteous behaviour

DEU 13:1–13:4 See also EZK 13:17–13:23, EZK 14:4–14:8; LAM 2:14

A true prophet's godly life will reflect his calling

MAT 7:15–7:20 See also ISA 28:7; JER 23:10–23:11, JER 23:14; ZEP 3:4

A true prophet will acknowledge Jesus Christ as divine

1JN 4:1–4:6

The motivations of false prophets

Their messages originate with themselves

JER 23:16 See also JER 14:13–14:14, JER 23:25–23:32, JER 23:36; EZK 13:1–13:7

Their messages may be occasioned by popular demand

JER 29:8–29:9 NUM 22:35, NUM 22:38, NUM 23:12, NUM 23:26, NUM 24:12–24:13; ; 1KI 22:13–22:14; ISA 30:10–30:11; ; JER 5:30–5:31; ; MIC 2:11

Their messages may be given for financial reward

NEH 6:12–6:13 See also JER 6:13–6:14; EZK 13:19; MIC 3:11; 2PE 2:1–2:3

Their messages may be the result of deception

1KI 22:19–22:23 parallel 2CH 18:18–18:22 See also EZK 14:9

Their messages may be inspired by divination

JER 14:14 See also EZK 13:6–13:7, EZK 22:28; ACT 13:6

Examples of false prophets

In the OT

NEH 6:10–6:13 See also 1KI 22:1–22:28 parallel 2CH 18:1–18:27; JER 27:9–27:10, JER 27:14–27:18

False prophetesses

NEH 6:14; ; EZK 13:17

Prophets of other gods

JER 23:13 See also 1KI 18:19–18:40; 2KI 10:19; JER 2:8

In the NT

MAT 24:24 parallel MRK 13:22 See also MAT 24:10–24:11; ACT 13:6–13:11REV 2:20, REV 16:13, REV 19:20, REV 20:10

The punishment of false prophets

DEU 18:20 See also DEU 13:5; ZEC 13:3; JER 14:15JER 28:15–28:17, JER 29:21–29:23, JER 29:30–29:32 JER 23:34–23:40; EZK 13:8–13:16; MIC 3:5–3:6

title: 7775: prophets, lives of
content:
God called individuals from differing backgrounds to speak as prophets on his behalf, sometimes in difficult circumstances.

The backgrounds of the prophets

EXO 2:1–2:10 Moses was brought up in the Egyptian royal court; ; 1SA 1:21–1:28 Samuel was a temple servant at Shiloh; ; 1KI 19:19–19:21 Elisha was a farmer; ; EZK 1:3 Ezekiel was a priest living in exile in Babylonia.; AMO 1:1, AMO 7:14

The calling of the prophets

Their initial encounter with God

ISA 6:1–6:4 See also EXO 3:1–3:6; EZK 1:4–1:28

God communicating his call

JER 1:4–1:5 See also ISA 6:8–6:10; EZK 2:3–3:5; JON 1:1, JON 3:1

Their response to God's call

JER 1:6–1:10 See also EXO 3:11–3:12

The family life of the prophets

JER 16:1–16:4 See also ISA 7:3, ISA 8:1–8:4; EZK 24:15–24:24; HOS 1:2–1:11, HOS 3:1–3:5

The hardships faced by prophets

Persecution

HEB 11:32–11:38 See also ACT 7:52

Reviling and beating

LUK 6:22–6:23 parallel MAT 5:11–5:12 See also JER 20:1–20:2; JAS 5:10

Imprisonment

JER 37:11–37:16 See also 1KI 22:26–22:27 parallel 2CH 18:25–18:26; 2CH 16:10; JER 37:21, JER 38:6–38:13

Killing

1KI 19:14 See also 1KI 18:4, 1KI 18:13, 1KI 19:2; JER 26:8–26:11, JER 26:20–26:23; MAT 23:29–23:31, MAT 23:37 parallel LUK 13:34; LUK 11:47–11:49; 1TH 2:4–2:15

The death of prophets

DEU 34:1–34:8 Moses; ; 1SA 28:3 Samuel; ; 2KI 2:11–2:12 Elijah did not die but was taken up in a chariot of fire.

title: 7776: prophet, Jesus Christ as
content: See 2318: Jesus Christ, as prophet

title: 7778: school of prophets
content:
A company of prophets working and prophesying together under the instruction and leadership of a more experienced prophet. There were also similar companies of false prophets.

Bethel

2KI 2:3

Jericho

2KI 2:15

Samuel and a school of prophets

1SA 19:20

Elisha and a school of prophets

2KI 2:15 See also 2KI 4:1, 2KI 4:38, 2KI 6:1–6:4

1KI 18:4 See also 1KI 18:13

Schools of false prophets

1KI 18:19, 1KI 18:40, 1KI 22:6 parallel 2CH 18:5; ; 1KI 22:10 parallel 2CH 18:9; ; 1KI 22:12–22:14 parallel 2CH 18:11–18:13; ; 1KI 22:19–22:23 parallel 2CH 18:18–18:22; ; NEH 6:14

title: 7781: seer
content:
Seer as an alternative title for a prophet

1SA 9:9 Literally, a “seer” is “one who sees”, especially one who claims to see into the future or to have insight into the will of God. See also 2SA 24:11 parallel 1CH 21:9

Seers used by people to enquire of God

1SA 9:8–9:9

David had seers to enquire of God for him

1CH 25:1–25:5 See also 2SA 24:11 parallel 1CH 21:9; 2SA 15:27; 2CH 29:25, 2CH 35:15

Seers were used by God to bring his word to the people

2KI 17:13

Seers were used by God to bring his word to the king

2CH 16:7–16:10, 2CH 33:18

The words of a seer were sometimes unwelcome

2CH 16:7–16:10; ; ISA 30:10; ; AMO 7:12–7:13

Samuel was called a seer

1SA 9:11–9:14, 1SA 9:18–9:19; ; 1CH 9:22, 1CH 26:28, 1CH 29:29

Asaph, the psalmist, called a seer

2CH 29:30

Seers wrote Israel's history

2CH 33:18–33:19 See also 1CH 29:29; 2CH 9:29, 2CH 12:15

God judges Israel by blinding the seers

ISA 29:10; ; MIC 3:7

title: 7784: shepherd
content: The work of a shepherd was important and responsible in the rural world of ancient Palestine. Since it involved leading, protecting and feeding a flock, it is seen as a metaphor for the task of leadership. Scripture declares that God is the Shepherd of his people.

title: 7785: shepherd, as occupation
content:
The occupation of a shepherd was both humble and honourable. In the marginal hill country of Palestine it was particularly demanding.

A shepherd as a keeper of flocks of animals

GEN 46:32 The Hebrew word refers to a keeper of goats and cattle as well as sheep.

The tasks of a shepherd

Tending, feeding and watering

PSA 23:2 See also GEN 29:3, GEN 26:19–26:22; EXO 22:5; LUK 13:15; JHN 21:15–21:17

Keeping and protecting

JER 31:10 See also GEN 4:2; PSA 23:4 The rod is a crook (for extracting sheep from difficulty) and the staff a club (for warding off wild animals); 1SA 17:34–17:37

Leading, not driving, sheep

JHN 10:3–10:4 See also EXO 3:1; PSA 23:2, PSA 77:20; ISA 40:11; JER 50:6

Gathering animals together

JHN 10:3 See also PSA 147:4; ISA 40:26; MAT 25:32

The shepherd's routine

The search for pasture

GEN 37:17

The midday rest

SNG 1:7

The night-watch

LUK 2:8; ; JER 43:12

Shearing, an annual festival

1SA 25:7–25:8

The use of pens and folds

PSA 50:9; ; HAB 3:17; ; JHN 10:1, JHN 10:16

Breeding

GEN 30:40

Rearing

Rebekah's ruse depended on a method used to get a ewe to accept an alien lamb: GEN 27:16, GEN 27:27

The problems faced by shepherds

Drought and dry winds

JER 12:4; ; HOS 4:3, HOS 13:15; ; JOL 1:17–1:20; ; AMO 1:2

With animals and thieves

1SA 17:34–17:37, 1SA 25:15; ; JHN 10:1, JHN 10:12

Shepherds as employers

JOB 1:3 See also 1SA 25:2; 2KI 3:4; AMO 1:1

Shepherds as employees

The status of a shepherd

1SA 16:11, 1SA 17:28; ; LUK 2:8

The responsibilities of a shepherd

GEN 31:39; ; AMO 3:12 A shepherd who lost an animal to wild beasts had to produce evidence; ; MAT 18:12 parallel LUK 15:4

title: 7786: shepherd, as king and leader
content:
Shepherd was a standard term for a leader or king throughout the ancient world. In this sense it often appears in the Bible.

General term for a king or leader

ISA 44:28 The prophet describes this non-Jewish king as God's shepherd; JER 6:3 See also 2SA 7:7; PSA 77:20; ISA 31:4; JER 12:10, JER 49:19, JER 50:44

The LORD as shepherd

Of Israel

PSA 80:1 See also GEN 49:24; PSA 28:9; ISA 40:11

Of the individual

PSA 23:1 See also GEN 48:15

David as the model of a good shepherd

Experience as a real shepherd

PSA 78:70–78:71 See also 1SA 17:34–17:37; 2SA 12:1–12:7 The king is brought to his senses by a “shepherd” story; PSA 23:1–23:4

The shepherd faces death for his flock

2SA 24:17 See also EXO 32:32; JHN 10:11, JHN 10:15, JHN 10:17

David as a model of the coming Messiah

EZK 34:23 See also JER 23:4–23:5, JER 30:9; HOS 3:5

A good shepherd as a leader ``after God's own heart''

JER 3:15 See also ISA 40:11; JER 31:10; EZK 34:12

A bad shepherd

1KI 22:17

Criticisms of the shepherds of Israel

JER 23:1–23:2 See also JER 10:21, JER 22:21–22:22; EZK 34:1–34:10

title: 7787: shepherd, God the
content: See 1220: God, as shepherd

content: See 2330: Jesus Christ, as shepherd

title: 7789: shepherd, as church leader
content:
Church leaders are likened to shepherds, on account of their pastoral responsibilities and tasks.

Jesus Christ, the archetypal shepherd

1PE 5:4

Appointment of shepherds

EPH 4:11 See also MRK 3:14; ACT 14:23; 1CO 12:28

Shepherd responsibilities

Feeding the flock

JHN 21:15 See also MRK 6:34; JHN 21:17; 1CO 3:2; HEB 5:12–5:14; JUD 1:12

Caring for the flock

JHN 21:16 See also MAT 25:36; ACT 20:28; PHP 1:1; 1TI 3:1–3:2; 2TI 1:17; TIT 1:7; JAS 1:27; 1PE 5:2

Protecting, from false teachers

Leading, not exploiting

1PE 5:3

Being accountable

See also HEB 13:17

Risking death

title: 7793: teachers
content:
Those who have responsibility for instructing others, especially in relation to matters of faith and life: in the home, parents; in Israel, priests and teachers of the law and in the NT church, apostles, prophets, pastors and teachers. At a less formal level Christians should seek to teach one another. God is the supreme teacher of his people.

God is the supreme teacher

HEB 8:10–8:11 See also JER 31:33–31:34; DEU 8:3; PSA 32:8; JHN 14:26; 1TH 4:9; TIT 2:11–2:12

Parents as teachers

EPH 6:4 See also DEU 4:9–4:10, DEU 6:4–6:9, DEU 11:18–11:19; PSA 78:5–78:6; 2TI 3:14–3:15

Priests as Israel's teachers

MAL 2:4–2:7 See also LEV 10:11; 2KI 12:2; 2CH 35:3; NEH 8:7–8:8; EZK 44:23

The apostles and their helpers as teachers

The apostles generally

ACT 5:42 See also MAT 28:19–28:20; ACT 4:2, ACT 4:18, ACT 5:21, ACT 5:28

Paul

ACT 20:20 See also ACT 11:26, ACT 15:35, ACT 18:11; 1CO 4:17, 1CO 14:19; 1TH 4:1–4:2; 1TI 2:7; 2TI 1:11

Timothy

1TI 4:6–4:16; ; 2TI 2:23–2:25

The gift of teachers in the church

EPH 4:11 See also ACT 13:1; 1CO 12:28–12:29, 1CO 14:29–14:34; GAL 6:6; 1TI 2:12; 2TI 2:2

Elders as teachers

TIT 1:9 See also 1TI 3:2, 1TI 5:17

Christians as teachers of one another

COL 3:16 See also ROM 15:14; 1CO 14:26

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 7796: teaching
content: The act of instructing someone in matters of faith and morals, especially in the home or church. Teaching was a major aspect of the ministry of Jesus Christ.

title: 7797: teaching
content:
The apostles' teaching formed the basis of the instruction given to the first Christians. Parts of the NT draw a distinction between preaching and teaching, seeing the former as a means of converting individuals, and the latter as a means of instructing them after conversion.

The importance of teaching

ECC 12:11–12:12 “goads” prod the sluggish to action, while “embedded nails” furnish a kind of mental anchorage. “Shepherd” is almost certainly a reference to God himself. See also PRO 1:8–1:9, PRO 3:1–3:2, PRO 4:1–4:4, PRO 6:20–6:23; MAT 5:19; 1CO 14:6; 2TI 4:2–4:3

Teaching given by parents

PRO 22:6 See also DEU 6:6–6:9, DEU 11:18–11:19; EPH 6:1–6:4

The apostles' teaching

ACT 2:42; TIT 1:9 See also MAT 28:19–28:20; ROM 6:17, ROM 16:17; 1TH 4:8; 2TH 2:15; 1TI 1:10–1:11, 1TI 4:6, 1TI 6:3; 2TI 1:13–1:14, 2TI 4:3; TIT 2:1

The gift of teaching in the church

ROM 12:7 See also 1TI 4:13–4:14

Ways of teaching believers

Through example

1TH 1:5–1:6 See also 1CO 4:17, 1CO 11:1; PHP 4:9; 1TH 2:14; 2TI 3:10; TIT 2:3–2:7

Through dialogue with the teacher

ACT 20:7, ACT 19:8–19:9

Through explanation of ceremonies

EXO 12:26–12:27, EXO 13:14–13:16

Through proverbs

PRO 1:1–1:6, PRO 1:20–1:28

Through the law

DEU 6:6–6:9, DEU 11:18–11:19, DEU 27:1–27:26; ; PSA 78:5–78:8

Through mutual edification

COL 3:16 See also ROM 15:14; 1TH 5:11; HEB 5:12

Through the instruction of different groups within the church

EPH 5:22–6:9; ; COL 3:18–4:1; ; TIT 2:1–2:10; ; 1PE 2:18–3:7, 1PE 5:1–5:5

The distinction between teaching and preaching

ACT 15:35 See also MAT 4:23, MAT 9:35, MAT 11:1; LUK 20:1; ACT 4:2, ACT 5:42, ACT 28:31

Examples of major themes taught in the NT church

Righteousness from God through faith in Jesus Christ

ROM 3:21–3:22 See also GAL 2:20–2:21

Freedom in Christ from the demands of the law

GAL 5:1–5:3

The humility of Jesus Christ

PHP 2:5–2:8 See also HEB 13:12–13:13

The supremacy of Jesus Christ

COL 1:18

The superiority of Jesus Christ

HEB 3:3 Hebrews also shows Jesus Christ to be superior to the angels, the high priest and the sacrifices.

Godly behaviour

EPH 4:22–4:24 See also ROM 12:1–12:2; 1TI 6:1–6:2
