title: 7903: baptism
content: A washing with water, which symbolises the cleansing of believers from the stain and dirt of sin through the grace of God. Jesus Christ submitted to baptism as an example to believers. Through the work of the Holy Spirit, baptism is linked with union with the risen Jesus Christ.

title: 7904: baptism, of Jesus Christ
content: See 2510: Jesus Christ, baptism of

title: 7905: baptism, with Holy Spirit
content: See 3242: Holy Spirit, baptism with

title: 7906: baptism, in the Gospels
content:
In the Gospels baptising is based on the symbolic practice in the OT and Judaism of cleansing with water.

Washing was the means of achieving ritual purity

EXO 30:19–30:20; ; MRK 7:3–7:4 The Greek word here translated “wash” is “baptizo”, from which the English “baptise” comes.

Sprinkling with water symbolises spiritual cleansing

EZK 36:25 See also ZEC 13:1; HEB 10:22

Being baptised by John was a confession that cleansing was needed

MAT 3:11 See also MAT 3:6; MRK 1:4–1:5 parallel LUK 3:3; ACT 19:1–19:4

John promised that the Messiah would cleanse with the baptism of the Holy Spirit

MAT 3:11 parallels MRK 1:8, LUK 3:16 See also ISA 44:3; EZK 36:25–36:26

Baptism involves both water and the Holy Spirit

JHN 3:5

The disciples of Jesus Christ baptised like John's

See also JHN 3:22–3:23, JHN 4:1

Jesus Christ was baptised by John

MAT 3:13–3:17 parallels MRK 1:9–1:11, LUK 3:21–3:22, JHN 1:32–1:33

title: 7907: baptism, practice of
content:
Baptism is associated with repenting of sin, believing the gospel message and becoming a member of Christ's body.

Baptism is ordained by Jesus Christ himself

MAT 28:19 See also ACT 9:17–9:18, ACT 16:14–16:15

Baptism is linked with repentance

ACT 2:38 See also HEB 6:1–6:2 The “baptisms” referred to here may imply teaching about Jewish rituals or the baptism offered by John the Baptist.

Baptism follows the decision to believe

ACT 2:41, ACT 18:8 See also ACT 8:12–8:13, ACT 16:31–16:33

Baptism in the name of God or Jesus Christ

Baptism in or into the name of Jesus Christ

ACT 19:5 See also ACT 2:38, ACT 8:16, ACT 10:48

Baptism in the name of the Trinity

MAT 28:18–28:20

Baptism was by immersion

ROM 6:4 The word “buried” implies immersion. See also ACT 8:38

Baptism is linked with the gift of the Holy Spirit

MRK 1:8; ACT 1:5 See also ACT 2:4, ACT 11:161CO 12:13 All the Christians at Corinth had been spiritually baptised into Christ and thus united with him.

Manifestations of the Holy Spirit may follow or precede water baptism

See also ACT 8:12–8:17, ACT 9:17–9:18, ACT 10:44–10:48

The person who baptises is of little importance

1CO 1:14 What matters is not who baptises but the Saviour in whose name it is done.

Baptism ``for the dead''

1CO 15:29 It is not certain if there was a ceremony of baptism “for the dead” in the NT church, nor whether, if it existed, Paul approved of it.

Passages which may imply infant baptism

The ``households'' (not merely individuals) may well have included children: ACT 16:15, ACT 16:33, ACT 18:8; ; 1CO 1:16; MRK 10:13–10:16; ; 1CO 7:14; ; COL 2:11–2:12 If baptism is here equated with circumcision, this suggests baptism is the sign of the new covenant.

Passages apparently negating infant baptism

ACT 2:38–2:41 infants cannot repent and believe; ; GAL 3:7 Under the new covenant it is faith in Jesus Christ, not an outward sign, that makes a person a child of God.

title: 7908: baptism, significance of
content:
The NT uses a variety of images to explain the meaning of baptism, such as dying and rising with Christ, sharing in his death and being cleansed from sin.

Baptism is a symbol of the death of Jesus Christ

LUK 12:50 Jesus Christ described his death as a baptism or a flood overwhelming him. See also PSA 42:7, PSA 69:1–69:2, PSA 88:7; MRK 10:38–10:39

Baptism is a symbol of the burial of Jesus Christ

ROM 6:3–6:4 Immersion in water symbolises how the old sinful life is buried with Christ. ROM 8:13; ; COL 3:5

Baptism is a symbol of being saved from the flood

1PE 3:21 The flood/baptism symbolises both judgment (the death of sinners and the death of Jesus Christ) and salvation (those in the ark and those in Christ passing safely through judgment). See also GEN 7:6–7:7

Baptism is the gospel equivalent of circumcision

COL 2:11–2:12 The “circumcision in Christ” may refer either to his death on the cross or to baptism. See also DEU 10:16

Baptism recalls the exodus

1CO 10:1–10:2 In and through the exodus experience Israel was united with and obedient to Moses, as Jesus Christ's people are to him. See also EXO 14:19–14:24

Baptism symbolises washing from sin

ACT 22:16 Baptism is an outward sign of cleansing from sin and from a defiled life and conscience. See also 1CO 6:11; TIT 3:5; 1PE 3:21

Baptism is a symbol of putting on Christ

GAL 3:27 Baptism is seen as putting on Christ, as one might put on a coat or garment, and thus be “clothed with Christ”.

Baptism as a symbol of unity

EPH 4:5 See also 1CO 12:13

title: 7912: collections
content:
The financial burden of God's work has always been shared amongst God's people. In the OT, this was mainly by compulsory tithes; in the NT by voluntary contributions.

The collection of compulsory tithes and other religious taxes

Tithes

LEV 27:30 See also NUM 18:21

Redemption money

EXO 30:12; ; NUM 3:46–3:47

Temple taxes

2CH 24:5; ; MAT 17:24

The collection of voluntary contributions

For the tabernacle

EXO 36:3 See also NUM 7:2–7:3, NUM 7:84–7:88

For the temple

1CH 29:1–29:9

For the rebuilding of the temple

EZR 1:4

For the teaching ministry of the church

GAL 6:6 See also LUK 8:1–8:3; PHP 4:15–4:16; 1TI 5:17–5:18

For the relief of the poor

ROM 15:26; 1CO 16:1 See also ACT 2:45, ACT 4:34–4:35, ACT 11:29–11:30

The aim of collections

To share burdens equally

2CO 8:13–8:15

To supply people's needs

2CO 9:12

To arouse thanksgiving

2CO 9:12–9:15

The right attitude to collections

Generosity

EXO 36:4–36:6; ; 2CO 8:11, 2CO 9:6

Cheerfulness

2CO 9:7

Discipline

1CO 16:2–16:4

Humility

MAT 6:2–6:4

A willing spirit

2CO 8:12

A sacrificial spirit

MRK 12:41–12:44 parallel LUK 21:1–21:4; ; 2CO 8:1–8:4

The faithful administration of collections

2KI 22:7; ; ACT 11:30; ; 2CO 8:19–8:21

title: 7915: confirmation
content:
The act of publicly affirming something or someone, usually with a view to declaring trustworthiness. God confirmed his covenants, oaths and promises to emphasise his intention to fulfil them and he confirms the gospel to demonstrate its truthfulness.

Confirmation of covenants

DEU 4:31 See also GEN 17:1–17:22; DEU 8:18; 2KI 23:3; 1CH 16:17; PSA 105:10; DAN 9:26–9:27

Confirmation of oaths

PSA 119:106 See also GEN 26:1–26:5

Confirmation of promises

ROM 15:8 See also DEU 29:12–29:13; 2CH 1:9; HEB 6:17

Confirmation of the gospel

MRK 16:20 See also ACT 14:3; 1CO 1:4–1:6; PHP 1:7; HEB 2:3

Confirmation of wisdom

JOB 28:25–28:28

Confirmation of vows

See also NUM 30:1–30:16 A vow made by a woman could be either confirmed or nullified by her father or husband.

Confirmation of a witness's testimony

DEU 19:15 Evidence accepted in court required confirmation by more than one witness. See also 1KI 1:11–1:14; MRK 14:56–14:59 The proper judicial procedure was not followed at the trial of Jesus Christ; ROM 9:1–9:4 Paul here invokes the Holy Spirit as a witness; HEB 6:16

Confirmation of letters

ACT 15:27 This constituted verification of the authenticity of a letter which could easily have been forged or tampered with. See also EST 9:29–9:32

Confirmation of office or position

1SA 11:15

title: 7918: excommunication
content:
The last but reversible step in church discipline, resulting in exclusion from the fellowship of believers. The grounds that Scripture suggests for excommunication include public sin and immorality.

Jesus Christ authorises the church to excommunicate a sinning brother

MAT 18:15–18:18 The binding of sinners is thought to refer to their exclusion from God's people, and loosing, to their restoration after repentance (compare Mt 16:19).

Paul exhorts the Corinthian church to excommunicate an immoral brother

1CO 5:1–5:6 By “hand this man over to Satan” (see also 1Ti 1:20) Paul means to expel him, by putting him out into the devil's territory, in the hope that being officially ostracised, he might repent and forsake his wicked way. Such discipline was necessary not only for the reclaiming of the sinner but also for the safety and purity of the church (verse 6). See also 1CO 5:11–5:13

Excommunicated offenders who repent are to be lovingly restored to fellowship

2CO 2:5–2:11 This passage probably refers to the offender mentioned in 1Corinthians chapter 5.

title: 7921: fellowship
content: Association based upon the sharing of something in common. Believers have fellowship with one another on the basis of their common fellowship with God, their participation in the blessings of the gospel and their common task of mission. True fellowship is demonstrated in concern for, and practical commitment to, one another.

title: 7922: fellowship, with God
content:
The relationship with God, disrupted by sin yet established through Jesus Christ, which provides the only proper basis for true human fellowship. God's desire for fellowship with humanity is made known through his calling of a people to be his own and to reflect his holiness and love.

God's fellowship with his people is shown by his presence

God's presence with Israel

LEV 26:12 See also EXO 33:14; ISA 63:9; HAG 1:13

God's presence in the tabernacle

EXO 25:8 See also EXO 29:45–29:46, EXO 40:34–40:36; LEV 26:11; DEU 12:11

God's presence in the temple

1KI 6:12–6:13 See also 1KI 8:29 parallel 2CH 6:20; 2CH 7:1–7:2; ISA 6:1

God's presence in the new Jerusalem

ZEC 2:10–2:13 See also EZK 37:26–37:28, EZK 43:4–43:7, EZK 48:35; REV 21:3

The church's fellowship with God

Fellowship with the Father, Son and Holy Spirit

JHN 14:23 See also JHN 14:7, JHN 14:16–14:17 fellowship with the Spirit

Fellowship is made possible through Jesus Christ

EPH 2:18–2:19 See also ROM 5:10; 2CO 5:18–5:19; COL 1:20–1:22; HEB 10:19–10:22

Fellowship with Jesus Christ

1CO 1:9 See also MAT 28:20 Jesus Christ's presence with his disciples; JHN 15:4–15:5 abiding in Christ; ROM 6:4–6:5 united with Christ in his death and resurrection; 1CO 10:15–10:16 communion with Christ at the Lord's Supper; PHP 3:10 sharing in Christ's sufferings

Fellowship with God is inseparable from fellowship with one another

1JN 1:3 See also MAT 18:20; MRK 9:37; JHN 17:21; 2CO 13:11

The demands of fellowship with God

Holiness

LEV 20:26; 2CO 6:14–6:18 See also EXO 34:12–34:14; EZR 6:21; 1CO 5:11; EPH 5:8–5:11; JAS 4:4

Obedience to God's will

1JN 3:24 See also ISA 57:15; MAT 12:49–12:50 parallels MRK 3:34–3:35, LUK 8:21; JHN 14:21

Sin separates people from fellowship with God

ISA 59:2; 1JN 1:5–1:6 See also GEN 3:8; EZK 39:23

Examples of fellowship with God

GEN 5:22 Enoch; ; GEN 6:9 Noah; ; 2CH 20:7 Abraham; EXO 33:11; ; NUM 12:3; JOS 1:9 Joshua; ; MAL 2:6 Levi

resource.label: 8604: prayer, as a response to God
9414: heaven, as community of the redeemed

title: 7923: fellowship, in the gospel
content:
A mutual participation in the blessings of God's grace. Believers are united with one another on the basis of their common reception of the benefits of salvation.

Fellowship and the community of God's people

God calls out a community of people, for fellowship with himself

DEU 7:6 See also EXO 19:5–19:6; 1PE 2:5, 1PE 2:9–2:11

God will bless a people united in fellowship

MAT 18:19–18:20; ; 2CH 7:14; ; JER 31:23–31:25; ; 1CO 11:29–11:34 Wrong relationships between God's people hinder the flow of his blessing.

God will restore fellowship with his scattered people

MIC 2:12 See also ISA 11:12–11:13; JER 3:18, JER 31:1, JER 50:4–50:5

Sharing in God's grace

Fellowship in a common blessing

PSA 106:4–106:5 See also NUM 10:32; JOS 22:19

Fellowship in a common salvation

JUD 1:3 See also 1CO 9:23 sharing in the blessings of the gospel; PHP 1:7 sharing in God's grace; TIT 1:4 sharing a common faith; HEB 3:1 sharing a common calling; 1PE 5:1 sharing a common hope; 2PE 1:4 sharing the divine nature; 1JN 1:7 sharing in sanctification

Fellowship in a common inheritance

COL 1:12 See also ROM 8:17; 1PE 3:7

Fellowship in God's family

HEB 2:11 See also MAL 2:10; MAT 6:9 parallel LUK 11:2 the family prayer in which believers together address God as Father; MAT 12:49–12:50 parallels MRK 3:34–3:35, LUK 8:21; MAT 23:8–23:9

Fellowship between Jew and Gentile

EPH 3:6 See also ROM 11:17, ROM 15:27; EPH 2:16–2:18

Fellowship in holding a common truth

2TH 2:15 See also PSA 119:63; 1CO 11:2, 1CO 15:2–15:3; 2TI 2:2, 2TI 3:14; TIT 2:15

Fellowship in union with Christ

1CO 10:16–10:17; EPH 2:19–2:22 See also ROM 12:5; 1CO 12:12, 1CO 12:27; EPH 4:4–4:5; COL 1:15; 1PE 2:4–2:5

Fellowship through the Holy Spirit

1CO 12:13 See also EZK 36:27–36:28; 2CO 13:14; EPH 4:3; PHP 2:1

title: 7924: fellowship, in Christian service
content:
Partnership in a common enterprise. God's people are called to work together especially in the task of mission, to recognise one another's gifts and to give support to one another's ministries.

Fellowship in mission

Partnership in preaching the gospel

GAL 2:9 See also MRK 10:7; LUK 10:1–10:2; PHP 1:5

Supporting the work of others

ACT 14:26; PHP 4:14–4:16 The Philippians share in Paul's work through their giving. See also ACT 13:2–13:3, ACT 15:40; 2CO 11:9; 3JN 1:5–1:8

Standing together in adversity

HEB 10:32–10:34 See also 2CO 1:7; PHP 1:27–1:30, PHP 4:14; HEB 11:25

Fellowship between Paul and his co-workers

PHP 4:3 ACT 11:26–11:30, ACT 13:42–13:50, ACT 14:1–14:23, ACT 15:22–15:29; ROM 16:3 Priscilla and Aquila; ; ROM 16:9 Urbanus; ; ROM 16:21 Timothy; ; 2CO 8:23 Titus; ; PHP 2:25 Epaphroditus; ; PHM 1:1 Philemon; ; PHM 1:24 Mark, Aristarchus, Demas and Luke

In fellowship different gifts are combined for effective service

1CO 12:12 See also 1CO 12:4–12:6

Spiritual gifts are given to all to share

1CO 12:7 See also ROM 12:4–12:8; 1CO 12:14–12:20; 1PE 4:10

Recognising one another's gifts

1CO 12:21–12:26

Accepting one another's ministries

GAL 2:7–2:8 See also 1CO 12:27–12:31, 1CO 16:15–16:18; 2PE 3:15–3:16

Examples of sharing in different roles

NEH 4:16–4:22; 1CO 3:5–3:8 See also EXO 4:15–4:16, EXO 17:10–17:13; 1CO 12:8–12:11

Examples of working together in fellowship

ECC 4:9–4:12 See also DEU 3:18–3:20 the Reubenites and the Gadites join with the rest of Israel to conquer Canaan; JDG 20:11 all Israel unites against Gibeah; EZR 3:8–3:10 Those returning from exile work together to rebuild the temple; NEH 4:6 The Israelites work together to rebuild the walls of Jerusalem; LUK 5:7–5:10 Peter and Andrew, James and John are business partners.

title: 7925: fellowship, among believers
content:
The fellowship that believers share as a result of their common union with God through Jesus Christ is expressed in life together. It is evident in worship together, in a love for one another which reflects God's own love and in a practical commitment to one another which is demonstrated in concern for the weak and readiness to share with the poor and needy.

Sharing in the fellowship of God's love

1JN 4:10–4:12 See also JHN 13:34, JHN 15:12; EPH 5:1–5:2; 1JN 3:10

Sharing in the fellowship of a common devotional life

ACT 2:42

Worshipping together

PSA 55:14 See also PSA 42:4; 1CO 14:26; EPH 5:19; COL 3:16

Praying together

ACT 1:14 See also ACT 4:24, ACT 12:12; JAS 5:16

Breaking bread together

1CO 10:16–10:17; ; 2PE 2:13; ; JUD 1:12 Love feasts accompanied the Lord's Supper though these were open to abuse.

True fellowship means sharing with those in need

HEB 13:16 See also ACT 20:34–20:35; EPH 4:28

Showing hospitality

ROM 12:13 See also ISA 58:7; HEB 13:1–13:2; 1PE 4:9; 3JN 1:8

Sharing money and possessions

DEU 15:10–15:11; ACT 2:44–2:45 See also DEU 10:18–10:19 God's people are to reflect his concern for the needy in society; MAT 25:35–25:36; LUK 3:11; ACT 4:32–4:35; 2CO 8:13–8:15; 1TI 6:17–6:18; JAS 1:27, JAS 2:15–2:16

Examples of sharing with the needy

JOB 31:16–31:20 Job's compassion for the needy; ; ACT 6:1 the daily distribution to widows; ; ACT 9:36 Tabitha's concern for the poor; ACT 11:29–11:30; ; ROM 15:26; ; 2CO 8:3–8:4

Strengthening one another in fellowship together

Bearing with the weak

GAL 6:1–6:2 See also ISA 42:3; ROM 14:1, ROM 15:1; 1TH 5:14

Strengthening the weak

ISA 35:3–35:4 See also JOB 4:3–4:4

Encouraging one another

HEB 10:24–10:25 See also 1SA 23:16; ROM 1:12; 1TH 5:11; HEB 13:3

Putting the needs of others first

ROM 15:2 See also 1CO 10:24, 1CO 10:32–10:33

True fellowship means living in harmony

1PE 3:8 See also ROM 12:16; EPH 4:2–4:3; PHP 2:1–2:4; COL 3:12–3:14

Showing equal concern for all

ACT 10:34; ; 1CO 12:25; ; JAS 2:1–2:4

Examples of fellowship

NUM 10:31–10:32 Moses and Hobab; ; 1SA 18:3 David and Jonathan; ; 2KI 10:15–10:16 Jehu and Jehonadab

Failure to exhibit true fellowship

1SA 30:22 Troublemakers in David's army are unwilling to share the spoils; ; 1CO 1:11–1:12 factions within the church at Corinth; ; 1CO 11:17–11:22 Selfishness at love feasts humiliates the poor.

title: 7927: hymn
content:
Poetry sung to the praise of God and for the mutual encouragement of believers. Hymns and songs are not clearly distinguished in Scripture, but the NT contains examples of possible early Christian hymns.

Hymns are sung to the praise of God's name

PSA 40:3; ROM 15:8–15:11 Because of Jesus Christ the Gentiles are now called upon to join with God's people in praising God. See also DEU 32:43; 2SA 22:50; PSA 18:49, PSA 117:1; HEB 2:12

Christians are to sing hymns together for mutual encouragement

1CO 14:26 See also EPH 5:19; COL 3:16 “Psalms” refers to the OT psalms (see Lk 20:42; 24:44; Ac 1:20; 13:33) some of which may have been set to music by Christians. “Psalm” could also describe a song newly composed for Christian worship (see 1Co 14:26 where “hymn” translates Greek “psalmos”).

Examples of singing hymns

By Jesus Christ and his disciples

MAT 26:30 parallel MRK 14:26

By Paul and Silas in prison

ACT 16:25

Examples of hymns

The Benedictus: Zechariah's song of thanksgiving at the birth of his son, John the Baptist

See also LUK 1:68–1:79

The Magnificat: Mary's song of praise as the future mother of the Lord

LUK 1:46–1:55

The Nunc Dimittis: Simeon's response to seeing the child Jesus

LUK 2:29–2:32

Other examples of possible early Christian hymns

ROM 11:33–11:36; ; EPH 5:14; ; PHP 2:6–2:11; ; COL 1:15–1:20; ; 1TI 3:16

title: 7930: Lord's Day, the
content:
As well as keeping the Sabbath, the first Christians assembled together on the first day of the week to commemorate Jesus Christ's resurrection through the Lord's Supper. The Lord's Day quickly became the focal point of the Christian week, eventually assuming the characteristics of the Jewish Sabbath, namely worship and rest.

The disciples continued to observe the Sabbath

LUK 23:56 See also ACT 13:14, ACT 13:42, ACT 16:13, ACT 17:2, ACT 18:4

The Lord's Day commemorated Jesus Christ's resurrection

The resurrection took place on the first day of the week

MRK 16:9 See also MAT 28:1–28:7 parallels MRK 16:1–16:7, LUK 24:1–24:6, JHN 20:1

The disciples assembled together on the first day of the week

ACT 20:7 See also JHN 20:19–20:20, JHN 20:24–20:26

The Lord's Day took over the role of the Sabbath

1CO 16:2 See also REV 1:10

title: 7933: Lord's Supper
content:
The commemoration and remembrance of Jesus Christ's last supper, and all the benefits that result to believers. Other terms have been used subsequently by Christians, including “Communion” and “Eucharist”.

Terms for the Lord's Supper in the NT

ACT 2:42 “breaking of bread” could mean both eating together and also celebrating the Lord's Supper; ; 1CO 10:16 The Greek word “koinonia”, here translated “participation”, is traditionally translated “communion”: hence the term “Holy Communion”; ; 1CO 11:20 the Lord's Supper; ; 1CO 11:24 Underlying “had given thanks” is the Greek “eucharisteo”, from which comes the term “Eucharist”.

Jesus Christ's institution of the Lord's Supper

1CO 11:23–11:25 parallels MAT 26:26–26:28, MRK 14:22–14:24, LUK 22:17–22:20

Celebrating the Lord's Supper in the NT

As part of an ordinary meal

1CO 11:21 The church was not necessarily wrong to include the sacrament in an ordinary meal; this should have been conducted in a charitable way.

On the Lord's day

ACT 20:7 See also JHN 20:26

The fourfold formula for breaking bread: taking, giving thanks, breaking, giving

MAT 26:26 parallels MRK 14:22, LUK 22:19 See also LUK 24:30; JHN 6:11; 1CO 11:24

The sharing of the cup

1CO 11:25 parallels MAT 26:27–26:28, MRK 14:23–14:24, LUK 22:20

Themes connected with the Lord's Supper

The Passover

1CO 5:7–5:8 See also JHN 11:50 Caiaphas' words may have an unintended Passover significance; JHN 13:1, JHN 19:14, JHN 19:33, JHN 19:36; EXO 12:46; NUM 9:12

The new covenant

1CO 11:25 parallels MAT 26:27–26:28, MRK 14:23–14:24, LUK 22:20 The reference to the (new) covenant implies a personal relationship with God and sins forgiven.

Remembrance

1CO 11:24 parallel LUK 22:19

Thanksgiving, fellowship and unity

1CO 10:16 See also MAT 26:26–26:27 parallels MRK 14:22–14:23, LUK 22:19; 1CO 11:20–11:21

The Lord's return

1CO 11:26 See also MAT 26:29 parallels MRK 14:25, LUK 22:16; 1CO 16:22; REV 22:20

Separation from sin

1CO 10:21 Paul is referring to feasts in heathen temples. See also 1CO 11:27–11:32

A foretaste of heaven

MAT 26:29 parallel MRK 14:25

title: 7936: love feast
content:
The first Christians generally celebrated the Lord's Supper with the accompaniment of a meal, called the “agape” or “love feast”.

OT antecedents of the love feast

The Passover meal

See also EXO 12:3–12:11, EXO 12:15–12:16; MAT 26:17–26:18 parallels MRK 14:12–14:15, LUK 22:7–22:12; 1CO 11:23–11:25

Other festive occasions

DEU 12:7; ; NEH 8:10

The NT practice of the love feast

It was linked with the Lord's Supper

ACT 2:46 See also ACT 20:7; 1CO 11:17–11:34

The name ``love feasts''

JUD 1:12 See also 2PE 2:13; JHN 13:2, JHN 13:34 It was at the Last Supper that Jesus Christ gave his new commandment to his disciples to love one another as he had loved them.

The charitable distribution of food

ACT 6:1

Abuses of the love feast

Selfishness, indulgence and ostentation

1CO 11:20–11:22 See also 1CO 11:33–11:34

Licentiousness

2PE 2:13

Ungodly participants, especially false teachers

JUD 1:4, JUD 1:12 See also 2PE 2:1, 2PE 2:13

The heavenly love feast

Foretold by Jesus Christ

MAT 26:29 parallel MRK 14:25

The marriage supper of the Lamb

REV 19:9 See also ISA 25:6; MAT 22:2–22:14 The parable of the marriage supper indicates the prerequisites for attendance at the supper.

title: 7939: ministry
content: Service of a general, as well as a religious, nature. In its broadest sense ministry refers to service rendered to God or to people. Its more restricted sense refers to the official service of individuals, specially set aside by the church.

title: 7940: ministry, of Jesus Christ
content: See 2300: Jesus Christ, ministry and work of

title: 7941: ministry, of Holy Spirit
content: See 3200: Holy Spirit, ministry and work of

title: 7942: ministry, nature of
content:
All creatures owe their Creator a ministry of service to him and to humanity. The example and teaching of Jesus Christ illustrate this.

All human beings owe a ministry of service to God

The debt owed by the creature to the Creator

ROM 1:25

The service of the nations and their rulers

PSA 148:7–148:11 See also JER 3:17; JOL 3:1–3:2; REV 15:4

Examples of the service of rulers

EZR 1:1–1:2 Cyrus of Persia; JER 25:9, JER 27:6, JER 43:10; ROM 13:4, ROM 13:6; ; 1PE 2:13–2:14

The ministry of the people of God

In the OT

EXO 19:6 See also ISA 61:6

In the NT

1PE 2:5, 1PE 2:9 See also REV 1:6, REV 20:6 resurrected martyrs

The ministry of priests, kings and prophets in the OT

The priests

Aaron: EXO 28:35; ; NUM 18:2; DEU 17:12; 1CH 15:2; ; JER 33:22; EZR 2:63; ; HEB 9:6, HEB 13:10

The rulers

David: 2SA 3:18; ; PSA 78:70; ; LUK 1:69; ; ACT 4:25; HAG 2:23 Zerubbabel, governor of Judah

The prophets

Moses: NUM 12:7–12:8; ; JOS 12:6; ; 2CO 3:7; ; HEB 3:5; 1SA 2:18 the young Samuel; 2KI 9:36, 2KI 10:10; 1CH 25:1; ; ISA 20:3 Isaiah; ; ISA 42:1–42:4; ; JER 7:25, JER 25:4; ; 1PE 1:12; ; REV 10:7, REV 11:18

The ministry of those specially set aside by the church

1CO 12:28; ; 1TI 3:10 Deacons; ; TIT 1:5 Elders; ; 1PE 5:2 Overseers; ; EPH 4:11

Jesus Christ fulfils the OT understanding of ministry

His ministry as servant

MAT 12:18 See also ISA 42:1; MAT 20:28 parallel MRK 10:45; ACT 3:13, ACT 3:26, ACT 4:27, ACT 4:30

His ministry as shepherd, teacher and prophet

JHN 10:11, JHN 13:13; ACT 3:22

His ministry as priest

HEB 6:20 See also HEB 8:6

His ministry as king

JHN 18:37

NT ministry follows the pattern of Jesus Christ

In servanthood

MAT 20:26–20:28 parallels MRK 10:43–10:45, LUK 22:26–22:27 See also MAT 23:11 parallel MRK 10:43

In teaching

MAT 28:20

In shepherding

JHN 21:15–21:17

In priesthood by self-sacrifice and intercession in prayer

PHP 2:17; 1TI 2:1 See also 1PE 2:5

Jesus Christ's servants can expect the same response that he received

JHN 15:20

The ministry of angels

Angels are both ministers to God's people and fellow-servants with them: HEB 1:7, HEB 1:14; ; REV 19:10, REV 22:9

title: 7943: ministry, in the church
content:
The regular ministry of officially appointed or recognised ministers is a particular instance of the duty and call of all God's people.

The responsibilities of those in recognised ministry

As shepherds

ACT 20:28 See also 1PE 5:2

As watchmen

HEB 13:17 See also ROM 16:17; PHP 3:2

As teachers

1TI 3:2 See also 2TI 2:2, 2TI 2:24

As examples

1TI 4:16 See also ACT 20:28; 1PE 5:3

What believers owe to those with recognised ministries

Respect

1TH 5:12–5:13 See also PHP 2:29; 1TI 5:17

Obedience

HEB 13:17 See also 1CO 16:15–16:16

Support

1CO 9:14 Despite Jesus Christ's command, Paul did not claim his rights in this matter, but supported himself as a tentmaker. See also LUK 10:7; GAL 6:6; 1TI 5:18; DEU 25:4

Prayer

ROM 15:30 See also EPH 6:19; PHP 1:19; 1TH 5:25; HEB 13:18

Hospitality

PHP 2:29–2:30 See also GAL 4:14

Imitation

HEB 13:7

Recognised ministers should encourage the ministries of other believers

COL 4:17 See also ROM 12:6–12:8; 1CO 12:4–12:11 The Holy Spirit allocates gifts and enables ministries. No individual has all the gifts; ministry is shared and mutual; EPH 4:7, EPH 4:11–4:13

The motivation of ministry is mutual love

GAL 5:13 See also ROM 12:10, ROM 13:8; COL 3:12–3:14; 1TH 3:12; HEB 13:1; 1PE 1:22; 1JN 3:23

How love is expressed in mutual ministry

In humility

EPH 4:2 See also ROM 12:10, ROM 12:16; GAL 5:26; 1PE 5:5

In patience

1TH 5:14 See also JAS 5:9

In acceptance and forbearance

ROM 15:7 See also COL 3:13

In kindness, compassion and forgiveness

EPH 4:32 See also COL 3:13; 1TH 5:15

In burden-bearing

GAL 6:2

In encouragement

1TH 5:11 See also HEB 3:13, HEB 10:24–10:25

In teaching and admonition

COL 3:16 See also ROM 15:14; EPH 5:19; 1TH 5:14

In prayer and confession of sin

JAS 5:16

In hospitality

1PE 4:9

title: 7944: ministry, qualifications for
content:
God, who calls his people to minister, also equips his people. The chief qualifications are a response to God's call, faithfulness, godliness and Christlikeness.

God calls people to minister

Qualification is by call, not gifting or achievement

DEU 7:7–7:8 See also DEU 9:4–9:5

God calls those who the world regards as weak or foolish

1CO 1:27–1:29

Feelings of inadequacy to God's call are common

EXO 3:11 Moses; ; JDG 6:15 Gideon; ; 1SA 9:21 Saul; ; 1SA 18:18 David; ; 1KI 3:7 Solomon; ; ISA 6:5 Isaiah; ; JER 1:6 Jeremiah

Responding to God's call to minister

Readiness and availability

1SA 3:10; ; ISA 6:8

Faith, rather than natural talent or moral perfection, is required

HEB 11:1–11:2 See also GEN 27:19–27:24 Jacob was a deceiver; NUM 27:12–27:14 Moses and Aaron disobeyed God.2SA 11:4, 2SA 11:14–11:15 1KI 11:9–11:13 Solomon disobeyed God's command.

NT ministers are recognised by call rather than their achievement

The Twelve

MAT 10:1–10:4 parallels MRK 3:14–3:19, LUK 6:12–6:16 The Twelve, including Peter and Judas, failed Jesus Christ at critical times.

Paul

ACT 9:15, ACT 26:6; ; 2CO 4:7–4:12, 2CO 12:7 Though greatly gifted, Paul was kept humble by his sense of unworthiness, difficulties and disappointments and his “thorn in the flesh”; ; 1TI 1:16

Ministry in the NT is described as service

Serving God

ROM 1:9; ; JAS 1:1

Serving Jesus Christ

ROM 1:1; ; JUD 1:1; ; REV 1:1

Serving the gospel

EPH 3:7; ; COL 1:23

Serving the church

ROM 15:31, ROM 16:1; ; 1CO 16:15; ; 2CO 9:1; ; EPH 6:21; ; COL 1:7, COL 1:25

Ministry is described in terms of its source, content or nature

Its source

It is of the Spirit: 2CO 3:6, 2CO 3:8; 2CO 4:1 It is from God.

Its content

ACT 6:2–6:4 the word of God; ; 2CO 5:18 reconciliation

Its nature

Apostolic: ACT 1:25; ; GAL 2:8; ROM 15:16 priestly

Various ministries are equally linked by qualifications of character

ACT 1:21 the replacement for Judas; ; ACT 6:3 the Seven; 1TI 3:2–3:7; ; TIT 1:7–1:9; 1TI 3:8–3:13 deacons; ; 1TI 6:11 Timothy

The personal qualifications for ministry

Faithfulness

1TI 6:11–6:14 Timothy; ; 2TI 4:7 Paul's claim for his own ministry; COL 1:7, COL 4:7

Godliness

ACT 8:21 Simon was not right with God.; 1TI 6:11, 1TI 6:20–6:21

Christlikeness

ACT 1:21–1:22 The replacement apostle for Judas had to have been with Jesus Christ from the beginning.

title: 7948: mission
content: Specific actions that bear witness to the good news of what God has done for his people. Israel, Jesus Christ and the church all in their different ways bear witness to the saving acts of God in history. Christian mission is empowered by the Holy Spirit.

title: 7949: mission, of Israel
content:
God chose Israel to be his own people, and bring the good news of his salvation to the world. God's mission through Israel is fulfilled in the mission of his servant and continues through the mission of the church.

Israel as a channel of God's blessing

Blessings for all people through Abraham

GEN 18:18 See also GEN 12:2–12:3, GEN 22:18, GEN 26:4 God's promise repeated to Isaac; GEN 28:13–28:14 God's promise repeated to Jacob; ACT 3:25; GAL 3:8

Israel's call to be a nation of priests

EXO 19:5–19:6 See also ISA 61:6

God's desire to display his glory

God's glory is to fill the earth

HAB 2:14 See also NUM 14:21; PSA 72:18–72:19; ISA 6:3

God's people display his glory

ISA 60:1–60:3 See also ISA 46:13, ISA 49:3, ISA 55:5

God's people declare his glory among the nations

PSA 96:3 See also PSA 57:9, PSA 96:10, PSA 105:1–105:2 parallel 1CH 16:8–16:9; PSA 145:11–145:12

The nations will be drawn to God's people

In recognition of God's presence among his people

ZEC 8:20–8:23 See also 1KI 10:1 parallel 2CH 9:1; ISA 45:14; JER 16:19; EZK 37:27–37:28, EZK 39:7

To Jerusalem as the centre of universal worship

ISA 2:2–2:4 parallel MIC 4:1–4:3 See also ISA 11:9, ISA 25:6–25:8, ISA 27:13, ISA 56:6–56:8; ZEC 14:16

God made known through his mighty acts

God's deliverance of Israel

EXO 7:5 See also PSA 98:1–98:3, PSA 102:15–102:16; ISA 49:26; EZK 38:23

God's action on behalf of Israel

EXO 34:10 See also EXO 8:19; JOS 4:24; 1KI 8:41–8:43 parallel 2CH 6:32–6:33; PSA 67:1–67:4, PSA 126:2

God's renewal of Israel

EZK 36:23 See also EZK 20:40–20:41, EZK 28:24–28:25, EZK 38:16

Israel as a witness to other nations

God's people sent to other nations

ISA 66:19 See also 1KI 17:9; JON 1:2, JON 3:2; MAT 23:15

Examples of God's people bearing witness to him

GEN 41:16 Joseph; ; EXO 7:16–7:17 Moses and Aaron; ; 2KI 5:3 Naaman's servant girl; ; DAN 2:44–2:47 Daniel; ; DAN 3:16–3:18 Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego

Israel's failure in mission

ISA 26:18 See also EZK 36:20–36:21

The fulfilment of God's mission through Israel

The mission of God's Messiah

ISA 11:10 See also ROM 15:12; PSA 72:17 God's promise to Abraham is fulfilled through the Messianic king; GAL 3:16

The mission of God's servant

ISA 49:6 See also ISA 42:1–42:6, ISA 52:15

The mission of the church

1PE 2:9 See also ROM 15:16

title: 7950: mission, of Jesus Christ
content:
Jesus Christ came to reveal God, to announce the coming of God's kingdom and to redeem a fallen humanity through his death on the cross. Though he came first to the Jews, the scope of Christ's mission includes the whole human race and continues through the Spirit-empowered witness of the church.

Jesus Christ's mission originated with God

It was purposed by God

1PE 1:18–1:20 See also ACT 2:23; EPH 1:4–1:5

The Father sent the Son

GAL 4:4–4:5 See also JHN 5:37–5:38, JHN 7:29, JHN 8:42, JHN 10:36, JHN 17:8; 1JN 4:14

Jesus Christ came in willing obedience to the Father

JHN 6:38; ; PHP 2:5–2:8

Jesus Christ's mission was motivated by God's love

JHN 3:16 See also ROM 5:8; 1JN 4:10

Jesus Christ's mission was to make God known

He reveals God in his own person

JHN 1:18 See also JHN 14:9, JHN 17:26; COL 1:15; HEB 1:1–1:3

He reveals God through his teaching

JHN 18:37 See also MAT 7:28–7:29; JHN 3:11–3:13

Jesus Christ's mission was to announce God's kingdom

LUK 4:43 See also MAT 4:17; MRK 1:14–1:15; MAT 12:28 parallel LUK 11:20 Jesus Christ's miracles reveal the presence of God's kingdom.

Jesus Christ's mission was to redeem humanity

He came to seek and save the lost

LUK 19:10 See also LUK 15:1–15:10

He came to save from sin

MAT 1:21 See also ROM 3:25–3:26; 1TI 1:15

He came to break the devil's power

1JN 3:8 See also GEN 3:15; JHN 12:31; HEB 2:14–2:15

He came to bring eternal life

JHN 10:10 See also JHN 6:38–6:40, JHN 17:2–17:3

He came to give access to God

EPH 2:18, EPH 3:12; ; HEB 10:19–10:20

He came to restore a fallen humanity

ROM 5:17–5:18; ; 1CO 15:21–15:22

Jesus Christ's mission was to establish the church

MAT 16:18 See also EPH 2:17–2:19, EPH 5:25–5:27; 1PE 2:4–2:5, 1PE 2:9–2:10

The scope of Jesus Christ's mission

Jesus Christ came first to Israel

MAT 10:5–10:6, MAT 15:24; ; JHN 1:11

Jesus Christ's mission extends to all peoples

ISA 49:6; ; MAT 8:11; ; JHN 10:16; ; MAT 8:10 parallel LUK 7:9 Jesus Christ commends the faith of a Roman centurion; ; MAT 15:28 parallel MRK 7:29 Jesus Christ grants the request of a Canaanite woman; ; ACT 1:8 Jesus Christ sends his disciples out into the whole world; ; ACT 26:17–26:18 Paul's commission to go to the Gentiles is received directly from Jesus Christ.

Jesus Christ's mission made the cross necessary

MAT 20:28 parallel MRK 10:45 See also MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31, LUK 9:22; LUK 12:50; COL 2:14–2:15; HEB 12:2

Jesus Christ's mission will be completed at his return

1JN 3:2 See also MAT 24:31 parallel MRK 13:27; COL 3:4; 1TH 4:16–4:17

Jesus Christ's mission continues through the church

JHN 20:21–20:22 See also MAT 28:18–28:20; JHN 15:26–15:27

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 7951: mission, of Holy Spirit
content: See 3257: Holy Spirit, gift of

title: 7952: mission, and Holy Spirit
content: See 3212: Holy Spirit, and mission

title: 7953: mission, of the church
content:
The continuation of Jesus Christ's mission through his followers. Believers are empowered by the Holy Spirit and sent out by Christ to bear witness to him and to preach, heal, teach, baptise and make disciples of all peoples.

The power and authority of the church's mission

Believers are sent out by Jesus Christ

JHN 15:16 See also MAT 9:37–9:38; LUK 10:1–10:3; JHN 4:36–4:38

Believers are given authority by Jesus Christ

LUK 9:1 See also MAT 10:1, MAT 28:18; MRK 6:7, MRK 16:17–16:18; LUK 10:17–10:19

Believers continue Jesus Christ's mission

JHN 20:21 See also JHN 17:18

Believers are empowered by the Holy Spirit

ACT 1:8 See also LUK 24:49; JHN 20:22; ACT 4:31; HEB 2:4

The task of the church in mission

Making disciples

MAT 28:19–28:20 See also ACT 2:41–2:42, ACT 14:15, ACT 16:14–16:15, ACT 18:8; ROM 10:14–10:15; 1JN 1:2–1:3

Preaching and healing

LUK 9:2 See also MAT 10:7–10:8; MRK 16:20; LUK 9:6

Proclaiming the gospel

ACT 20:24 See also ACT 8:40; ROM 1:9, ROM 15:20; 2TI 1:11

Bearing witness to Jesus Christ

ACT 5:30–5:32 See also LUK 24:48; JHN 15:26–15:27; ACT 4:20

Bringing honour to God

EPH 3:10–3:11 See also JHN 15:8; 1PE 2:12

The universal scope of the church's mission

LUK 24:47 See also MAT 24:14 parallel MRK 13:10; MRK 16:15

The church reaching out in mission

To the Jews

MAT 10:5–10:6 The disiples' mission began with the Jews. See also MAT 10:9–10:15 parallels MRK 6:8–6:11, LUK 9:3–9:5, LUK 10:4–10:12; ACT 11:19 The scattered believers at first preached only to Jews.

To the Samaritans

ACT 8:4–8:8 See also ACT 8:14–8:17, ACT 8:25

To the Gentiles

Paul as the apostle to the Gentiles: ACT 9:15; ; ROM 11:13, ROM 15:16; ACT 10:34–10:35 Peter preaches to Cornelius and his family; ; ACT 11:20–11:21 Scattered believers preach to the Gentiles; ; ACT 13:1–13:3 Paul begins his first missionary journey (with Barnabas); ; ACT 15:40–15:41 Paul begins his second missionary journey (with Silas); ; ACT 16:9–16:10 Paul is called to preach the gospel in Macedonia; ; ACT 18:23 Paul's third missionary journey; ; ACT 28:31 Paul preaches the gospel in Rome.

Missions undertaken by church officials

ACT 11:22–11:23 Barnabas is sent to Antioch to strengthen the new church.; ACT 11:30, ACT 12:25; ACT 15:22–15:23 Judas and Silas are sent to Antioch with a letter from the apostles and elders.

title: 7957: sacraments
content:
The sacraments of baptism and the Lord's Supper were instituted by Jesus Christ as signs and seals of the covenant. They explain the basis of this covenant and apply its benefits to believers. They replace circumcision and the Passover which were the sacraments of the old covenant.

The sacraments as signs of the new covenant

Baptism

MAT 28:19 Baptism is one sign of being a disciple of Jesus Christ. See also ACT 2:38, ACT 2:41

The Lord's Supper

1CO 11:23–11:25 parallels MAT 26:26–26:28, MRK 14:22–14:24, LUK 22:17–22:20 The new covenant was sealed by the blood of Jesus Christ.

The sacraments as a participation in the body of Christ

Baptism

GAL 3:27 In the NT baptism immediately follows conversion and testifies to the individual's union with Christ. See also ROM 6:3

The Lord's Supper

1CO 10:16–10:17 The “body of Christ” is a term which goes beyond Jesus Christ to the church. Those who partake of the bread in this way are declaring their membership of the church. See also MAT 26:26 parallels MRK 14:22, LUK 22:19, 1CO 11:24; JHN 6:32–6:35, JHN 6:48 Jesus Christ likens himself to bread as he is the source of spiritual life and nourishment; JHN 6:50–6:58 The believer who eats the bread of the Lord's Supper is renewing his or her dependence on Jesus Christ for spiritual life.

The sacraments as a participation in the death of Christ

Baptism

ROM 6:3–6:4 Through baptism individuals declare that they have applied to themselves the death of Jesus Christ which atones for sin. See also ACT 2:38, ACT 22:16 Baptism does not accomplish the cleansing from sin but it is the outward sign that this has taken place by faith; ROM 6:5–6:7; COL 2:12; TIT 3:5; 1PE 3:21

The Lord's Supper

1CO 11:26 In taking the emblems of Jesus Christ's broken body and shed blood individuals perpetually apply the merits of Christ's death (i.e., forgiveness of sins) to themselves. See also MAT 26:27–26:28 parallels MRK 14:23–14:24, LUK 22:20, 1CO 11:25; JHN 6:53–6:56; 1CO 10:16

The sacraments of the old covenant

Circumcision

ACT 7:8 Circumcision was the initiation rite under the old covenant. See also GEN 17:10–17:14; ROM 2:28–2:29 Circumcision was never meant as merely an outward rite. To be effective it required a right attitude of heart; ROM 4:10–4:11 Circumcision was the seal of Abraham's righteousness imputed to him by his faith; COL 2:11–2:12 Circumcision has now been superseded by baptism for the Christian as the rite of initiation.

The Passover

EXO 12:25–12:27 The Passover meal was instituted as a regular commemoration of Israel's redemption from slavery in Egypt. See also LUK 22:15–22:16 The death of Jesus Christ is closely associated with the Passover by its timing and meaning. The Lord's Supper has superseded the Passover as the corporate meal commemorating the act of redemption; 1CO 5:7

title: 7960: singing
content:
The musical voicing of praise to God. On account of God's majesty and great acts of salvation singing was a regular part of the life of Israel and of the early church. In Israel musicians and singers were set aside solely for this purpose.

The call to sing praise to God

PSA 33:1–33:3, PSA 96:1–96:2 See also PSA 5:11, PSA 9:11, PSA 30:4, PSA 47:6, PSA 68:4, PSA 68:32, PSA 81:1–81:2, PSA 95:1, PSA 98:1, PSA 98:4–98:5, PSA 98:8–98:9, PSA 117:1, PSA 147:7, PSA 149:1; ISA 44:23, ISA 49:13, ISA 52:9; ROM 15:11

The resolve to sing praise to God

PSA 104:33 See also PSA 7:17, PSA 9:2, PSA 13:6, PSA 57:8–57:9, PSA 59:16–59:17, PSA 89:1, PSA 101:1, PSA 147:1

Singing in response to God's deliverance from enemies and sin

ISA 35:10 See also PSA 18:47–18:49, PSA 71:22–71:24; ISA 54:1, ISA 55:12; REV 5:11–5:14, REV 15:3–15:4

God sings of his delight and love for his people

ZEP 3:17

Singing in Israel

Singing accompanied major events

See also 1CH 15:27–15:28 the bringing of the ark to Jerusalem; 2CH 5:12–5:13 the dedication of the temple; 2CH 20:21–20:22 a march into battle; 2CH 23:11–23:13 a coronation; 2CH 29:27–29:28 the purification of the temple2CH 30:21, 2CH 35:15 NEH 12:27–12:29 the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem

Part of day-to-day worship

2CH 23:18; ; NEH 11:23

Accompanied by instruments

1CH 15:16 See also 1KI 10:12 parallel 2CH 9:11; PSA 137:1–137:4

Musicians and singers appointed to lead Israel's singing

1CH 9:33, 1CH 15:22 See also 1CH 6:31–6:32, 1CH 25:6–25:7; EZR 2:41 parallel NEH 7:44; EZR 2:65; NEH 11:22, NEH 12:46

Singing in the NT

EPH 5:19–5:20; JAS 5:13 See also MAT 26:30 parallel MRK 14:26; ACT 16:25; 1CO 14:15; COL 3:16

Singing is not always appropriate

PRO 25:20 See also ECC 7:5AMO 5:23 God detests the songs of Israel that are not accompanied by obedience and justice. See also ISA 24:14–24:17, ISA 25:5; EZK 26:13; AMO 6:5–6:7, AMO 8:3, AMO 8:10

title: 7963: song
content:
In Scripture, songs express praise, thanksgiving, joy, victory and love. They also express sadness and derision and can be used to teach and warn. In heaven believers will sing a new song to God.

Different kinds of song

Songs of praise and thanksgiving

2CH 5:13; PSA 69:30 See also NEH 12:8, NEH 12:46; PSA 28:7, PSA 95:2; JER 30:18–30:19; JON 2:9; JAS 5:13

Songs of joy and celebration

PSA 98:4 See also 2SA 6:5 parallel 1CH 13:8; 1CH 15:16; PSA 100:2, PSA 107:22, PSA 126:2, PSA 126:6; ISA 52:9, ISA 55:12

Songs of victory and deliverance

EXO 15:1–15:18 The song celebrating God's spectacular victory over Pharaoh and his army (compare with Rev 15:3). See also JDG 5:1–5:3; 1SA 18:6–18:7; 2SA 22:1–22:4; PSA 18:2, PSA 32:7, PSA 42:8, PSA 118:15; ISA 26:1, ISA 44:23, ISA 49:13

Psalms and hymns

COL 3:16 “psalms, hymns and spiritual songs” are not clearly distinguished from each other in Scripture. See also 1CH 16:7; PSA 40:3, PSA 47:7; MAT 26:30 parallel MRK 14:26; 1CO 14:26; ACT 16:25; EPH 5:19

Songs of ascent

PSA 122:0 Psalms 120–134, the so-called “Songs of Ascent”, are thought to have been sung by pilgrims on their way to Jerusalem.

Songs of love

PSA 45:0 The title to this psalm describes it as “a wedding song”; ; SNG 1:1; ; ISA 5:1; ; EZK 33:32

Laments

2SA 1:17–1:19 See also 2SA 3:33–3:34; 2CH 35:25; PSA 102:1–102:2; JER 7:29, JER 9:10, JER 9:20; LAM 2:5 The book of Lamentations is a series of laments after the fall of Jerusalem; EZK 19:1, EZK 19:14, EZK 26:17–26:18, EZK 27:2, EZK 27:32, EZK 28:12, EZK 32:16; AMO 5:1

Songs of derision

JOB 30:9; ; PSA 69:12; ; LAM 3:14, LAM 3:63; ; MIC 2:4

God is described as the song of believers

PSA 118:14 See also EXO 15:2; ISA 12:2

Songs can teach and warn

DEU 32:44–32:46 See also DEU 31:19, DEU 31:22, DEU 31:30; PSA 119:54; EPH 5:19

A new song

Singing a new song to God expresses a renewed desire to praise him

PSA 40:1–40:3 See also PSA 33:3, PSA 98:1, PSA 144:9, PSA 149:1; ISA 42:10

In heaven God's people will sing a new song

REV 5:9–5:10 See also REV 14:3

title: 7966: spiritual gifts
content: Although all believers have received the gift of the Holy Spirit, Scripture points to God giving individuals certain special gifts of a spiritual nature for the fulfilling of specific tasks.

title: 7967: spiritual gifts, and responsibility
content:
A spiritual gift may be intended to equip its recipient for a specific function or appointment. Those who are equipped in this way need the enabling power of God's spirit to carry out their appointed tasks.

OT examples of God's gifts or appointments

God appoints prophets

JER 1:5

God appoints Israel's kings

1SA 15:11 See also 2SA 7:8, 2SA 12:7

God appoints the Persian king Cyrus

ISA 45:1 See also ISA 41:2

God empowers leaders and kings with his Spirit

NUM 27:18 See also JDG 3:10, JDG 6:34, JDG 11:29, JDG 13:25, JDG 14:6, JDG 14:19, JDG 15:14; 1SA 10:6, 1SA 10:10, 1SA 11:6, 1SA 16:13–16:14

The Spirit is given to the servant of the LORD

ISA 42:1 See also ISA 61:1

Jesus Christ is endowed with the Holy Spirit

MAT 3:16–3:17 parallels MRK 1:10–1:11, LUK 3:21–3:22 See also JHN 1:32–1:33; ACT 10:38

The apostles are endowed with the Holy Spirit

ACT 1:8

Spiritual gifts given to the church

EPH 4:11 See also 1CO 12:28

Many functions within the church yet unity is preserved

EPH 4:4–4:7 See also 1CO 12:4–12:11

All Christians are appointed to build up the church

EPH 4:15–4:16 See also 1CO 12:12

The experience of spiritual gifts

Associated with the laying on of hands

2TI 1:6–1:7 See also NUM 8:10, NUM 27:18; DEU 34:9; ACT 6:6; 1TI 4:14, 1TI 5:22

Associated with prayer and fasting

ACT 14:23 See also ACT 13:3

Spiritual gifts must not to be neglected

1TI 4:14 See also LUK 19:11–19:26

resource.label: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 7968: spiritual gifts, nature of
content:
In both the OT and the NT God graciously pours out gifts on his people. They are to be welcomed and used for the good of all.

OT examples of God giving special gifts

God's gracious provision from above

JOL 2:23–2:24 This gracious provision of material needs from above can be seen as foreshadowing God's bestowal of spiritual gifts. See also EXO 16:4, EXO 16:8, EXO 16:13–16:14; DEU 11:14; 1KI 17:6; JOB 5:10; ISA 55:10

Distribution of land to tribes

JOS 13:6–13:7 The Hebrew word for “give” often includes the sense of “apportion” or “assign”. See also EZK 47:21

The future promise of spiritual gifts

JOL 2:28–2:29 See also ACT 2:17–2:18

In his earthly ministry Jesus Christ offers supernatural gifts

MAT 11:28 See also MAT 16:19; LUK 10:19; JHN 4:14, JHN 6:51

Spiritual gifts linked with grace

ROM 12:6 See also MAT 10:8; 1CO 4:7

Specific reference to spiritual gifts

1CO 12:1 See also ROM 1:11; 1CO 14:1, 1CO 14:12, 1CO 14:37

Diverse gifts, one giver

1CO 12:4–12:6 See also ROM 12:6–12:8; 1CO 7:7, 1CO 7:17, 1CO 12:8–12:11, 1CO 12:27; EPH 4:11; HEB 2:4; 1PE 4:10–4:11

The purpose of spiritual gifts is to build up the church

1CO 14:12 See also 1CO 12:7, 1CO 14:2–14:5, 1CO 14:17–14:19, 1CO 14:26, 1CO 14:31; EPH 4:16

The body of Christ benefits from these varied gifts

1CO 12:12 See also 1CO 12:14–12:31; ROM 12:4–12:6

Encouragement to aspire to the greater spiritual gifts

1CO 12:31, 1CO 14:1

The importance of love in exercising spiritual gifts

1CO 13:1 See also ROM 12:5–12:9; 1CO 14:1

Warnings about spiritual gifts

1CO 14:39 See also 1CO 14:37; 1TH 5:19–5:20

title: 7972: tongues, gift of
content:
The divine enabling of a believer to use a language, unknown to the speaker. The gift of tongues may be used to praise God or to utter a message from God that, supplemented by the gift of interpretation of tongues, edifies other believers. Note that “tongues” can simply mean “other languages” at times.

Tongues is a gift of the Holy Spirit

It is given to individual believers

ACT 2:4 See also 1CO 12:11, 1CO 12:30 it is not universal among Christians

It was foretold in the OT

1CO 14:21; ; ISA 28:11

It is a divine gift in response to faith

MRK 16:17

It may be given through the laying on of hands

ACT 19:6

It is a sign of the presence of the Holy Spirit

ACT 10:44–10:46

Tongues in relation to other gifts

The need for love

1CO 13:1

The need for prophecy

1CO 14:4–14:6

The need for intelligibility

See also 1CO 14:9, 1CO 14:13, 1CO 14:16–14:19, 1CO 14:23

The need for interpretation

1CO 12:8–12:10, 1CO 14:12–14:13

The transience of tongues

1CO 13:8

The use of tongues within the church

In prayer and praise

See also ROM 8:26; 1CO 14:14–14:15, 1CO 14:26, 1CO 14:28; EPH 6:18; JUD 1:20

For the benefit of believers

1CO 12:7–12:10 See also 1CO 14:5, 1CO 14:16–14:17, 1CO 14:26

In public it needs to be augmented by the gift of interpretation

1CO 14:26–14:28 See also 1CO 12:30, 1CO 14:5, 1CO 14:13
